translation: Coverdale Psalter (Book of Common Prayer tradition, 1539)

source.note: Public domain (per the source repo's own bundled-translation metadata), except that printing the BCP text in the United Kingdom is subject to Crown Copyright - see SOURCES.md. Labeled "Coverdale Psalter" with standard Hebrew/Masoretic numbering by the source repo itself (distinct from its separate "BCP (1979) Psalter" and "...KJV/Douay Versification" files, which are not used here).

gloriaPatri: Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Ghost; * As it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world without end. Amen.

1.1: Blessed is the man that hath not walked in the counsel of the ungodly, nor stood in the way of sinners: and hath not sat in the seat of the scornful.
1.2: But his delight is in the law of the Lord: and in his law will he exercise himself day and night.
1.3: And he shall be like a tree planted by the water-side: that will bring forth his fruit in due season.
1.4: His leaf also shall not wither: and look, whatsoever he doeth, it shall prosper.
1.5: As for the ungodly, it is not so with them: but they are like the chaff, which the wind scattereth away from the face of the earth.
1.6: Therefore the ungodly shall not be able to stand in the judgement: neither the sinners in the congregation of the righteous.
1.7: But the Lord knoweth the way of the righteous: and the way of the ungodly shall perish.

2.1: Why do the heathen so furiously rage together: and why do the people imagine a vain thing?
2.2: The kings of the earth stand up, and the rulers take counsel together: against the Lord, and against his Anointed.
2.3: Let us break their bonds asunder: and cast away their cords from us.
2.4: He that dwelleth in heaven shall laugh them to scorn: the Lord shall have them in derision.
2.5: Then shall he speak unto them in his wrath: and vex them in his sore displeasure.
2.6: Yet have I set my King: upon my holy hill of Sion.
2.7: I will preach the law, whereof the Lord hath said unto me: Thou art my Son, this day have I begotten thee.
2.8: Desire of me, and I shall give thee the heathen for thine inheritance: and the utmost parts of the earth for thy possession.
2.9: Thou shalt bruise them with a rod of iron: and break them in pieces like a potter's vessel.
2.10: Be wise now therefore, O ye kings: be learned, ye that are judges of the earth.
2.11: Serve the Lord in fear: and rejoice unto him with reverence.
2.12: Kiss the Son, lest he be angry, and so ye perish from the right way: if his wrath be kindled, (yea, but a little,) blessed are all they that put their trust in him.

3.1: Lord, how are they increased that trouble me: many are they that rise against me.
3.2: Many one there be that say of my soul: There is no help for him in his God.
3.3: But thou, O Lord, art my defender: thou art my worship, and the lifter up of my head.
3.4: I did call upon the Lord with my voice: and he heard me out of his holy hill.
3.5: I laid me down and slept, and rose up again: for the Lord sustained me.
3.6: I will not be afraid for ten thousands of the people: that have set themselves against me round about.
3.7: Up, Lord, and help me, O my God: for thou smitest all mine enemies upon the cheek-bone; thou hast broken the teeth of the ungodly.
3.8: Salvation belongeth unto the Lord: and thy blessing is upon thy people.

4.1: Hear me when I call, O God of my righteousness: thou hast set me at liberty when I was in trouble; have mercy upon me, and hearken unto my prayer.
4.2: O ye sons of men, how long will ye blaspheme mine honour: and have such pleasure in vanity, and seek after leasing?
4.3: Know this also, that the Lord hath chosen to himself the man that is godly: when I call upon the Lord, he will hear me.
4.4: Stand in awe, and sin not: commune with your own heart, and in your chamber, and be still.
4.5: Offer the sacrifice of righteousness: and put your trust in the Lord.
4.6: There be many that say: Who will shew us any good?
4.7: Lord, lift thou up: the light of thy countenance upon us.
4.8: Thou hast put gladness in my heart: since the time that their corn and wine and oil increased.
4.9: I will lay me down in peace, and take my rest: for it is thou, Lord, only, that makest me dwell in safety.

5.1: Ponder my words, O Lord: consider my meditation.
5.2: O hearken thou unto the voice of my calling, my King, and my God: for unto thee will I make my prayer.
5.3: My voice shalt thou hear betimes, O Lord: early in the morning will I direct my prayer unto thee, and will look up.
5.4: For thou art the God that hast no pleasure in wickedness: neither shall any evil dwell with thee.
5.5: Such as be foolish shall not stand in thy sight: for thou hatest all them that work vanity.
5.6: Thou shalt destroy them that speak leasing: the Lord will abhor both the blood-thirsty and deceitful man.
5.7: But as for me, I will come into thine house, even upon the multitude of thy mercy: and in thy fear will I worship toward thy holy temple.
5.8: Lead me, O Lord, in thy righteousness, because of mine enemies: make thy way plain before my face.
5.9: For there is no faithfulness in his mouth: their inward parts are very wickedness.
5.10: Their throat is an open sepulchre: they flatter with their tongue.
5.11: Destroy thou them, O God; let them perish through their own imaginations: cast them out in the multitude of their ungodliness; for they have rebelled against thee.
5.12: And let all them that put their trust in thee rejoice: they shall ever be giving of thanks, because thou defendest them; they that love thy Name shall be joyful in thee;
5.13: For thou, Lord, wilt give thy blessing unto the righteous: and with thy favourable kindness wilt thou defend him as with a shield.

6.1: O Lord, rebuke me not in thine indignation: neither chasten me in thy displeasure.
6.2: Have mercy upon me, O Lord, for I am weak: Lord, heal me, for my bones are vexed.
6.3: My soul also is sore troubled: but, Lord, how long wilt thou punish me?
6.4: Turn thee, O Lord, and deliver my soul: O save me for thy mercy's sake.
6.5: For in death no man remembereth thee: and who will give thee thanks in the pit?
6.6: I am weary of my groaning; every night wash I my bed: and water my couch with my tears.
6.7: My beauty is gone for very trouble: and worn away because of all mine enemies.
6.8: Away from me, all ye that work vanity: for the Lord hath heard the voice of my weeping.
6.9: The Lord hath heard my petition: the Lord will receive my prayer.
6.10: All mine enemies shall be confounded, and sore vexed: they shall be turned back, and put to shame suddenly.

7.1: O Lord my God, in thee have I put my trust: save me from all them that persecute me, and deliver me;
7.2: Lest he devour my soul, like a lion, and tear it in pieces: while there is none to help.
7.3: O Lord my God, if I have done any such thing: or if there be any wickedness in my hands;
7.4: If I have rewarded evil unto him that dealt friendly with me: yea, I have delivered him that without any cause is mine enemy;
7.5: Then let mine enemy persecute my soul, and take me: yea, let him tread my life down upon the earth, and lay mine honour in the dust.
7.6: Stand up, O Lord, in thy wrath, and lift up thyself, because of the indignation of mine enemies: arise up for me in the judgement that thou hast commanded.
7.7: And so shall the congregation of the people come about thee: for their sakes therefore lift up thyself again.
7.8: The Lord shall judge the people; give sentence with me, O Lord: according to my righteousness, and according to the innocency that is in me.
7.9: O let the wickedness of the ungodly come to an end: but guide thou the just.
7.10: For the righteous God: trieth the very hearts and reins.
7.11: My help cometh of God: who preserveth them that are true of heart.
7.12: God is a righteous Judge, strong and patient: and God is provoked every day.
7.13: If a man will not turn, he will whet his sword: he hath bent his bow, and made it ready.
7.14: He hath prepared for him the instruments of death: he ordaineth his arrows against the persecutors.
7.15: Behold, he travaileth with mischief: he hath conceived sorrow, and brought forth ungodliness.
7.16: He hath graven and digged up a pit: and is fallen himself into the destruction that he made for other.
7.17: For his travail shall come upon his own head: and his wickedness shall fall on his own pate.
7.18: I will give thanks unto the Lord, according to his righteousness: and I will praise the Name of the Lord most High.

8.1: O Lord our Governor, how excellent is thy Name in all the world: thou that hast set thy glory above the heavens!
8.2: Out of the mouth of very babes and sucklings hast thou ordained strength, because of thine enemies: that thou mightest still the enemy and the avenger.
8.3: For I will consider thy heavens, even the works of thy fingers: the moon and the stars, which thou hast ordained.
8.4: What is man, that thou art mindful of him: and the son of man, that thou visitest him?
8.5: Thou madest him lower than the angels: to crown him with glory and worship.
8.6: Thou makest him to have dominion of the works of thy hands: and thou hast put all things in subjection under his feet;
8.7: All sheep and oxen: yea, and the beasts of the field;
8.8: The fowls of the air, and the fishes of the sea: and whatsoever walketh through the paths of the seas.
8.9: O Lord our Governor: how excellent is thy Name in all the world!

9.1: I will give thanks unto thee, O Lord, with my whole heart: I will speak of all thy marvellous works.
9.2: I will be glad and rejoice in thee: yea, my songs will I make of thy Name, O thou most Highest.
9.3: While mine enemies are driven back: they shall fall and perish at thy presence.
9.4: For thou hast maintained my right and my cause: thou art set in the throne that judgest right.
9.5: Thou hast rebuked the heathen, and destroyed the ungodly: thou hast put out their name forever and ever.
9.6: O thou enemy, destructions are come to a perpetual end: even as the cities which thou hast destroyed, their memorial is perished with them.
9.7: But the Lord shall endure for ever: he hath also prepared his seat for judgement.
9.8: For he shall judge the world in righteousness: and minister true judgement unto the people.
9.9: The Lord also will be a defence for the oppressed: even a refuge in due time of trouble.
9.10: And they that know thy Name will put their trust in thee: for thou, Lord, hast never failed them that seek thee.
9.11: O praise the Lord which dwelleth in Sion: shew the people of his doings.
9.12: For when he maketh inquisition for blood, he remembereth them: and forgetteth not the complaint of the poor.
9.13: Have mercy upon me, O Lord; consider the trouble which I suffer of them that hate me: thou that liftest me up from the gates of death.
9.14: That I may shew all thy praises within the ports of the daughter of Sion: I will rejoice in thy salvation.
9.15: The heathen are sunk down in the pit that they made: in the same net which they hid privily, is their foot taken.
9.16: The Lord is known to execute judgement: the ungodly is trapped in the work of his own hands.
9.17: The wicked shall be turned into hell: and all the people that forget God.
9.18: For the poor shall not alway be forgotten: the patient abiding of the meek shall not perish for ever.
9.19: Up, Lord, and let not man have the upper hand: let the heathen be judged in thy sight.
9.20: Put them in fear, O Lord: that the heathen may know themselves to be but men.

10.1: Why standest thou so far off, O Lord: and hidest thy face in the needful time of trouble?
10.2: The ungodly for his own lust doth persecute the poor: let them be taken in the crafty wiliness that they have imagined.
10.3: For the ungodly hath made boast of his own heart's desire: and speaketh good of the covetous, whom God abhorreth.
10.4: The ungodly is so proud, that he careth not for God: neither is God in all his thoughts.
10.5: His ways are alway grievous: thy judgements are far above out of his sight, and therefore defieth he all his enemies.
10.6: For he hath said in his heart, Tush, I shall never be cast down: there shall no harm happen unto me.
10.7: His mouth is full of cursing, deceit, and fraud: under his tongue is ungodliness and vanity.
10.8: He sitteth lurking in the thievish corners of the streets: and privily in his lurking dens doth he murder the innocent; his eyes are set against the poor.
10.9: For he lieth waiting secretly, even as a lion lurketh he in his den: that he may ravish the poor.
10.10: He doth ravish the poor: when he getteth him into his net.
10.11: He falleth down, and humbleth himself: that the congregation of the poor may fall into the hands of his captains.
10.12: He hath said in his heart, Tush, God hath forgotten: he hideth away his face, and he will never see it.
10.13: Arise, O Lord God, and lift up thine hand: forget not the poor.
10.14: Wherefore should the wicked blaspheme God: while he doth say in his heart, Tush, thou God carest not for it.
10.15: Surely thou hast seen it: for thou beholdest ungodliness and wrong.
10.16: That thou mayest take the matter into thy hand: the poor committeth himself unto thee; for thou art the helper of the friendless.
10.17: Break thou the power of the ungodly and malicious: take away his ungodliness, and thou shalt find none.
10.18: The Lord is King for ever and ever: and the heathen are perished out of the land.
10.19: Lord, thou hast heard the desire of the poor: thou preparest their heart, and thine ear hearkeneth thereto;
10.20: To help the fatherless and poor unto their right: that the man of the earth be no more exalted against them.

11.1: In the Lord put I my trust: how say ye then to my soul, that she should flee as a bird unto the hill?
11.2: For lo, the ungodly bend their bow, and make ready their arrows within the quiver: that they may privily shoot at them which are true of heart.
11.3: For the foundations will be cast down: and what hath the righteous done?
11.4: The Lord is in his holy temple: the Lord's seat is in heaven.
11.5: His eyes consider the poor: and his eye-lids try the children of men.
11.6: The Lord alloweth the righteous: but the ungodly, and him that delighteth in wickedness, doth his soul abhor.
11.7: Upon the ungodly he shall rain snares, fire and brimstone, storm and tempest: this shall be their portion to drink.
11.8: For the righteous Lord loveth righteousness: his countenance will behold the thing that is just.

12.1: Help me, Lord, for there is not one godly man left: for the faithful are minished from among the children of men.
12.2: They talk of vanity every one with his neighbour: they do but flatter with their lips, and dissemble in their double heart.
12.3: The Lord shall root out all deceitful lips: and the tongue that speaketh proud things;
12.4: Which have said, With our tongue will we prevail: we are they that ought to speak, who is lord over us?
12.5: Now for the comfortless trouble's sake of the needy: and because of the deep sighing of the poor,
12.6: I will up, saith the Lord: and will help every one from him that swelleth against him, and will set him at rest.
12.7: The words of the Lord are pure words: even as the silver, which from the earth is tried, and purified seven times in the fire.
12.8: Thou shalt keep them, O Lord: thou shalt preserve him from this generation for ever.
12.9: The ungodly walk on every side: when they are exalted, the children of men are put to rebuke.

13.1: How long wilt thou forget me, O Lord, for ever: how long wilt thou hide thy face from me?
13.2: How long shall I seek counsel in my soul, and be so vexed in my heart: how long shall mine enemies triumph over me?
13.3: Consider, and hear me, O Lord my God: lighten mine eyes, that I sleep not in death.
13.4: Lest mine enemy say, I have prevailed against him: for if I be cast down, they that trouble me will rejoice at it.
13.5: But my trust is in thy mercy: and my heart is joyful in thy salvation.
13.6: I will sing of the Lord, because he hath dealt so lovingly with me: yea, I will praise the Name of the Lord most Highest.

14.1: The fool hath said in his heart: There is no God.
14.2: They are corrupt, and become abominable in their doings: there is none that doeth good, no not one.
14.3: The Lord looked down from heaven upon the children of men: to see if there were any that would understand, and seek after God.
14.4: But they are all gone out of the way, they are altogether become abominable: there is none that doeth good, no not one.
14.5: Their throat is an open sepulchre, with their tongues have they deceived: the poison of asps is under their lips.
14.6: Their mouth is full of cursing and bitterness: their feet are swift to shed blood.
14.7: Destruction and unhappiness is in their ways, and the way of peace have they not known: there is no fear of God before their eyes.
14.8: Have they no knowledge, that they are all such workers of mischief: eating up my people as it were bread, and call not upon the Lord?
14.9: There were they brought in great fear, even where no fear was: for God is in the generation of the righteous.
14.10: As for you, ye have made a mock at the counsel of the poor: because he putteth his trust in the Lord.
14.11: Who shall give salvation unto Israel out of Sion? When the Lord turneth the captivity of his people: then shall Jacob rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.

15.1: Lord, who shall dwell in thy tabernacle: or who shall rest upon thy holy hill?
15.2: Even he that leadeth an uncorrupt life: and doeth the thing which is right, and speaketh the truth from his heart.
15.3: He that hath used no deceit in his tongue, nor done evil to his neighbour: and hath not slandered his neighbour.
15.4: He that setteth not by himself, but is lowly in his own eyes: and maketh much of them that fear the Lord.
15.5: He that sweareth unto his neighbour, and disappointeth him not: though it were to his own hindrance.
15.6: He that hath not given his money upon usury: nor taken reward against the innocent. Whoso doeth these things: shall never fall.

16.1: Preserve me, O God: for in thee have I put my trust.
16.2: O my soul, thou hast said unto the Lord: Thou art my God, my goods are nothing unto thee.
16.3: All my delight is upon the saints, that are in the earth: and upon such as excel in virtue.
16.4: But they that run after another god: shall have great trouble.
16.5: Their drink-offerings of blood will I not offer: neither make mention of their names within my lips.
16.6: The Lord himself is the portion of mine inheritance, and of my cup: thou shalt maintain my lot.
16.7: The lot is fallen unto me in a fair ground: yea, I have a goodly heritage.
16.8: I will thank the Lord for giving me warning: my reins also chasten me in the night-season.
16.9: I have set God always before me: for he is on my right hand, therefore I shall not fall.
16.10: Wherefore my heart was glad, and my glory rejoiced: my flesh also shall rest in hope.
16.11: For why? thou shalt not leave my soul in hell: neither shalt thou suffer thy Holy One to see corruption.
16.12: Thou shalt shew me the path of life; in thy presence is the fulness of joy: and at thy right hand there is pleasure for evermore.

17.1: Hear the right, O Lord, consider my complaint: and hearken unto my prayer, that goeth not out of feigned lips.
17.2: Let my sentence come forth from thy presence: and let thine eyes look upon the thing that is equal.
17.3: Thou hast proved and visited mine heart in the night-season; thou hast tried me, and shalt find no wickedness in me: for I am utterly purposed that my mouth shall not offend.
17.4: Because of men's works, that are done against the words of thy lips: I have kept me from the ways of the destroyer.
17.5: O hold thou up my goings in thy paths: that my footsteps slip not.
17.6: I have called upon thee, O God, for thou shalt hear me: incline thine ear to me, and hearken unto my words.
17.7: Shew thy marvellous loving-kindness, thou that art the Saviour of them which put their trust in thee: from such as resist thy right hand.
17.8: Keep me as the apple of an eye: hide me under the shadow of thy wings,
17.9: From the ungodly that trouble me: mine enemies compass me round about to take away my soul.
17.10: They are inclosed in their own fat: and their mouth speaketh proud things.
17.11: They lie waiting in our way on every side: turning their eyes down to the ground;
17.12: Like as a lion that is greedy of his prey: and as it were a lion's whelp, lurking in secret places.
17.13: Up, Lord, disappoint him, and cast him down: deliver my soul from the ungodly, which is a sword of thine;
17.14: From the men of thy hand, O Lord, from the men, I say, and from the evil world: which have their portion in this life, whose bellies thou fillest with thy hid treasure.
17.15: They have children at their desire: and leave the rest of their substance for their babes.
17.16: But as for me, I will behold thy presence in righteousness: and when I awake up after thy likeness, I shall be satisfied with it.

18.1: I will love thee, O Lord, my strength; the Lord is my stony rock, and my defence: my Saviour, my God, and my might, in whom I will trust, my buckler, the horn also of my salvation, and my refuge.
18.2: I will call upon the Lord, which is worthy to be praised: so shall I be safe from mine enemies.
18.3: The sorrows of death compassed me: and the overflowings of ungodliness made me afraid.
18.4: The pains of hell came about me: the snares of death overtook me.
18.5: In my trouble I will call upon the Lord: and complain unto my God.
18.6: So shall he hear my voice out of his holy temple: and my complaint shall come before him, it shall enter even into his ears.
18.7: The earth trembled and quaked: the very foundations also of the hills shook, and were removed, because he was wroth.
18.8: There went a smoke out in his presence: and a consuming fire out of his mouth, so that coals were kindled at it.
18.9: He bowed the heavens also, and came down: and it was dark under his feet.
18.10: He rode upon the cherubins, and did fly: he came flying upon the wings of the wind.
18.11: He made darkness his secret place: his pavilion round about him, with dark water and thick clouds to cover him.
18.12: At the brightness of his presence his clouds removed: hail-stones, and coals of fire.
18.13: The Lord also thundered out of heaven, and the Highest gave his thunder: hail-stones, and coals of fire.
18.14: He sent out his arrows, and scattered them: he cast forth lightnings, and destroyed them.
18.15: The springs of waters were seen, and the foundations of the round world were discovered, at thy chiding, O Lord: at the blasting of the breath of thy displeasure.
18.16: He shall send down from on high to fetch me: and shall take me out of many waters.
18.17: He shall deliver me from my strongest enemy, and from them which hate me: for they are too mighty for me.
18.18: They prevented me in the day of my trouble: but the Lord was my upholder.
18.19: He brought me forth also into a place of liberty: he brought me forth, even because he had a favour unto me.
18.20: The Lord shall reward me after my righteous dealing: according to the cleanness of my hands shall he recompense me.
18.21: Because I have kept the ways of the Lord: and have not forsaken my God, as the wicked doth.
18.22: For I have an eye unto all his laws: and will not cast out his commandments from me.
18.23: I was also uncorrupt before him: and eschewed mine own wickedness.
18.24: Therefore shall the Lord reward me after my righteous dealing: and according unto the cleanness of my hands in his eye-sight.
18.25: With the holy thou shalt be holy: and with a perfect man thou shalt be perfect.
18.26: With the clean thou shalt be clean: and with the froward thou shalt learn frowardness.
18.27: For thou shalt save the people that are in adversity: and shalt bring down the high looks of the proud.
18.28: Thou also shalt light my candle: the Lord my God shall make my darkness to be light.
18.29: For in thee I shall discomfit an host of men: and with the help of my God I shall leap over the wall.
18.30: The way of God is an undefiled way: the word of the Lord also is tried in the fire; he is the defender of all them that put their trust in him.
18.31: For who is God, but the Lord: or who hath any strength, except our God?
18.32: It is God, that girdeth me with strength of war: and maketh my way perfect.
18.33: He maketh my feet like harts feet: and setteth me up on high.
18.34: He teacheth mine hands to fight: and mine arms shall break even a bow of steel.
18.35: Thou hast given me the defence of thy salvation: thy right hand also shall hold me up, and thy loving correction shall make me great.
18.36: Thou shalt make room enough under me for to go: that my footsteps shall not slide.
18.37: I will follow upon mine enemies, and overtake them: neither will I turn again till I have destroyed them.
18.38: I will smite them, that they shall not be able to stand: but fall under my feet.
18.39: Thou hast girded me with strength unto the battle: thou shalt throw down mine enemies under me.
18.40: Thou hast made mine enemies also to turn their backs upon me: and I shall destroy them that hate me.
18.41: They shall cry, but there shall be none to help them: yea, even unto the Lord shall they cry, but he shall not hear them.
18.42: I will beat them as small as the dust before the wind: I will cast them out as the clay in the streets.
18.43: Thou shalt deliver me from the strivings of the people: and thou shalt make me the head of the heathen.
18.44: A people whom I have not known: shall serve me.
18.45: As soon as they hear of me, they shall obey me: but the strange children shall dissemble with me.
18.46: The strange children shall fail: and be afraid out of their prisons.
18.47: The Lord liveth, and blessed be my strong helper: and praised be the God of my salvation;
18.48: Even the God that seeth that I be avenged: and subdueth the people unto me.
18.49: It is he that delivereth me from my cruel enemies, and setteth me up above mine adversaries: thou shalt rid me from the wicked man.
18.50: For this cause will I give thanks unto thee, O Lord, among the Gentiles: and sing praises unto thy Name.
18.51: Great prosperity giveth he unto his King: and sheweth loving-kindness unto David his Anointed, and unto his seed for evermore.

19.1: The heavens declare the glory of God: and the firmament sheweth his handywork.
19.2: One day telleth another: and one night certifieth another.
19.3: There is neither speech nor language: but their voices are heard among them.
19.4: Their sound is gone out into all lands: and their words into the ends of the world.
19.5: In them hath he set a tabernacle for the sun: which cometh forth as a bridegroom out of his chamber, and rejoiceth as a giant to run his course.
19.6: It goeth forth from the uttermost part of the heaven, and runneth about unto the end of it again: and there is nothing hid from the heat thereof.
19.7: The law of the Lord is an undefiled law, converting the soul: the testimony of the Lord is sure, and giveth wisdom unto the simple.
19.8: The statutes of the Lord are right, and rejoice the heart: the commandment of the Lord is pure, and giveth light unto the eyes.
19.9: The fear of the Lord is clean, and endureth for ever: the judgements of the Lord are true, and righteous altogether.
19.10: More to be desired are they than gold, yea, than much fine gold: sweeter also than honey, and the honey-comb.
19.11: Moreover, by them is thy servant taught: and in keeping of them there is great reward.
19.12: Who can tell how oft he offendeth: O cleanse thou me from my secret faults.
19.13: Keep thy servant also from presumptuous sins, lest they get the dominion over me: so shall I be undefiled, and innocent from the great offence.
19.14: Let the words of my mouth, and the meditation of my heart: be alway acceptable in thy sight,
19.15: O Lord: my strength, and my redeemer.

20.1: The Lord hear thee in the day of trouble: the Name of the God of Jacob defend thee;
20.2: Send thee help from the sanctuary: and strengthen thee out of Sion;
20.3: Remember all thy offerings: and accept thy burnt-sacrifice;
20.4: Grant thee thy heart's desire: and fulfil all thy mind.
20.5: We will rejoice in thy salvation, and triumph in the Name of the Lord our God: the Lord perform all thy petitions.
20.6: Now know I that the Lord helpeth his Anointed, and will hear him from his holy heaven: even with the wholesome strength of his right hand.
20.7: Some put their trust in chariots, and some in horses: but we will remember the Name of the Lord our God.
20.8: They are brought down, and fallen: but we are risen, and stand upright.
20.9: Save, Lord, and hear us, O King of heaven; when we call upon thee.

21.1: The King shall rejoice in thy strength, O Lord: exceeding glad shall he be of thy salvation.
21.2: Thou hast given him his heart's desire: and hast not denied him the request of his lips.
21.3: For thou shalt prevent him with the blessings of goodness: and shalt set a crown of pure gold upon his head.
21.4: He asked life of thee, and thou gavest him a long life: even for ever and ever.
21.5: His honour is great in thy salvation: glory and great worship shalt thou lay upon him.
21.6: For thou shalt give him everlasting felicity: and make him glad with the joy of thy countenance.
21.7: And why? because the King putteth his trust in the Lord: and in the mercy of the most Highest he shall not miscarry.
21.8: All thine enemies shall feel thine hand: thy right hand shall find out them that hate thee.
21.9: Thou shalt make them like a fiery oven in time of thy wrath: the Lord shall destroy them in his displeasure, and the fire shall consume them.
21.10: Their fruit shalt thou root out of the earth: and their seed from among the children of men.
21.11: For they intended mischief against thee: and imagined such a device as they are not able to perform.
21.12: Therefore shalt thou put them to flight: and the strings of thy bow shalt thou make ready against the face of them.
21.13: Be thou exalted, Lord, in thine own strength: so will we sing, and praise thy power.

22.1: O my God, my God, look upon me; why hast thou forsaken me: and art so far from my health, and from the words of my complaint?
22.2: O my God, I cry in the day-time, but thou hearest not: and in the night-season also I take no rest.
22.3: And thou continuest holy: O thou worship of Israel.
22.4: Our fathers hoped in thee: they trusted in thee, and thou didst deliver them.
22.5: They called upon thee, and were holpen: they put their trust in thee, and were not confounded.
22.6: But as for me, I am a worm, and no man: a very scorn of men, and the outcast of the people.
22.7: All they that see me laugh me to scorn: they shoot out their lips, and shake their heads, saying,
22.8: He trusted in God, that he would deliver him: let him deliver him, if he will have him.
22.9: But thou art he that took me out of my mother's womb: thou wast my hope, when I hanged yet upon my mother's breasts.
22.10: I have been left unto thee ever since I was born: thou art my God, even from my mother's womb.
22.11: O go not from me, for trouble is hard at hand: and there is none to help me.
22.12: Many oxen are come about me: fat bulls of Basan close me in on every side.
22.13: They gape upon me with their mouths: as it were a ramping and a roaring lion.
22.14: I am poured out like water, and all my bones are out of joint: my heart also in the midst of my body is even like melting wax.
22.15: My strength is dried up like a potsherd, and my tongue cleaveth to my gums: and thou shalt bring me into the dust of death.
22.16: For many dogs are come about me: and the council of the wicked layeth siege against me.
22.17: They pierced my hands and my feet; I may tell all my bones: they stand staring and looking upon me.
22.18: They part my garments among them: and cast lots upon my vesture.
22.19: But be not thou far from me, O Lord: thou art my succour, haste thee to help me.
22.20: Deliver my soul from the sword: my darling from the power of the dog.
22.21: Save me from the lion's mouth: thou hast heard me also from among the horns of the unicorns.
22.22: I will declare thy Name unto my brethren: in the midst of the congregation will I praise thee.
22.23: O praise the Lord, ye that fear him: magnify him, all ye of the seed of Jacob, and fear him, all ye seed of Israel;
22.24: For he hath not despised, nor abhorred, the low estate of the poor: he hath not hid his face from him, but when he called unto him he heard him.
22.25: My praise is of thee in the great congregation: my vows will I perform in the sight of them that fear him.
22.26: The poor shall eat and be satisfied: they that seek after the Lord shall praise him; your heart shall live for ever.
22.27: All the ends of the world shall remember themselves, and be turned unto the Lord: and all the kindreds of the nations shall worship before him.
22.28: For the kingdom is the Lord's: and he is the Governor among the people.
22.29: All such as be fat upon earth: have eaten and worshipped.
22.30: All they that go down into the dust shall kneel before him: and no man hath quickened his own soul.
22.31: My seed shall serve him: they shall be counted unto the Lord for a generation.
22.32: They shall come, and the heavens shall declare his righteousness: unto a people that shall be born, whom the Lord hath made.

23.1: The Lord is my shepherd: therefore can I lack nothing.
23.2: He shall feed me in a green pasture: and lead me forth beside the waters of comfort.
23.3: He shall convert my soul: and bring me forth in the paths of righteousness, for his Name's sake.
23.4: Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil: for thou art with me; thy rod and thy staff comfort me.
23.5: Thou shalt prepare a table before me against them that trouble me: thou hast anointed my head with oil, and my cup shall be full.
23.6: But thy loving-kindness and mercy shall follow me all the days of my life: and I will dwell in the house of the Lord for ever.

24.1: The earth is the Lord's, and all that therein is: the compass of the world, and they that dwell therein.
24.2: For he hath founded it upon the seas: and prepared it upon the floods.
24.3: Who shall ascend into the hill of the Lord: or who shall rise up in his holy place?
24.4: Even he that hath clean hands, and a pure heart: and that hath not lift up his mind unto vanity, nor sworn to deceive his neighbour.
24.5: He shall receive the blessing from the Lord: and righteousness from the God of his salvation.
24.6: This is the generation of them that seek him: even of them that seek thy face, O Jacob.
24.7: Lift up your heads, O ye gates, and be ye lift up, ye everlasting doors: and the King of glory shall come in.
24.8: Who is the King of glory: it is the Lord strong and mighty, even the Lord mighty in battle.
24.9: Lift up your heads, O ye gates, and be ye lift up, ye everlasting doors: and the King of glory shall come in.
24.10: Who is the King of glory: even the Lord of hosts, he is the King of glory.

25.1: Unto thee, O Lord, will I lift up my soul; my God, I have put my trust in thee: O let me not be confounded, neither let mine enemies triumph over me.
25.2: For all they that hope in thee shall not be ashamed: but such as transgress without a cause shall be put to confusion.
25.3: Shew me thy ways, O Lord: and teach me thy paths.
25.4: Lead me forth in thy truth, and learn me: for thou art the God of my salvation; in thee hath been my hope all the day long.
25.5: Call to remembrance, O Lord, thy tender mercies: and thy loving-kindnesses, which have been ever of old.
25.6: O remember not the sins and offences of my youth: but according to thy mercy think thou upon me, O Lord, for thy goodness.
25.7: Gracious and righteous is the Lord: therefore will he teach sinners in the way.
25.8: Them that are meek shall he guide in judgement: and such as are gentle, them shall he learn his way.
25.9: All the paths of the Lord are mercy and truth: unto such as keep his covenant and his testimonies.
25.10: For thy Name's sake, O Lord: be merciful unto my sin, for it is great.
25.11: What man is he that feareth the Lord: him shall he teach in the way that he shall choose.
25.12: His soul shall dwell at ease: and his seed shall inherit the land.
25.13: The secret of the Lord is among them that fear him: and he will shew them his covenant.
25.14: Mine eyes are ever looking unto the Lord: for he shall pluck my feet out of the net.
25.15: Turn thee unto me, and have mercy upon me: for I am desolate and in misery.
25.16: The sorrows of my heart are enlarged: O bring thou me out of my troubles.
25.17: Look upon my adversity and misery: and forgive me all my sin.
25.18: Consider mine enemies, how many they are: and they bear a tyrannous hate against me.
25.19: O keep my soul, and deliver me: let me not be confounded, for I have put my trust in thee.
25.20: Let perfectness and righteous dealing wait upon me: for my hope hath been in thee.
25.21: Deliver Israel, O God: out of all his troubles.

26.1: Be thou my Judge, O Lord, for I have walked innocently: my trust hath been also in the Lord, therefore shall I not fall.
26.2: Examine me, O Lord, and prove me: try out my reins and my heart.
26.3: For thy loving-kindness is ever before mine eyes: and I will walk in thy truth.
26.4: I have not dwelt with vain persons: neither will I have fellowship with the deceitful.
26.5: I have hated the congregation of the wicked: and will not sit among the ungodly.
26.6: I will wash my hands in innocency, O Lord: and so will I go to thine altar;
26.7: That I may shew the voice of thanksgiving: and tell of all thy wondrous works.
26.8: Lord, I have loved the habitation of thy house: and the place where thine honour dwelleth.
26.9: O shut not up my soul with the sinners: nor my life with the blood-thirsty;
26.10: In whose hands is wickedness: and their right hand is full of gifts.
26.11: But as for me, I will walk innocently: O deliver me, and be merciful unto me.
26.12: My foot standeth right: I will praise the Lord in the congregations.

27.1: The Lord is my light and my salvation; whom I then shall I fear: the Lord is the strength of my life; of whom then shall I be afraid?
27.2: When the wicked, even mine enemies and my foes, came upon me to eat up my flesh: they stumbled and fell.
27.3: Though an host of men were laid against me, yet shall not my heart be afraid: and though there rose up war against me, yet will I put my trust in him.
27.4: One thing have I desired of the Lord, which I will require: even that I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life, to behold the fair beauty of the Lord, and to visit his temple.
27.5: For in the time of trouble he shall hide me in his tabernacle: yea, in the secret place of his dwelling shall he hide me, and set me up upon a rock of stone.
27.6: And now shall he lift up mine head: above mine enemies round about me.
27.7: Therefore will I offer in his dwelling an oblation with great gladness: I will sing, and speak praises unto the Lord.
27.8: Hearken unto my voice, O Lord, when I cry unto thee: have mercy upon me, and hear me.
27.9: My heart hath talked of thee, Seek ye my face: Thy face, Lord, will I seek.
27.10: O hide not thou thy face from me: nor cast thy servant away in displeasure.
27.11: Thou hast been my succour: leave me not, neither forsake me, O God of my salvation,
27.12: When my father and my mother forsake me: the Lord taketh me up.
27.13: Teach me thy way, O Lord: and lead me in the right way, because of mine enemies.
27.14: Deliver me not over into the will of mine adversaries: for there are false witnesses risen up against me, and such as speak wrong.
27.15: I should utterly have fainted: but that I believe verily to see the goodness of the Lord in the land of the living.
27.16: O tarry thou the Lord's leisure: be strong, and he shall comfort thine heart; and put thou thy trust in the Lord.

28.1: Unto thee will I cry, O Lord my strength: think no scorn of me; lest, if thou make as though thou hearest not, I become like them that go down into the pit.
28.2: Hear the voice of my humble petitions, when I cry unto thee: when I hold up my hands towards the mercy-seat of thy holy temple.
28.3: O pluck me not away, neither destroy me, with the ungodly and wicked doers: which speak friendly to their neighbours, but imagine mischief in their hearts.
28.4: Reward them according to their deeds: and according to the wickedness of their own inventions.
28.5: Recompense them after the work of their hands: pay them that they have deserved.
28.6: For they regard not in their mind the works of the Lord, nor the operation of his hands: therefore shall he break them down, and not build them up.
28.7: Praised be the Lord: for he hath heard the voice of my humble petitions.
28.8: The Lord is my strength and my shield; my heart hath trusted in him, and I am helped: therefore my heart danceth for joy, and in my song will I praise him.
28.9: The Lord is my strength: and he is the wholesome defence of his Anointed.
28.10: O save thy people, and give thy blessing unto thine inheritance: feed them, and set them up for ever.

29.1: Bring unto the Lord, O ye mighty, bring young rams unto the Lord: ascribe unto the Lord worship and strength.
29.2: Give the Lord the honour due unto his Name: worship the Lord with holy worship.
29.3: It is the Lord that commandeth the waters: it is the glorious God that maketh the thunder.
29.4: It is the Lord that ruleth the sea; the voice of the Lord is mighty in operation: the voice of the Lord is a glorious voice.
29.5: The voice of the Lord breaketh the cedar-trees: yea, the Lord breaketh the cedars of Libanus.
29.6: He maketh them also to skip like a calf: Libanus also, and Sirion, like a young unicorn.
29.7: The voice of the Lord divideth the flames of fire; the voice of the Lord shaketh the wilderness: yea, the Lord shaketh the wilderness of Cades.
29.8: The voice of the Lord maketh the hinds to bring forth young, and discovereth the thick bushes: in his temple doth every man speak of his honour.
29.9: The Lord sitteth above the water-flood: and the Lord remaineth a King for ever.
29.10: The Lord shall give strength unto his people: the Lord shall give his people the blessing of peace.

30.1: I will magnify thee, O Lord, for thou hast set me up: and not made my foes to triumph over me.
30.2: O Lord my God, I cried unto thee: and thou hast healed me.
30.3: Thou, Lord, hast brought my soul out of hell: thou hast kept my life from them that go down to the pit.
30.4: Sing praises unto the Lord, O ye saints of his: and give thanks unto him for a remembrance of his holiness.
30.5: For his wrath endureth but the twinkling of an eye, and in his pleasure is life: heaviness may endure for a night, but joy cometh in the morning.
30.6: And in my prosperity I said, I shall never be removed: thou, Lord, of thy goodness hast made my hill so strong.
30.7: Thou didst turn thy face from me: and I was troubled.
30.8: Then cried I unto thee, O Lord: and gat me to my Lord right humbly.
30.9: What profit is there in my blood: when I go down to the pit?
30.10: Shall the dust give thanks unto thee: or shall it declare thy truth?
30.11: Hear, O Lord, and have mercy upon me: Lord, be thou my helper.
30.12: Thou hast turned my heaviness into joy: thou hast put off my sackcloth, and girded me with gladness.
30.13: Therefore shall every good man sing of thy praise without ceasing: O my God, I will give thanks unto thee for ever.

31.1: In thee, O Lord, have I put my trust: let me never be put to confusion, deliver me in thy righteousness.
31.2: Bow down thine ear to me: make haste to deliver me.
31.3: And be thou my strong rock, and house of defence: that thou mayest save me.
31.4: For thou art my strong rock, and my castle: be thou also my guide, and lead me for thy Name's sake.
31.5: Draw me out of the net that they have laid privily for me: for thou art my strength.
31.6: Into thy hands I commend my spirit: for thou hast redeemed me, O Lord, thou God of truth.
31.7: I have hated them that hold of superstitious vanities: and my trust hath been in the Lord.
31.8: I will be glad and rejoice in thy mercy: for thou hast considered my trouble, and hast known my soul in adversities.
31.9: Thou hast not shut me up into the hand of the enemy: but hast set my feet in a large room.
31.10: Have mercy upon me, O Lord, for I am in trouble: and mine eye is consumed for very heaviness; yea, my soul and my body.
31.11: For my life is waxen old with heaviness: and my years with mourning.
31.12: My strength faileth me, because of mine iniquity: and my bones are consumed.
31.13: I became a reproof among all mine enemies, but especially among my neighbours: and they of mine acquaintance were afraid of me; and they that did see me without conveyed themselves from me.
31.14: I am clean forgotten, as a dead man out of mind: I am become like a broken vessel.
31.15: For I have heard the blasphemy of the multitude: and fear is on every side, while they conspire together against me, and take their counsel to take away my life.
31.16: But my hope hath been in thee, O Lord: I have said, Thou art my God.
31.17: My time is in thy hand, deliver me from the hand of mine enemies: and from them that persecute me.
31.18: Shew thy servant the light of thy countenance: and save me for thy mercy's sake.
31.19: Let me not be confounded, O Lord, for I have called upon thee: let the ungodly be put to confusion, and be put to silence in the grave.
31.20: Let the lying lips be put to silence: which cruelly, disdainfully, and despitefully, speak against the righteous.
31.21: O how plentiful is thy goodness, which thou hast laid up for them that fear thee: and that thou hast prepared for them that put their trust in thee, even before the sons of men!
31.22: Thou shalt hide them privily by thine own presence from the provoking of all men: thou shalt keep them secretly in thy tabernacle from the strife of tongues.
31.23: Thanks be to the Lord: for he hath shewed me marvellous great kindness in a strong city.
31.24: And when I made haste, I said: I am cast out of the sight of thine eyes.
31.25: Nevertheless, thou heardest the voice of my prayer: when I cried unto thee.
31.26: O love the Lord, all ye his saints: for the Lord preserveth them that are faithful, and plenteously rewardeth the proud doer.
31.27: Be strong, and he shall establish your heart: all ye that put your trust in the Lord.

32.1: Blessed is he whose unrighteousness is forgiven: and whose sin is covered.
32.2: Blessed is the man unto whom the Lord imputeth no sin: and in whose spirit there is no guile.
32.3: For while I held my tongue: my bones consumed away through my daily complaining.
32.4: For thy hand is heavy upon me day and night: and my moisture is like the drought in summer.
32.5: I will acknowledge my sin unto thee: and mine unrighteousness have I not hid.
32.6: I said, I will confess my sins unto the Lord: and so thou forgavest the wickedness of my sin.
32.7: For this shall every one that is godly make his prayer unto thee, in a time when thou mayest be found: but in the great water-floods they shall not come nigh him.
32.8: Thou art a place to hide me in, thou shalt preserve me from trouble: thou shalt compass me about with songs of deliverance.
32.9: I will inform thee, and teach thee in the way wherein thou shalt go: and I will guide thee with mine eye.
32.10: Be ye not like to horse and mule, which have no understanding: whose mouths must be held with bit and bridle, lest they fall upon thee.
32.11: Great plagues remain for the ungodly: but whoso putteth his trust in the Lord, mercy embraceth him on every side.
32.12: Be glad, O ye righteous, and rejoice in the Lord: and be joyful, all ye that are true of heart.

33.1: Rejoice in the Lord, O ye righteous: for it becometh well the just to be thankful.
33.2: Praise the Lord with harp: sing praises unto him with the lute, and instrument of ten strings.
33.3: Sing unto the Lord a new song: sing praises lustily unto him with a good courage.
33.4: For the word of the Lord is true: and all his works are faithful.
33.5: He loveth righteousness, and judgement: the earth is full of the goodness of the Lord.
33.6: By the word of the Lord were the heavens made: and all the hosts of them by the breath of his mouth.
33.7: He gathereth the waters of the sea together, as it were upon an heap: and layeth up the deep, as in a treasure-house.
33.8: Let all the earth fear the Lord: stand in awe of him, all ye that dwell in the world.
33.9: For he spake, and it was done: he commanded, and it stood fast.
33.10: The Lord bringeth the counsel of the heathen to nought: and maketh the devices of the people to be of none effect, and casteth out the counsels of princes.
33.11: The counsel of the Lord shall endure for ever: and the thoughts of his heart from generation to generation.
33.12: Blessed are the people, whose God is the Lord Jehovah: and blessed are the folk, that he hath chosen to him, to be his inheritance.
33.13: The Lord looked down from heaven, and beheld all the children of men: from the habitation of his dwelling he considereth all them that dwell on the earth.
33.14: He fashioneth all the hearts of them: and understandeth all their works.
33.15: There is no king that can be saved by the multitude of an host: neither is any mighty man delivered by much strength.
33.16: A horse is counted but a vain thing to save a man: neither shall he deliver any man by his great strength.
33.17: Behold, the eye of the Lord is upon them that fear him: and upon them that put their trust in his mercy;
33.18: To deliver their soul from death: and to feed them in the time of dearth.
33.19: Our soul hath patiently tarried for the Lord: for he is our help and our shield.
33.20: For our heart shall rejoice in him: because we have hoped in his holy Name.
33.21: Let thy merciful kindness, O Lord, be upon us: like as we do put our trust in thee.

34.1: I will alway give thanks unto the Lord: his praise shall ever be in my mouth.
34.2: My soul shall make her boast in the Lord: the humble shall hear thereof, and be glad.
34.3: O praise the Lord with me: and let us magnify his Name together.
34.4: I sought the Lord, and he heard me: yea, he delivered me out of all my fear.
34.5: They had an eye unto him, and were lightened: and their faces were not ashamed.
34.6: Lo, the poor crieth, and the Lord heareth him: yea, and saveth him out of all his troubles.
34.7: The angel of the Lord tarrieth round about them that fear him: and delivereth them.
34.8: O taste, and see, how gracious the Lord is: blessed is the man that trusteth in him.
34.9: O fear the Lord, ye that are his saints: for they that fear him lack nothing.
34.10: The lions do lack, and suffer hunger: but they who seek the Lord shall want no manner of thing that is good.
34.11: Come, ye children, and hearken unto me: I will teach you the fear of the Lord.
34.12: What man is he that lusteth to live: and would fain see good days?
34.13: Keep thy tongue from evil: and thy lips, that they speak no guile.
34.14: Eschew evil, and do good: seek peace, and ensue it.
34.15: The eyes of the Lord are over the righteous: and his ears are open unto their prayers.
34.16: The countenance of the Lord is against them that do evil: to root out the remembrance of them from the earth.
34.17: The righteous cry, and the Lord heareth them: and delivereth them out of all their troubles.
34.18: The Lord is nigh unto them that are of a contrite heart: and will save such as be of an humble spirit.
34.19: Great are the troubles of the righteous: but the Lord delivereth him out of all.
34.20: He keepeth all his bones: so that not one of them is broken.
34.21: But misfortune shall slay the ungodly: and they that hate the righteous shall be desolate.
34.22: The Lord delivereth the souls of his servants: and all they that put their trust in him shall not be destitute.

35.1: Plead thou my cause, O Lord, with them that strive with me: and fight thou against them that fight against me.
35.2: Lay hand upon the shield and buckler: and stand up to help me.
35.3: Bring forth the spear, and stop the way against them that persecute me: say unto my soul, I am thy salvation.
35.4: Let them be confounded and put to shame, that seek after my soul: let them be turned back and brought to confusion, that imagine mischief for me.
35.5: Let them be as the dust before the wind: and the angel of the Lord scattering them.
35.6: Let their way be dark and slippery: and let the angel of the Lord persecute them.
35.7: For they have privily laid their net to destroy me without a cause: yea, even without a cause have they made a pit for my soul.
35.8: Let a sudden destruction come upon him unawares, and his net, that he hath laid privily, catch himself: that he may fall into his own mischief.
35.9: And, my soul, be joyful in the Lord: it shall rejoice in his salvation.
35.10: All my bones shall say, Lord, who is like unto thee, who deliverest the poor from him that is too strong for him: yea, the poor, and him that is in misery, from him that spoileth him?
35.11: False witnesses did rise up: they laid to my charge things that I knew not.
35.12: They rewarded me evil for good: to the great discomfort of my soul.
35.13: Nevertheless, when they were sick, I put on sackcloth, and humbled my soul with fasting: and my prayer shall turn into mine own bosom.
35.14: I behaved myself as though it had been my friend or my brother: I went heavily, as one that mourneth for his mother.
35.15: But in mine adversity they rejoiced, and gathered themselves together: yea, the very abjects came together against me unawares, making mouths at me, and ceased not.
35.16: With the flatterers were busy mockers: who gnashed upon me with their teeth.
35.17: Lord, how long wilt thou look upon this: O deliver my soul from the calamities which they bring on me, and my darling from the lions.
35.18: So will I give thee thanks in the great congregation: I will praise thee among much people.
35.19: O let not them that are mine enemies triumph over me ungodly: neither let them wink with their eyes that hate me without a cause.
35.20: And why? their communing is not for peace: but they imagine deceitful words against them that are quiet in the land.
35.21: They gaped upon me with their mouths, and said: Fie on thee, fie on thee, we saw it with our eyes.
35.22: This thou hast seen, O Lord: hold not thy tongue then, go not far from me, O Lord.
35.23: Awake, and stand up to judge my quarrel: avenge thou my cause, my God, and my Lord.
35.24: Judge me, O Lord my God, according to thy righteousness: and let them not triumph over me.
35.25: Let them not say in their hearts, There, there, so would we have it: neither let them say, We have devoured him.
35.26: Let them be put to confusion and shame together, that rejoice at my trouble: let them be clothed with rebuke and dishonour, that boast themselves against me.
35.27: Let them be glad and rejoice, that favour my righteous dealing: yea, let them say alway, Blessed be the Lord, who hath pleasure in the prosperity of his servant.
35.28: And as for my tongue, it shall be talking of thy righteousness: and of thy praise all the day long.

36.1: My heart sheweth me the wickedness of the ungodly: that there is no fear of God before his eyes.
36.2: For he flattereth himself in his own sight: until his abominable sin be found out.
36.3: The words of his mouth are unrighteous, and full of deceit: he hath left off to behave himself wisely, and to do good.
36.4: He imagineth mischief upon his bed, and hath set himself in no good way: neither doth he abhor any thing that is evil.
36.5: Thy mercy, O Lord, reacheth unto the heavens: and thy faithfulness unto the clouds.
36.6: Thy righteousness standeth like the strong mountains: thy judgements are like the great deep.
36.7: Thou, Lord, shalt save both man and beast; How excellent is thy mercy, O God: and the children of men shall put their trust under the shadow of thy wings.
36.8: They shall be satisfied with the plenteousness of thy house: and thou shalt give them drink of thy pleasures, as out of the river.
36.9: For with thee is the well of life: and in thy light shall we see light.
36.10: O continue forth thy loving-kindness unto them that know thee: and thy righteousness unto them that are true of heart.
36.11: O let not the foot of pride come against me: and let not the hand of the ungodly cast me down.
36.12: There are they fallen, all that work wickedness: they are cast down, and shall not be able to stand.

37.1: Fret not thyself because of the ungodly: neither be thou envious against the evil-doers,
37.2: For they shall soon be cut down like the grass: and be withered even as the green herb.
37.3: Put thou thy trust in the Lord, and be doing good: dwell in the land, and verily thou shalt be fed.
37.4: Delight thou in the Lord: and he shall give thee thy heart's desire.
37.5: Commit thy way unto the Lord, and put thy trust in him: and he shall bring it to pass.
37.6: He shall make thy righteousness as clear as the light: and thy just dealing as the noon-day.
37.7: Hold thee still in the Lord, and abide patiently upon him: but grieve not thyself at him whose way doth prosper, against the man that doeth after evil counsels.
37.8: Leave off from wrath, and let go displeasure: fret not thyself, else shalt thou be moved to do evil.
37.9: Wicked doers shall be rooted out: and they that patiently abide the Lord, those shall inherit the land.
37.10: Yet a little while, and the ungodly shall be clean gone: thou shalt look after his place, and he shall be away.
37.11: But the meek-spirited shall possess the earth: and shall be refreshed in the multitude of peace.
37.12: The ungodly seeketh counsel against the just: and gnasheth upon him with his teeth.
37.13: The Lord shall laugh him to scorn: for he hath seen that his day is coming.
37.14: The ungodly have drawn out the sword, and have bent their bow: to cast down the poor and needy, and to slay such as are of a right conversation.
37.15: Their sword shall go through their own heart: and their bow shall be broken.
37.16: A small thing that the righteous hath: is better than great riches of the ungodly.
37.17: For the arms of the ungodly shall be broken: and the Lord upholdeth the righteous.
37.18: The Lord knoweth the days of the godly: and their inheritance shall endure for ever.
37.19: They shall not be confounded in the perilous time: and in the days of dearth they shall have enough.
37.20: As for the ungodly; they shall perish; and the enemies of the Lord shall consume as the fat of lambs: yea, even as the smoke shall they consume away.
37.21: The ungodly borroweth, and payeth not again: but the righteous is merciful and liberal.
37.22: Such as are blessed of God shall possess the land: and they that are cursed of him shall be rooted out.
37.23: The Lord ordereth a good man's going: and maketh his way acceptable to himself.
37.24: Though he fall, he shall not be cast away: for the Lord upholdeth him with his hand.
37.25: I have been young, and now am old: and yet saw I never the righteous forsaken, nor his seed begging their bread.
37.26: The righteous is ever merciful, and lendeth: and his seed is blessed.
37.27: Flee from evil, and do the thing that is good: and dwell for evermore.
37.28: For the Lord loveth the thing that is right: he forsaketh not his that be godly, but they are preserved for ever.
37.29: The unrighteous shall be punished: as for the seed of the ungodly, it shall be rooted out.
37.30: The righteous shall inherit the land: and dwell therein for ever.
37.31: The mouth of the righteous is exercised in wisdom: and his tongue will be talking of judgement.
37.32: The law of his God is in his heart: and his goings shall not slide.
37.33: The ungodly seeth the righteous: and seeketh occasion to slay him.
37.34: The Lord will not leave him in his hand: nor condemn him when he is judged.
37.35: Hope thou in the Lord, and keep his way, and he shall promote thee, that thou shalt possess the land: when the ungodly shall perish, thou shalt see it.
37.36: I myself have seen the ungodly in great power: and flourishing like a green bay-tree.
37.37: I went by, and lo, he was gone: I sought him, but his place could no where be found.
37.38: Keep innocency, and take heed unto the thing that is right: for that shall bring a man peace at the last.
37.39: As for the transgressors, they shall perish together: and the end of the ungodly is, they shall be rooted out at the last.
37.40: But the salvation of the righteous cometh of the Lord: who is also their strength in the time of trouble.
37.41: And the Lord shall stand by them, and save them: he shall deliver them from the ungodly, and shall save them, because they put their trust in him.

38.1: Put me not to rebuke, O Lord, in thine anger: neither chasten me in thy heavy displeasure.
38.2: For thine arrows stick fast in me: and thy hand presseth me sore.
38.3: There is no health in my flesh, because of thy displeasure: neither is there any rest in my bones, by reason of my sin.
38.4: For my wickednesses are gone over my head: and are like a sore burden, too heavy for me to bear.
38.5: My wounds stink, and are corrupt: through my foolishness.
38.6: I am brought into so great trouble and misery: that I go mourning all the day long.
38.7: For my loins are filled with a sore disease: and there is no whole part in my body.
38.8: I am feeble, and sore smitten: I have roared for the very disquietness of my heart.
38.9: Lord, thou knowest all my desire: and my groaning is not hid from thee.
38.10: My heart panteth, my strength hath failed me: and the light of mine eyes is gone from me.
38.11: My lovers and my neighbours did stand looking upon my trouble: and my kinsmen stood afar off.
38.12: They also that sought after my life laid snares for me: and they that went about to do me evil talked of wickedness, and imagined deceit all the day long.
38.13: As for me, I was like a deaf man, and heard not: and as one that is dumb, who doth not open his mouth.
38.14: I became even as a man that heareth not: and in whose mouth are no reproofs.
38.15: For in thee, O Lord, have I put my trust: thou shalt answer for me, O Lord my God.
38.16: I have required that they, even mine enemies, should not triumph over me: for when my foot slipped, they rejoiced greatly against me.
38.17: And I, truly, am set in the plague: and my heaviness is ever in my sight.
38.18: For I will confess my wickedness: and be sorry for my sin.
38.19: But mine enemies live, and are mighty: and they that hate me wrongfully are many in number.
38.20: They also that reward evil for good are against me: because I follow the thing that good is.
38.21: Forsake me not, O Lord my God: be not thou far from me.
38.22: Haste thee to help me: O Lord God of my salvation.

39.1: I said, I will take heed to my ways: that I offend not in my tongue.
39.2: I will keep my mouth as it were with a bridle: while the ungodly is in my sight.
39.3: I held my tongue, and spake nothing: I kept silence, yea, even from good words; but it was pain and grief to me.
39.4: My heart was hot within me, and while I was thus musing the fire kindled: and at the last I spake with my tongue;
39.5: Lord, let me know mine end, and the number of my days: that I may be certified how long I have to live.
39.6: Behold, thou hast made my days as it were a span long: and mine age is even as nothing in respect of thee; and verily every man living is altogether vanity.
39.7: For man walketh in a vain shadow, and disquieteth himself in vain: he heapeth up riches, and cannot tell who shall gather them.
39.8: And now, Lord, what is my hope: truly my hope is even in thee.
39.9: Deliver me from all mine offences: and make me not a rebuke unto the foolish.
39.10: I became dumb, and opened not my mouth: for it was thy doing.
39.11: Take thy plague away from me: I am even consumed by the means of thy heavy hand.
39.12: When thou with rebukes dost chasten man for sin, thou makest his beauty to consume away, like as it were a moth fretting a garment: every man therefore is but vanity.
39.13: Hear my prayer, O Lord, and with thine ears consider my calling: hold not thy peace at my tears.
39.14: For I am a stranger with thee: and a sojourner, as all my fathers were.
39.15: O spare me a little, that I may recover my strength: before I go hence, and be no more seen.

40.1: I waited patiently for the Lord: and he inclined unto me, and heard my calling.
40.2: He brought me also out of the horrible pit, out of the mire and clay: and set my feet upon the rock, and ordered my goings.
40.3: And he hath put a new song in my mouth: even a thanksgiving unto our God.
40.4: Many shall see it, and fear: and shall put their trust in the Lord.
40.5: Blessed is the man that hath set his hope in the Lord: and turned not unto the proud, and to such as go about with lies.
40.6: O Lord my God, great are the wondrous works which thou hast done, like as be also thy thoughts which are to us-ward: and yet there is no man that ordereth them unto thee.
40.7: If I should declare them, and speak of them: they should be more than I am able to express.
40.8: Sacrifice and meat-offering thou wouldest not: but mine ears hast thou opened.
40.9: Burnt-offerings, and sacrifice for sin, hast thou not required: then said I, Lo, I come,
40.10: In the volume of the book it is written of me, that I should fulfil thy will, O my God: I am content to do it; yea, thy law is within my heart.
40.11: I have declared thy righteousness in the great congregation: lo, I will not refrain my lips, Lord, and that thou knowest.
40.12: I have not hid thy righteousness within my heart: my talk hath been of thy truth and of thy salvation.
40.13: I have not kept back thy loving mercy and truth: from the great congregation.
40.14: Withdraw not thou thy mercy from me, O Lord: let thy loving-kindness and thy truth alway preserve me.
40.15: For innumerable troubles are come about me; my sins have taken such hold upon me that I am not able to look up: yea, they are more in number than the hairs of my head, and my heart hath failed me.
40.16: O Lord, let it be thy pleasure to deliver me: make haste, O Lord, to help me.
40.17: Let them be ashamed and confounded together, that seek after my soul to destroy it: let them be driven backward and put to rebuke, that wish me evil.
40.18: Let them be desolate, and rewarded with shame: that say unto me, Fie upon thee, fie upon thee.
40.19: Let all those that seek thee be joyful and glad in thee: and let such as love thy salvation say alway, The Lord be praised.
40.20: As for me, I am poor and needy: but the Lord careth for me.
40.21: Thou art my helper and redeemer: make no long tarrying, O my God.

41.1: Blessed is he that considereth the poor and needy: the Lord shall deliver him in the time of trouble.
41.2: The Lord preserve him, and keep him alive, that he may be blessed upon earth: and deliver not thou him into the will of his enemies.
41.3: The Lord comfort him, when he lieth sick upon his bed: make thou all his bed in his sickness.
41.4: I said, Lord, be merciful unto me: heal my soul, for I have sinned against thee.
41.5: Mine enemies speak evil of me: When shall he die, and his name perish?
41.6: And if he come to see me, he speaketh vanity: and his heart conceiveth falsehood within himself, and when he cometh forth he telleth it.
41.7: All mine enemies whisper together against me: even against me do they imagine this evil.
41.8: Let the sentence of guiltiness proceed against him: and now that he lieth, let him rise up no more.
41.9: Yea, even mine own familiar friend, whom I trusted: who did also eat of my bread, hath laid great wait for me.
41.10: But be thou merciful unto me, O Lord: raise thou me up again, and I shall reward them.
41.11: By this I know thou favourest me: that mine enemy doth not triumph against me.
41.12: And when I am in my health, thou upholdest me: and shalt set me before thy face for ever.
41.13: Blessed be the Lord God of Israel: world without end. Amen.

42.1: Like as the hart desireth the water-brooks: so longeth my soul after thee, O God.
42.2: My soul is athirst for God, yea, even for the living God: when shall I come to appear before the presence of God?
42.3: My tears have been my meat day and night: while they daily say unto me, Where is now thy God?
42.4: Now when I think thereupon, I pour out my heart by myself: for I went with the multitude, and brought them forth into the house of God;
42.5: In the voice of praise and thanksgiving: among such as keep holy-day.
42.6: Why art thou so full of heaviness, O my soul: and why art thou so disquieted within me?
42.7: Put thy trust in God: for I will yet give him thanks for the help of his countenance.
42.8: My God, my soul is vexed within me: therefore will I remember thee concerning the land of Jordan, and the little hill of Hermon.
42.9: One deep calleth another, because of the noise of thy water-pipes: all thy waves and storms are gone over me.
42.10: The Lord hath granted his loving-kindness in the day-time: and in the night-season did I sing of him, and made my prayer unto the God of my life.
42.11: I will say unto the God of my strength, Why hast thou forgotten me: why go I thus heavily, while the enemy oppresseth me?
42.12: My bones are smitten asunder as with a sword: while mine enemies that trouble me cast me in the teeth;
42.13: Namely, while they say daily unto me: Where is now thy God?
42.14: Why art thou so vexed, O my soul: and why art thou so disquieted within me?
42.15: O put thy trust in God: for I will yet thank him, which is the help of my countenance, and my God.

43.1: Give sentence with me, O God, and defend my cause against the ungodly people: O deliver me from the deceitful and wicked man.
43.2: For thou art the God of my strength, why hast thou put me from thee: and why go I so heavily, while the enemy oppresseth me?
43.3: O send out thy light and thy truth, that they may lead me: and bring me unto thy holy hill, and to thy dwelling.
43.4: And that I may go unto the altar of God, even unto the God of my joy and gladness: and upon the harp will I give thanks unto thee, O God, my God.
43.5: Why art thou so heavy, O my soul: and why art thou so disquieted within me?
43.6: O put thy trust in God: for I will yet give him thanks, which is the help of my countenance, and my God.

44.1: We have heard with our ears, O God, our fathers have told us: what thou hast done in their time of old;
44.2: How thou hast driven out the heathen with thy hand, and planted them in: how thou hast destroyed the nations and cast them out.
44.3: For they gat not the land in possession through their own sword: neither was it their own arm that helped them;
44.4: But thy right hand, and thine arm, and the light of thy countenance: because thou hadst a favour unto them.
44.5: Thou art my King, O God: send help unto Jacob.
44.6: Through thee will we overthrow our enemies: and in thy Name will we tread them under, that rise up against us.
44.7: For I will not trust in my bow: it is not my sword that shall help me;
44.8: But it is thou that savest us from our enemies: and puttest them to confusion that hate us.
44.9: We make our boast of God all day long: and will praise thy Name for ever.
44.10: But now thou art far off, and puttest us to confusion: and goest not forth with our armies.
44.11: Thou makest us to turn our backs upon our enemies: so that they which hate us spoil our goods.
44.12: Thou lettest us be eaten up like sheep: and hast scattered us among the heathen.
44.13: Thou sellest thy people for nought: and takest no money for them.
44.14: Thou makest us to be rebuked of our neighbours: to be laughed to scorn, and had in derision of them that are round about us.
44.15: Thou makest us to be a by-word among the heathen: and that the people shake their heads at us.
44.16: My confusion is daily before me: and the shame of my face hath covered me;
44.17: For the voice of the slanderer and blasphemer: for the enemy and avenger.
44.18: And though all this be come upon us, yet do we not forget thee: nor behave ourselves frowardly in thy covenant.
44.19: Our heart is not turned back: neither our steps gone out of thy way;
44.20: No, not when thou hast smitten us into the place of dragons: and covered us with the shadow of death.
44.21: If we have forgotten the Name of our God, and holden up our hands to any strange god: shall not God search it out? for he knoweth the very secrets of the heart.
44.22: For thy sake also are we killed all the day long: and are counted as sheep appointed to be slain.
44.23: Up, Lord, why sleepest thou: awake, and be not absent from us for ever.
44.24: Wherefore hidest thou thy face: and forgettest our misery and trouble?
44.25: For our soul is brought low, even unto the dust: our belly cleaveth unto the ground.
44.26: Arise, and help us: and deliver us for thy mercy's sake.

45.1: My heart is inditing of a good matter: I speak of the things which I have made unto the King.
45.2: My tongue is the pen: of a ready writer.
45.3: Thou art fairer than the children of men: full of grace are thy lips, because God hath blessed thee for ever.
45.4: Gird thee with thy sword upon thy thigh, O thou most Mighty: according to thy worship and renown.
45.5: Good luck have thou with thine honour: ride on, because of the word of truth, of meekness, and righteousness; and thy right hand shall teach thee terrible things.
45.6: Thy arrows are very sharp, and the people shall be subdued unto thee: even in the midst among the King's enemies.
45.7: Thy seat, O God, endureth for ever: the sceptre of thy kingdom is a right sceptre.
45.8: Thou hast loved righteousness, and hated iniquity: wherefore God, even thy God, hath anointed thee with the oil of gladness above thy fellows.
45.9: All thy garments smell of myrrh, aloes, and cassia: out of the ivory palaces, whereby they have made thee glad.
45.10: Kings daughters were among thy honourable women: upon thy right hand did stand the queen in a vesture of gold, wrought about with divers colours.
45.11: Hearken, O daughter, and consider, incline thine ear: forget also thine own people, and thy father's house.
45.12: So shall the King have pleasure in thy beauty: for he is thy Lord God, and worship thou him.
45.13: And the daughter of Tyre shall be there with a gift: like as the rich also among the people shall make their supplication before thee.
45.14: The King's daughter is all glorious within: her clothing is of wrought gold.
45.15: She shall be brought unto the King in raiment of needle-work: the virgins that be her fellows shall bear her company, and shall be brought unto thee.
45.16: With joy and gladness shall they be brought: and shall enter into the King's palace.
45.17: Instead of thy fathers thou shalt have children: whom thou mayest make princes in all lands.
45.18: I will remember thy Name from one generation to another: therefore shall the people give thanks unto thee, world without end.

46.1: God is our hope and strength: a very present help in trouble.
46.2: Therefore will we not fear, though the earth be moved: and though the hills be carried into the midst of the sea;
46.3: Though the waters thereof rage and swell: and though the mountains shake at the tempest of the same.
46.4: The rivers of the flood thereof shall make glad the city of God: the holy place of the tabernacle of the most Highest.
46.5: God is in the midst of her, therefore shall she not be removed: God shall help her, and that right early.
46.6: The heathen make much ado, and the kingdoms are moved: but God hath shewed his voice, and the earth shall melt away.
46.7: The Lord of hosts is with us: the God of Jacob is our refuge.
46.8: O come hither, and behold the works of the Lord: what destruction he hath brought upon the earth.
46.9: He maketh wars to cease in all the world: he breaketh the bow, and knappeth the spear in sunder, and burneth the chariots in the fire.
46.10: Be still then, and know that I am God: I will be exalted among the heathen, and I will be exalted in the earth.
46.11: The Lord of hosts is with us: the God of Jacob is our refuge.

47.1: O clap your hands together, all ye people: O sing unto God with the voice of melody.
47.2: For the Lord is high, and to be feared: he is the great King upon all the earth.
47.3: He shall subdue the people under us: and the nations under our feet.
47.4: He shall choose out an heritage for us: even the worship of Jacob, whom he loved.
47.5: God is gone up with a merry noise: and the Lord with the sound of the trump.
47.6: O sing praises, sing praises unto our God: O sing praises, sing praises unto our King.
47.7: For God is the King of all the earth: sing ye praises with understanding.
47.8: God reigneth over the heathen: God sitteth upon his holy seat.
47.9: The princes of the people are joined unto the people of the God of Abraham: for God, which is very high exalted, doth defend the earth, as it were with a shield.

48.1: Great is the Lord, and highly to be praised: in the city of our God, even upon his holy hill.
48.2: The hill of Sion is a fair place, and the joy of the whole earth: upon the north-side lieth the city of the great King; God is well known in her palaces as a sure refuge.
48.3: For lo, the kings of the earth: are gathered, and gone by together.
48.4: They marvelled to see such things: they were astonished, and suddenly cast down.
48.5: Fear came there upon them, and sorrow: as upon a woman in her travail.
48.6: Thou shalt break the ships of the sea: through the east-wind.
48.7: Like as we have heard, so have we seen in the city of the Lord of hosts, in the city of our God: God upholdeth the same for ever.
48.8: We wait for thy loving-kindness, O God: in the midst of thy temple.
48.9: O God, according to thy Name, so is thy praise unto the world's end: thy right hand is full of righteousness.
48.10: Let the mount Sion rejoice, and the daughter of Judah be glad: because of thy judgements.
48.11: Walk about Sion, and go round about her: and tell the towers thereof.
48.12: Mark well her bulwarks, set up her houses: that ye may tell them that come after.
48.13: For this God is our God for ever and ever: he shall be our guide unto death.

49.1: O hear ye this, all ye people: ponder it with your ears, all ye that dwell in the world;
49.2: High and low, rich and poor: one with another.
49.3: My mouth shall speak of wisdom: and my heart shall muse of understanding.
49.4: I will incline mine ear to the parable: and shew my dark speech upon the harp.
49.5: Wherefore should I fear in the days of wickedness: and when the wickedness of my heels compasseth me round about?
49.6: There be some that put their trust in their goods: and boast themselves in the multitude of their riches.
49.7: But no man may deliver his brother: nor make agreement unto God for him;
49.8: For it cost more to redeem their souls: so that he must let that alone for ever;
49.9: Yea, though he live long: and see not the grave.
49.10: For he seeth that wise men also die, and perish together: as well as the ignorant and foolish, and leave their riches for other.
49.11: And yet they think that their houses shall continue for ever: and that their dwelling-places shall endure from one generation to another; and call the lands after their own names.
49.12: Nevertheless, man will not abide in honour: seeing he may be compared unto the beasts that perish; this is the way of them.
