49.13: This is their foolishness: and their posterity praise their saying.
49.14: They lie in the hell like sheep, death gnaweth upon them, and the righteous shall have domination over them in the morning: their beauty shall consume in the sepulchre out of their dwelling.
49.15: But God hath delivered my soul from the place of hell: for he shall receive me.
49.16: Be not thou afraid, though one be made rich: or if the glory of his house be increased;
49.17: For he shall carry nothing away with him when he dieth: neither shall his pomp follow him.
49.18: For while he lived, he counted himself an happy man: and so long as thou doest well unto thyself, men will speak good of thee.
49.19: He shall follow the generation of his fathers: and shall never see light.
49.20: Man being in honour hath no understanding: but is compared unto the beasts that perish.

50.1: The Lord, even the most mighty God, hath spoken: and called the world, from the rising up of the sun unto the going down thereof.
50.2: Out of Sion hath God appeared: in perfect beauty.
50.3: Our God shall come, and shall not keep silence: there shall go before him a consuming fire, and a mighty tempest shall be stirred up round about him.
50.4: He shall call the heaven from above: and the earth, that he may judge his people.
50.5: Gather my saints together unto me: those that have made a covenant with me with sacrifice.
50.6: And the heavens shall declare his righteousness: for God is Judge himself.
50.7: Hear, O my people, and I will speak: I myself will testify against thee, O Israel; for I am God, even thy God.
50.8: I will not reprove thee because of thy sacrifices, or for thy burnt-offerings: because they were not alway before me.
50.9: I will take no bullock out of thine house: nor he-goat out of thy folds.
50.10: For all the beasts of the forest are mine: and so are the cattle upon a thousand hills.
50.11: I know all the fowls upon the mountains: and the wild beasts of the field are in my sight.
50.12: If I be hungry, I will not tell thee: for the whole world is mine, and all that is therein.
50.13: Thinkest thou that I will eat bulls flesh: and drink the blood of goats?
50.14: Offer unto God thanksgiving: and pay thy vows unto the most Highest.
50.15: And call upon me in the time of trouble: so will I hear thee, and thou shalt praise me.
50.16: But unto the ungodly said God: Why dost thou preach my laws, and takest my covenant in thy mouth;
50.17: Whereas thou hatest to be reformed: and hast cast my words behind thee?
50.18: When thou sawest a thief, thou consentedst unto him: and hast been partaker with the adulterers.
50.19: Thou hast let thy mouth speak wickedness: and with thy tongue thou hast set forth deceit.
50.20: Thou satest, and spakest against thy brother: yea, and hast slandered thine own mother's son.
50.21: These things hast thou done, and I held my tongue, and thou thoughtest wickedly, that I am even such a one as thyself: but I will reprove thee, and set before thee the things that thou hast done.
50.22: O consider this, ye that forget God: lest I pluck you away, and there be none to deliver you.
50.23: Whoso offereth me thanks and praise, he honoureth me: and to him that ordereth his conversation right will I shew the salvation of God.

51.1: Have mercy upon me, O God, after thy great goodness: according to the multitude of thy mercies do away mine offences.
51.2: Wash me throughly from my wickedness: and cleanse me from my sin.
51.3: For I acknowledge my faults: and my sin is ever before me.
51.4: Against thee only have I sinned, and done this evil in thy sight: that thou mightest be justified in thy saying, and clear when thou art judged.
51.5: Behold, I was shapen in wickedness: and in sin hath my mother conceived me.
51.6: But lo, thou requirest truth in the inward parts: and shalt make me to understand wisdom secretly.
51.7: Thou shalt purge me with hyssop, and I shall be clean: thou shalt wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow.
51.8: Thou shalt make me hear of joy and gladness: that the bones which thou hast broken may rejoice.
51.9: Turn thy face from my sins: and put out all my misdeeds.
51.10: Make me a clean heart, O God: and renew a right spirit within me.
51.11: Cast me not away from thy presence: and take not thy holy Spirit from me.
51.12: O give me the comfort of thy help again: and stablish me with thy free Spirit.
51.13: Then shall I teach thy ways unto the wicked: and sinners shall be converted unto thee.
51.14: Deliver me from blood-guiltiness, O God, thou that art the God of my health: and my tongue shall sing of thy righteousness.
51.15: Thou shalt open my lips, O Lord: and my mouth shall shew thy praise.
51.16: For thou desirest no sacrifice, else would I give it thee: but thou delightest not in burnt-offerings.
51.17: The sacrifice of God is a troubled spirit: a broken and contrite heart, O God, shalt thou not despise.
51.18: O be favourable and gracious unto Sion: build thou the walls of Jerusalem.
51.19: Then shalt thou be pleased with the sacrifice of righteousness, with the burnt-offerings and oblations: then shall they offer young bullocks upon thine altar.

52.1: Why boastest thou thyself, thou tyrant: that thou canst do mischief;
52.2: Whereas the goodness of God: endureth yet daily?
52.3: Thy tongue imagineth wickedness: and with lies thou cuttest like a sharp rasor.
52.4: Thou hast loved unrighteousness more than goodness: and to talk of lies more than righteousness.
52.5: Thou hast loved to speak all words that may do hurt: O thou false tongue.
52.6: Therefore shall God destroy thee for ever: he shall take thee, and pluck thee out of thy dwelling, and root thee out of the land of the living.
52.7: The righteous also shall see this, and fear: and shall laugh him to scorn;
52.8: Lo, this is the man that took not God for his strength: but trusted unto the multitude of his riches, and strengthened himself in his wickedness.
52.9: As for me, I am like a green olive-tree in the house of God: my trust is in the tender mercy of God for ever and ever.
52.10: I will always give thanks unto thee for that thou hast done: and I will hope in thy Name, for thy saints like it well.

53.1: The foolish body hath said in his heart: There is no God.
53.2: Corrupt are they, and become abominable in their wickedness: there is none that doeth good.
53.3: God looked down from heaven upon the children of men: to see if there were any that would understand, and seek after God.
53.4: But they are all gone out of the way, they are altogether become abominable: there is also none that doeth good, no not one.
53.5: Are not they without understanding, that work wickedness: eating up my people as if they would eat bread? they have not called upon God.
53.6: They were afraid where no fear was: for God hath broken the bones of him that besieged thee; thou hast put them to confusion, because God hath despised them.
53.7: O that the salvation were given unto Israel out of Sion: O that the Lord would deliver his people out of captivity!
53.8: Then should Jacob rejoice: and Israel should be right glad.

54.1: Save me, O God, for thy Name's sake: and avenge me in thy strength.
54.2: Hear my prayer, O God: and hearken unto the words of my mouth.
54.3: For strangers are risen up against me: and tyrants, which have not God before their eyes, seek after my soul.
54.4: Behold, God is my helper: the Lord is with them that uphold my soul.
54.5: He shall reward evil unto mine enemies: destroy thou them in thy truth.
54.6: An offering of a free heart will I give thee, and praise thy Name, O Lord: because it is so comfortable.
54.7: For he hath delivered me out of all my trouble: and mine eye hath seen his desire upon mine enemies.

55.1: Hear my prayer, O God: and hide not thyself from my petition.
55.2: Take heed unto me, and hear me: how I mourn in my prayer, and am vexed.
55.3: The enemy crieth so, and the ungodly cometh on so fast: for they are minded to do me some mischief; so maliciously are they set against me.
55.4: My heart is disquieted within me: and the fear of death is fallen upon me.
55.5: Tearfulness and trembling are come upon me: and an horrible dread hath overwhelmed me.
55.6: And I said, O that I had wings like a dove: for then would I flee away, and be at rest.
55.7: Lo, then would I get me away far off: and remain in the wilderness.
55.8: I would make haste to escape: because of the stormy wind and tempest.
55.9: Destroy their tongues, O Lord, and divide them: for I have spied unrighteousness and strife in the city.
55.10: Day and night they go about within the walls thereof: mischief also and sorrow are in the midst of it.
55.11: Wickedness is therein: deceit and guile go not out of her streets.
55.12: For it is not an open enemy, that hath done me this dishonour: for then I could have borne it.
55.13: Neither was it mine adversary, that did magnify himself against me: for then peradventure I would have hid myself from him.
55.14: But it was even thou, my companion: my guide, and mine own familiar friend.
55.15: We took sweet counsel together: and walked in the house of God as friends.
55.16: Let death come hastily upon them, and let them go down quick into hell: for wickedness is in their dwellings, and among them.
55.17: As for me, I will call upon God: and the Lord shall save me.
55.18: In the evening, and morning, and at noon-day will I pray, and that instantly: and he shall hear my voice.
55.19: It is he that hath delivered my soul in peace from the battle that was against me: for there were many with me.
55.20: Yea, even God, that endureth for ever, shall hear me, and bring them down: for they will not turn, nor fear God.
55.21: He laid his hands upon such as be at peace with him: and he brake his covenant.
55.22: The words of his mouth were softer than butter, having war in his heart: his words were smoother than oil, and yet be they very swords.
55.23: O cast thy burden upon the Lord, and he shall nourish thee: and shall not suffer the righteous to fall for ever.
55.24: And as for them: thou, O God, shalt bring them into the pit of destruction.
55.25: The blood-thirsty and deceitful men shall not live out half their days: nevertheless, my trust shall be in thee, O Lord.

56.1: Be merciful unto me, O God, for man goeth about to devour me: he is daily fighting, and troubling me.
56.2: Mine enemies are daily in hand to swallow me up: for they be many that fight against me, thou most Highest.
56.3: Nevertheless, though I am sometime afraid: yet put I my trust in thee.
56.4: I will praise God, because of his word: I have put my trust in God, and will not fear what flesh can do unto me.
56.5: They daily mistake my words: all that they imagine is to do me evil.
56.6: They hold all together, and keep themselves close: and mark my steps, when they lay wait for my soul.
56.7: Shall they escape for their wickedness: thou, O God, in thy displeasure shalt cast them down.
56.8: Thou tellest my Sittings; put my tears into thy bottle: are not these things noted in thy book?
56.9: Whensoever I call upon thee, then shall mine enemies be put to flight: this I know; for God is on my side.
56.10: In God's word will I rejoice: in the Lord's word will I comfort me.
56.11: Yea, in God have I put my trust: I will not be afraid what man can do unto me.
56.12: Unto thee, O God, will I pay my vows: unto thee will I give thanks.
56.13: For thou hast delivered my soul from death, and my feet from falling: that I may walk before God in the light of the living.

57.1: Be merciful unto me, O God, be merciful unto me, for my soul trusteth in thee: and under the shadow of thy wings shall be my refuge, until this tyranny be over-past.
57.2: I will call unto the most high God: even unto the God that shall perform the cause which I have in hand.
57.3: He shall send from heaven: and save me from the reproof of him that would eat me up.
57.4: God shall send forth his mercy and truth: my soul is among lions.
57.5: And I lie even among the children of men, that are set on fire: whose teeth are spears and arrows, and their tongue a sharp sword.
57.6: Set up thyself, O God, above the heavens: and thy glory above all the earth.
57.7: They have laid a net for my feet, and pressed down my soul: they have digged a pit before me, and are fallen into the midst of it themselves.
57.8: My heart is fixed, O God, my heart is fixed: I will sing, and give praise.
57.9: Awake up, my glory; awake, lute and harp: I myself will awake right early.
57.10: I will give thanks unto thee, O Lord, among the people: and I will sing unto thee among the nations.
57.11: For the greatness of thy mercy reacheth unto the heavens: and thy truth unto the clouds.
57.12: Set up thyself, O God, above the heavens: and thy glory above all the earth.

58.1: Are your minds set upon righteousness, O ye congregation: and do ye judge the thing that is right, O ye sons of men?
58.2: Yea, ye imagine mischief in your heart upon the earth: and your hands deal with wickedness.
58.3: The ungodly are froward, even from their mother's womb: as soon as they are born, they go astray, and speak lies.
58.4: They are as venomous as the poison of a serpent: even like the deaf adder that stoppeth her ears;
58.5: Which refuseth to hear the voice of the charmer: charm he never so wisely.
58.6: Break their teeth, O God, in their mouths; smite the jaw-bones of the lions, O Lord: let them fall away like water that runneth apace; and when they shoot their arrows let them be rooted out.
58.7: Let them consume away like a snail, and be like the untimely fruit of a woman: and let them not see the sun.
58.8: Or ever your pots be made hot with thorns: so let indignation vex him, even as a thing that is raw.
58.9: The righteous shall rejoice when he seeth the vengeance: he shall wash his footsteps in the blood of the ungodly.
58.10: So that a man shall say, Verily there is a reward for the righteous: doubtless there is a God that judgeth the earth.

59.1: Deliver me from mine enemies, O God: defend me from them that rise up against me.
59.2: O deliver me from the wicked doers: and save me from the blood-thirsty men.
59.3: For lo, they lie waiting for my soul: the mighty men are gathered against me, without any offence or fault of me, O Lord.
59.4: They run and prepare themselves without my fault: arise thou therefore to help me, and behold.
59.5: Stand up, O Lord God of hosts, thou God of Israel, to visit all the heathen: and be not merciful unto them that offend of malicious wickedness.
59.6: They go to and fro in the evening: they grin like a dog, and run about through the city.
59.7: Behold, they speak with their mouth, and swords are in their lips: for who doth hear?
59.8: But thou, O Lord, shalt have them in derision: and thou shalt laugh all the heathen to scorn.
59.9: My strength will I ascribe unto thee: for thou art the God of my refuge.
59.10: God sheweth me his goodness plenteously: and God shall let me see my desire upon mine enemies.
59.11: Slay them not, lest my people forget it: but scatter them abroad among the people, and put them down, O Lord, our defence.
59.12: For the sin of their mouth, and for the words of their lips, they shall be taken in their pride: and why? their preaching is of cursing and lies.
59.13: Consume them in thy wrath, consume them, that they may perish: and know that it is God that ruleth in Jacob, and unto the ends of the world.
59.14: And in the evening they will return: grin like a dog, and will go about the city.
59.15: They will run here and there for meat: and grudge if they be not satisfied.
59.16: As for me, I will sing of thy power, and will praise thy mercy betimes in the morning: for thou hast been my defence and refuge in the day of my trouble.
59.17: Unto thee, O my strength, will I sing: for thou, O God, art my refuge, and my merciful God.

60.1: O God, thou hast cast us out, and scattered us abroad: thou hast also been displeased; O turn thee unto us again.
60.2: Thou hast moved the land, and divided it: heal the sores thereof, for it shaketh.
60.3: Thou hast shewed thy people heavy things: thou hast given us a drink of deadly wine.
60.4: Thou hast given a token for such as fear thee: that they may triumph because of the truth.
60.5: Therefore were thy beloved delivered: help me with thy right hand, and hear me.
60.6: God hath spoken in his holiness, I will rejoice, and divide Sichem: and mete out the valley of Succoth.
60.7: Gilead is mine, and Manasses is mine: Ephraim also is the strength of my head; Judah is my law-giver;
60.8: Moab is my wash-pot; over Edom will I cast out my shoe: Philistia, be thou glad of me.
60.9: Who will lead me into the strong city: who will bring me into Edom?
60.10: Hast not thou cast us out, O God: wilt not thou, O God, go out with our hosts?
60.11: O be thou our help in trouble: for vain is the help of man.
60.12: Through God will we do great acts: for it is he that shall tread down our enemies.

61.1: Hear my crying, O God: give ear unto my prayer.
61.2: From the ends of the earth will I call upon thee: when my heart is in heaviness.
61.3: O set me up upon the rock that is higher than I: for thou hast been my hope, and a strong tower for me against the enemy.
61.4: I will dwell in thy tabernacle for ever: and my trust shall be under the covering of thy wings.
61.5: For thou, O Lord, hast heard my desires: and hast given an heritage unto those that fear thy Name.
61.6: Thou shalt grant the King a long life: that his years may endure throughout all generations.
61.7: He shall dwell before God for ever: O prepare thy loving mercy and faithfulness, that they may preserve him.
61.8: So will I always sing praise unto thy Name: that I may daily perform my vows.

62.1: My soul truly waiteth still upon God: for of him cometh my salvation.
62.2: He verily is my strength and my salvation: he is my defence, so that I shall not greatly fall.
62.3: How long will ye imagine mischief against every man: ye shall be slain all the sort of you; yea, as a tottering wall shall ye be, and like a broken hedge.
62.4: Their device is only how to put him out whom God will exalt: their delight is in lies; they give good words with their mouth, but curse with their heart.
62.5: Nevertheless, my soul, wait thou still upon God: for my hope is in him.
62.6: He truly is my strength and my salvation: he is my defence, so that I shall not fall.
62.7: In God is my health, and my glory: the rock of my might, and in God is my trust.
62.8: O put your trust in him alway, ye people: pour out your hearts before him, for God is our hope.
62.9: As for the children of men, they are but vanity: the children of men are deceitful upon the weights, they are altogether lighter than vanity itself.
62.10: O trust not in wrong and robbery, give not yourselves unto vanity: if riches increase, set not your heart upon them.
62.11: God spake once, and twice I have also heard the same: that power belongeth unto God;
62.12: And that thou, Lord, art merciful: for thou rewardest every man according to his work.

63.1: O God, thou art my God: early will I seek thee.
63.2: My soul thirsteth for thee, my flesh also longeth after thee: in a barren and dry land where no water is.
63.3: Thus have I looked for thee in holiness: that I might behold thy power and glory.
63.4: For thy loving-kindness is better than the life itself: my lips shall praise thee.
63.5: As long as I live will I magnify thee on this manner: and lift up my hands in thy Name.
63.6: My soul shall be satisfied, even as it were with marrow and fatness: when my mouth praiseth thee with joyful lips.
63.7: Have I not remembered thee in my bed: and thought upon thee when I was waking?
63.8: Because thou hast been my helper: therefore under the shadow of thy wings will I rejoice.
63.9: My soul hangeth upon thee: thy right hand hath upholden me.
63.10: These also that seek the hurt of my soul: they shall go under the earth.
63.11: Let them fall upon the edge of the sword: that they may be a portion for foxes.
63.12: But the King shall rejoice in God; all they also that swear by him shall be commended: for the mouth of them that speak lies shall be stopped.

64.1: Hear my voice, O God, in my prayer: preserve my life from fear of the enemy.
64.2: Hide me from the gathering together of the froward: and from the insurrection of wicked doers;
64.3: Who have whet their tongue like a sword: and shoot out their arrows, even bitter words;
64.4: That they may privily shoot at him that is perfect: suddenly do they hit him, and fear not.
64.5: They encourage themselves in mischief: and commune among themselves how they may lay snares, and say that no man shall see them.
64.6: They imagine wickedness, and practise it: that they keep secret among themselves, every man in the deep of his heart.
64.7: But God shall suddenly shoot at them with a swift arrow: that they shall be wounded.
64.8: Yea, their own tongues shall make them fall: insomuch that whoso seeth them shall laugh them to scorn.
64.9: And all men that see it shall say, This hath God done: for they shall perceive that it is his work.
64.10: The righteous shall rejoice in the Lord, and put his trust in him: and all they that are true of heart shall be glad.

65.1: Thou, O God, art praised in Sion: and unto thee shall the vow be performed in Jerusalem.
65.2: Thou that hearest the prayer: unto thee shall all flesh come.
65.3: My misdeeds prevail against me: O be thou merciful unto our sins.
65.4: Blessed is the man whom thou choosest, and receivest unto thee: he shall dwell in thy court, and shall be satisfied with the pleasures of thy house, even of thy holy temple.
65.5: Thou shalt shew us wonderful things in thy righteousness, O God of our salvation: thou that art the hope of all the ends of the earth, and of them that remain in the broad sea.
65.6: Who in his strength setteth fast the mountains: and is girded about with power.
65.7: Who stilleth the raging of the sea: and the noise of his waves, and the madness of the people.
65.8: They also that dwell in the uttermost parts of the earth shall be afraid at thy tokens: thou that makest the outgoings of the morning and evening to praise thee.
65.9: Thou visitest the earth, and blessest it: thou makest it very plenteous.
65.10: The river of God is full of water: thou preparest their corn, for so thou providest for the earth.
65.11: Thou waterest her furrows, thou sendest rain into the little valleys thereof: thou makest it soft with the drops of rain, and blessest the increase of it.
65.12: Thou crownest the year with thy goodness: and thy clouds drop fatness.
65.13: They shall drop upon the dwellings of the wilderness: and the little hills shall rejoice on every side.
65.14: The folds shall be full of sheep: the valleys also shall stand so thick with corn, that they shall laugh and sing.

66.1: O be joyful in God, all ye lands: sing praises unto the honour of his Name, make his praise to be glorious.
66.2: Say unto God, O how wonderful art thou in thy works: through the greatness of thy power shall thine enemies be found liars unto thee.
66.3: For all the world shall worship thee: sing of thee, and praise thy Name.
66.4: O come hither, and behold the works of God: how wonderful he is in his doing toward the children of men.
66.5: He turned the sea into dry land: so that they went through the water on foot; there did we rejoice thereof.
66.6: He ruleth with his power for ever; his eyes behold the people: and such as will not believe shall not be able to exalt themselves.
66.7: O praise our God, ye people: and make the voice of his praise to be heard;
66.8: Who holdeth our soul in life: and suffereth not our feet to slip.
66.9: For thou, O God, hast proved us: thou also hast tried us, like as silver is tried.
66.10: Thou broughtest us into the snare: and laidest trouble upon our loins.
66.11: Thou sufferedst men to ride over our heads: we went through fire and water, and thou broughtest us out into a wealthy place.
66.12: I will go into thine house with burnt-offerings: and will pay thee my vows, which I promised with my lips, and spake with my mouth, when I was in trouble.
66.13: I will offer unto thee fat burnt-sacrifices, with the incense of rams: I will offer bullocks and goats.
66.14: O come hither, and hearken, all ye that fear God: and I will tell you what he hath done for my soul.
66.15: I called unto him with my mouth: and gave him praises with my tongue.
66.16: If I incline unto wickedness with mine heart: the Lord will not hear me.
66.17: But God hath heard me: and considered the voice of my prayer.
66.18: Praised be God, who hath not cast out my prayer: nor turned his mercy from me.

67.1: God be merciful unto us, and bless us: and shew us the light of his countenance, and be merciful unto us:
67.2: That thy way may be known upon earth: thy saving health among all nations.
67.3: Let the people praise thee, O God: yea, let all the people praise thee.
67.4: O let the nations rejoice and be glad: for thou shalt judge the folk righteously, and govern the nations upon earth.
67.5: Let the people praise thee, O God: let all the people praise thee.
67.6: Then shall the earth bring forth her increase: and God, even our own God, shall give us his blessing.
67.7: God shall bless us: and all the ends of the world shall fear him.

68.1: Let God arise, and let his enemies be scattered: let them also that hate him flee before him.
68.2: Like as the smoke vanisheth, so shalt thou drive them away: and like as wax melteth at the fire, so let the ungodly perish at the presence of God.
68.3: But let the righteous be glad and rejoice before God: let them also be merry and joyful.
68.4: O sing unto God, and sing praises unto his Name: magnify him that rideth upon the heavens, as it were upon an horse; praise him in his Name JAH, and rejoice before him.
68.5: He is a father of the fatherless, and defendeth the cause of the widows: even God in his holy habitation.
68.6: He is the God that maketh men to be of one mind in an house, and bringeth the prisoners out of captivity: but letteth the runagates continue in scarceness.
68.7: O God, when thou wentest forth before the people: when thou wentest through the wilderness;
68.8: The earth shook, and the heavens dropped at the presence of God: even as Sinai also was moved at the presence of God, who is the God of Israel.
68.9: Thou, O God, sentest a gracious rain upon thine inheritance: and refreshedst it when it was weary.
68.10: Thy congregation shall dwell therein: for thou, O God, hast of thy goodness prepared for the poor.
68.11: The Lord gave the word: great was the company of the preachers.
68.12: Kings with their armies did flee, and were discomfited: and they of the household divided the spoil.
68.13: Though ye have lien among the pots, yet shall ye be as the wings of a dove: that is covered with silver wings, and her feathers like gold.
68.14: When the Almighty scattered kings for their sake: then were they as white as snow in Salmon.
68.15: As the hill of Basan, so is God's hill: even an high hill, as the hill of Basan.
68.16: Why hop ye so, ye high hills? this is God's hill, in the which it pleaseth him to dwell: yea, the Lord will abide in it for ever.
68.17: The chariots of God are twenty thousand, even thousands of angels: and the Lord is among them, as in the holy place of Sinai.
68.18: Thou art gone up on high, thou hast led captivity captive, and received gifts for men: yea, even for thine enemies, that the Lord God might dwell among them.
68.19: Praised be the Lord daily: even the God who helpeth us, and poureth his benefits upon us.
68.20: He is our God, even the God of whom cometh salvation: God is the Lord, by whom we escape death.
68.21: God shall wound the head of his enemies: and the hairy scalp of such a one as goeth on still in his wickedness.
68.22: The Lord hath said, I will bring my people again, as I did from Basan: mine own will I bring again, as I did sometime from the deep of the sea.
68.23: That thy foot may be dipped in the blood of thine enemies: and that the tongue of thy dogs may be red through the same.
68.24: It is well seen, O God, how thou goest: how thou, my God and King, goest in the sanctuary.
68.25: The singers go before, the minstrels follow after: in the midst are the damsels playing with the timbrels.
68.26: Give thanks, O Israel, unto God the Lord in the congregations: from the ground of the heart.
68.27: There is little Benjamin their ruler, and the princes of Judah their counsel: the princes of Zabulon, and the princes of Nephthali.
68.28: Thy God hath sent forth strength for thee: stablish the thing, O God, that thou hast wrought in us,
68.29: For thy temple's sake at Jerusalem: so shall kings bring presents unto thee.
68.30: When the company of the spear-men and multitude of the mighty are scattered abroad among the beasts of the people, so that they humbly bring pieces of silver: and when he hath scattered the people that delight in war;
68.31: Then shall the princes come out of Egypt: the Morians' land shall soon stretch out her hands unto God.
68.32: Sing unto God, O ye kingdoms of the earth: O sing praises unto the Lord;
68.33: Who sitteth in the heavens over all from the beginning: lo, he doth send out his voice, yea, and that a mighty voice.
68.34: Ascribe ye the power to God over Israel: his worship and strength is in the clouds.
68.35: O God, wonderful art thou in thy holy places: even the God of Israel, he will give strength and power unto his people; blessed be God.

69.1: Save me, O God: for the waters are come in, even unto my soul.
69.2: I stick fast in the deep mire, where no ground is: I am come into deep waters, so that the floods run over me.
69.3: I am weary of crying; my throat is dry: my sight faileth me for waiting so long upon my God.
69.4: They that hate me without a cause are more than the hairs of my head: they that are mine enemies, and would destroy me guiltless, are mighty.
69.5: I paid them the things that I never took: God, thou knowest my simpleness, and my faults are not hid from thee.
69.6: Let not them that trust in thee, O Lord God of hosts, be ashamed for my cause: let not those that seek thee be confounded through me, O Lord God of Israel.
69.7: And why? for thy sake have I suffered reproof: shame hath covered my face.
69.8: I am become a stranger unto my brethren: even an alien unto my mother's children.
69.9: For the zeal of thine house hath even eaten me: and the rebukes of them that rebuked thee are fallen upon me.
69.10: I wept, and chastened myself with fasting: and that was turned to my reproof.
69.11: I put on sackcloth also: and they jested upon me.
69.12: They that sit in the gate speak against me: and the drunkards make songs upon me.
69.13: But, Lord, I make my prayer unto thee: in an acceptable time.
69.14: Hear me, O God, in the multitude of thy mercy: even in the truth of thy salvation.
69.15: Take me out of the mire, that I sink not: O let me be delivered from them that hate me, and out of the deep waters.
69.16: Let not the water-flood drown me, neither let the deep swallow me up: and let not the pit shut her mouth upon me.
69.17: Hear me, O Lord, for thy loving-kindness is comfortable: turn thee unto me according to the multitude of thy mercies.
69.18: And hide not thy face from thy servant, for I am in trouble: O haste thee, and hear me.
69.19: Draw nigh unto my soul, and save it: O deliver me, because of mine enemies.
69.20: Thou hast known my reproof, my shame, and my dishonour: mine adversaries are all in thy sight.
69.21: Thy rebuke hath broken my heart; I am full of heaviness: I looked for some to have pity on me, but there was no man, neither found I any to comfort me.
69.22: They gave me gall to eat: and when I was thirsty they gave me vinegar to drink.
69.23: Let their table be made a snare to take themselves withal: and let the things that should have been for their wealth be unto them an occasion of falling.
69.24: Let their eyes be blinded, that they see not: and ever bow thou down their backs.
69.25: Pour out thine indignation upon them: and let thy wrathful displeasure take hold of them.
69.26: Let their habitation be void: and no man to dwell in their tents.
69.27: For they persecute him whom thou hast smitten: and they talk how they may vex them whom thou hast wounded.
69.28: Let them fall from one wickedness to another: and riot come into thy righteousness.
69.29: Let them be wiped out of the book of the living: and not be written among the righteous.
69.30: As for me, when I am poor and in heaviness: thy help, O God, shall lift me up.
69.31: I will praise the Name of God with a song: and magnify it with thanksgiving.
69.32: This also shall please the Lord: better than a bullock that hath horns and hoofs.
69.33: The humble shall consider this, and be glad: seek ye after God, and your soul shall live.
69.34: For the Lord heareth the poor: and despiseth not his prisoners.
69.35: Let heaven and earth praise him: the sea, and all that moveth therein.
69.36: For God will save Sion, and build the cities of Judah: that men may dwell there, and have it in possession.
69.37: The posterity also of his servants shall inherit it: and they that love his Name shall dwell therein.

70.1: Haste thee, O God, to deliver me: make haste to help me, O Lord.
70.2: Let them be ashamed and confounded that seek after my soul: let them be turned back ward and put to confusion that wish me evil.
70.3: Let them for their reward be soon brought to shame: that cry over me, There, there.
70.4: But let all those that seek thee be joyful and glad in thee: and let all such as delight in thy salvation say alway, The Lord be praised.
70.5: As for me, I am poor and in misery: haste thee unto me, O God.
70.6: Thou art my helper and my redeemer: O Lord, make no long tarrying.

71.1: In thee, O Lord, have I put my trust, let me never be put to confusion: but rid me and deliver me in thy righteousness, incline thine ear unto me, and save me.
71.2: Be thou my strong hold, whereunto I may alway resort: thou hast promised to help me, for thou art my house of defence and my castle.
71.3: Deliver me, O my God, out of the hand of the ungodly: out of the hand of the unrighteous and cruel man.
71.4: For thou, O Lord God, art the thing that I long for: thou art my hope, even from my youth.
71.5: Through thee have I been holden up ever since I was born: thou art he that took me out of my mother's womb; my praise shall be always of thee.
71.6: I am become as it were a monster unto many: but my sure trust is in thee.
71.7: O let my mouth be filled with thy praise: that I may sing of thy glory and honour all the day long.
71.8: Cast me not away in the time of age: forsake me not when my strength faileth me.
71.9: For mine enemies speak against me, and they that lay wait for my soul take their counsel together, saying: God hath forsaken him; persecute him, and take him, for there is none to deliver him.
71.10: Go not far from me, O God: my God, haste thee to help me.
71.11: Let them be confounded and perish that are against my soul: let them be covered with shame and dishonour that seek to do me evil.
71.12: As for me, I will patiently abide alway: and will praise thee more and more.
71.13: My mouth shall daily speak of thy righteousness and salvation: for I know no end thereof.
71.14: I will go forth in the strength of the Lord God: and will make mention of thy righteousness only.
71.15: Thou, O God, hast taught me from my youth up until now: therefore will I tell of thy wondrous works.
71.16: Forsake me not, O God, in mine old age, when I am gray-headed: until I have shewed thy strength unto this generation, and thy power to all them that are yet for to come.
71.17: Thy righteousness, O God, is very high: and great things are they that thou hast done; O God, who is like unto thee?
71.18: O what great troubles and adversities hast thou shewed me, and yet didst thou turn and refresh me: yea, and broughtest me from the deep of the earth again.
71.19: Thou hast brought me to great honour: and comforted me on every side.
71.20: Therefore will I praise thee and thy faithfulness, O God, playing upon an instrument of music: unto thee will I sing upon the harp, O thou Holy One of Israel.
71.21: My lips will be fain when I sing unto thee: and so will my soul whom thou hast delivered.
71.22: My tongue also shall talk of thy righteousness all the day long: for they are confounded and brought unto shame that seek to do me evil.

72.1: Give the King thy judgements, O God: and thy righteousness unto the King's son.
72.2: Then shall he judge thy people according unto right: and defend the poor.
72.3: The mountains also shall bring peace: and the little hills righteousness unto the people.
72.4: He shall keep the simple folk by their right; defend the children of the poor, and punish the wrong-doer.
72.5: They shall fear thee, as long as the sun and moon endureth: from one generation to another.
72.6: He shall come down like the rain into a fleece of wool: even as the drops that water the earth.
72.7: In his time shall the righteous flourish: yea, and abundance of peace, so long as the moon endureth.
72.8: His dominion shall be also from the one sea to the other: and from the flood unto the world's end.
72.9: They that dwell in the wilderness shall kneel before him: his enemies shall lick the dust.
72.10: The kings of Tharsis and of the isles shall give presents: the kings of Arabia and Saba shall bring gifts.
72.11: All kings shall fall down before him: all nations shall do him service.
72.12: For he shall deliver the poor when he crieth: the needy also, and him that hath no helper.
72.13: He shall be favourable to the simple and needy: and shall preserve the souls of the poor.
72.14: He shall deliver their souls from falsehood and wrong: and dear shall their blood be in his sight.
72.15: He shall live, and unto him shall be given of the gold of Arabia: prayer shall be made ever unto him, and daily shall he be praised.
72.16: There shall be an heap of corn in the earth, high upon the hills: his fruit shall shake like Libanus, and shall be green in the city like grass upon the earth.
72.17: His Name shall endure for ever, his Name shall remain under the sun among the posterities: which shall be blessed through him, and all the heathen shall praise him.
72.18: Blessed be the Lord God, even the God of Israel: which only doeth wondrous things;
72.19: And blessed be the Name of his majesty for ever: and all the earth shall be filled with his majesty. Amen, Amen.

73.1: Truly God is loving unto Israel: even unto such as are of a clean heart.
73.2: Nevertheless, my feet were almost gone: my treadings had well-nigh slipt.
73.3: And why? I was grieved at the wicked: I do also see the ungodly in such prosperity.
73.4: For they are in no peril of death: but are lusty and strong.
73.5: They come in no misfortune like other folk: neither are they plagued like other men.
73.6: And this is the cause that they are so holden with pride: and overwhelmed with cruelty.
73.7: Their eyes swell with fatness: and they do even what they lust.
73.8: They corrupt other, and speak of wicked blasphemy: their talking is against the most High.
73.9: For they stretch forth their mouth unto the heaven: and their tongue goeth through the world.
73.10: Therefore fall the people unto them: and thereout suck they no small advantage.
73.11: Tush, say they, how should God perceive it: is there knowledge in the most High?
73.12: Lo, these are the ungodly, these prosper in the world, and these have riches in possession: and I said, Then have I cleansed my heart in vain, and washed my hands in innocency.
73.13: All the day long have I been punished: and chastened every morning.
73.14: Yea, and I had almost said even as they: but lo, then I should have condemned the gene ration of thy children.
73.15: Then thought I to understand this: but it was too hard for me,
73.16: Until I went into the sanctuary of God: then understood I the end of these men;
73.17: Namely, how thou dost set them in slippery places: and castest them down, and destroyest them.
73.18: O how suddenly do they consume: perish, and come to a fearful end!
73.19: Yea, even like as a dream when one awaketh: so shalt thou make their image to vanish out of the city.
73.20: Thus my heart was grieved: and it went even through my reins.
73.21: So foolish was I, and ignorant: even as it were a beast before thee.
73.22: Nevertheless, I am alway by thee: for thou hast holden me by my right hand.
73.23: Thou shalt guide me with thy counsel: and after that receive me with glory.
73.24: Whom have I in heaven but thee: and there is none upon earth that I desire in comparison of thee.
73.25: My flesh and my heart faileth: but God is the strength of my heart, and my portion for ever.
73.26: For lo, they that forsake thee shall perish: thou hast destroyed all them that commit fornication against thee.
73.27: But it is good for me to hold me fast by God, to put my trust in the Lord God: and to speak of all thy works in the gates of the daughter of Sion.

74.1: O God, wherefore art thou absent from us so long: why is thy wrath so hot against the sheep of thy pasture?
74.2: O think upon thy congregation: whom thou hast purchased and redeemed of old.
74.3: Think upon the tribe of thine inheritance: and Mount Sion, wherein thou hast dwelt.
74.4: Lift up thy feet, that thou mayest utterly destroy every enemy: which hath done evil in thy sanctuary.
74.5: Thine adversaries roar in the midst of thy congregations: and set up their banners for tokens.
74.6: He that hewed timber afore out of the thick trees: was known to bring it to an excellent work.
74.7: But now they break down all the carved work thereof: with axes and hammers.
74.8: They have set fire upon thy holy places: and have defiled the dwelling-place of thy Name, even unto the ground.
74.9: Yea, they said in their hearts, Let us make havock of them altogether: thus have they burnt up all the houses of God in the land.
74.10: We see not our tokens, there is not one prophet more: no, not one is there among us, that understandeth any more.
74.11: O God, how long shall the adversary do this dishonour: how long shall the enemy blaspheme thy Name, for ever?
74.12: Why withdrawest thou thy hand: why pluckest thou not thy right hand out of thy bosom to consume the enemy?
74.13: For God is my King of old: the help that is done upon earth he doeth it himself.
74.14: Thou didst divide the sea through thy power: thou brakest the heads of the dragons in the waters.
74.15: Thou smotest the heads of leviathan in pieces: and gavest him to be meat for the people in the wilderness.
74.16: Thou broughtest out fountains and waters out of the hard rocks: thou driedst up mighty waters.
74.17: The day is thine, and the night is thine: thou hast prepared the light and the sun.
74.18: Thou hast set all the borders of the earth: thou hast made summer and winter.
74.19: Remember this, O Lord, how the enemy hath rebuked: and how the foolish people hath blasphemed thy Name.
74.20: O deliver not the soul of thy turtle-dove unto the multitude of the enemies: and forget not the congregation of the poor for ever,
74.21: Look upon the covenant: for all the earth is full of darkness and cruel habitations.
74.22: O let not the simple go away ashamed: but let the poor and needy give praise unto thy Name.
74.23: Arise, O God, maintain thine own cause: remember how the foolish man blasphemeth thee daily.
74.24: Forget not the voice of thine enemies: the presumption of them that hate thee increaseth ever more and more.

75.1: Unto thee, O God, do we give thanks: yea, unto thee do we give thanks.
75.2: Thy Name also is so nigh: and that do thy wondrous works declare.
75.3: When I receive the congregation: I shall judge according unto right.
75.4: The earth is weak, and all the inhabiters thereof: I bear up the pillars of it.
75.5: I said unto the fools, Deal not so madly: and to the ungodly, Set not up your horn.
75.6: Set not up your horn on high: and speak not with a stiff neck.
75.7: For promotion cometh neither from the east, nor from the west: nor yet from the south.
75.8: And why? God is the Judge: he putteth down one, and setteth up another.
75.9: For in the hand of the Lord there is a cup, and the wine is red: it is full mixed, and he poureth out of the same.
75.10: As for the dregs thereof: all the ungodly of the earth shall drink them, and suck them out.
75.11: But I will talk of the God of Jacob: and praise him for ever.
75.12: All the horns of the ungodly also will I break: and the horns of the righteous shall be exalted.

76.1: In Jewry is God known: his Name is great in Israel.
76.2: At Salem is his tabernacle: and his dwelling in Sion.
76.3: There brake he the arrows of the bow: the shield, the sword, and the battle.
76.4: Thou art of more honour and might: than the hills of the robbers.
76.5: The proud are robbed, they have slept their sleep: and all the men whose hands were mighty have found nothing.
76.6: At thy rebuke, O God of Jacob: both the chariot and horse are fallen.
76.7: Thou, even thou art to be feared: and who may stand in thy sight when thou art angry?
76.8: Thou didst cause thy judgement to be heard from heaven: the earth trembled, and was still;
76.9: When God arose to judgement: and to help all the meek upon earth.
76.10: The fierceness of man shall turn to thy praise: and the fierceness of them shalt thou refrain.
76.11: Promise unto the Lord your God, and keep it, all ye that are round about him: bring presents unto him that ought to be feared.
76.12: He shall refrain the spirit of princes: and is wonderful among the kings of the earth.

77.1: I will cry unto God with my voice: even unto God will I cry with my voice, and he shall hearken unto me.
77.2: In the time of my trouble I sought the Lord: my sore ran and ceased not in the night-season; my soul refused comfort.
77.3: When I am in heaviness, I will think upon God: when my heart is vexed, I will complain.
77.4: Thou boldest mine eyes waking: I am so feeble, that I cannot speak.
77.5: I have considered the days of old: and the years that are past.
77.6: I call to remembrance my song: and in the night I commune with mine own heart, and search out my spirit.
77.7: Will the Lord absent himself for ever: and will he be no more intreated?
77.8: Is his mercy clean gone for ever: and is his promise come utterly to an end for evermore?
77.9: Hath God forgotten to be gracious: and will he shut up his loving-kindness in displeasure?
77.10: And I said, It is mine own infirmity: but I will remember the years of the right hand of the most Highest.
77.11: I will remember the works of the Lord: and call to mind thy wonders of old time.
77.12: I will think also of all thy works: and my talking shall be of thy doings.
77.13: Thy way, O God, is holy: who is so great a God as our God?
77.14: Thou art the God that doeth wonders: and hast declared thy power among the people.
77.15: Thou hast mightily delivered thy people: even the sons of Jacob and Joseph.
77.16: The waters saw thee, O God, the waters saw thee, and were afraid: the depths also were troubled.
77.17: The clouds poured out water, the air thundered: and thine arrows went abroad.
77.18: The voice of thy thunder was heard round about: the lightnings shone upon the ground; the earth was moved, and shook withal.
77.19: Thy way is in the sea, and thy paths in the great waters: and thy footsteps are not known.
77.20: Thou leddest thy people like sheep: by the hand of Moses and Aaron.

78.1: Hear my law, O my people: incline your ears unto the words of my mouth.
78.2: I will open my mouth in a parable: I will declare hard sentences of old;
78.3: Which we have heard and known: and such as our fathers have told us;
78.4: That we should not hide them from the children of the generations to come: but to shew the honour of the Lord, his mighty and wonderful works that he hath done.
78.5: He made a covenant with Jacob, and gave Israel a law: which he commanded our forefathers to teach their children;
78.6: That their posterity might know it: and the children which were yet unborn;
78.7: To the intent that when they came up: they might shew their children the same;
78.8: That they might put their trust in God: and not to forget the works of God, but to keep his commandments;
78.9: And not to be as their forefathers, a faithless and stubborn generation: a generation that set not their heart aright, and whose spirit cleaveth not stedfastly unto God;
78.10: Like as the children of Ephraim: who being harnessed, and carrying bows, turned themselves back in the day of battle.
78.11: They kept not the covenant of God: and would not walk in his law;
78.12: But forgat what he had done: and the wonderful works that he had shewed for them.
78.13: Marvellous things did he in the sight of our forefathers, in the land of Egypt: even in the field of Zoan.
78.14: He divided the sea, and let them go through: he made the waters to stand on an heap.
78.15: In the day-time also he led them with a cloud: and all the night through with a light of fire.
78.16: He clave the hard rocks in the wilderness: and gave them drink thereof, as it had been out of the great depth.
78.17: He brought waters out of the stony rock: so that it gushed out like the rivers.
78.18: Yet for all this they sinned more against him: and provoked the most Highest in the wilderness.
78.19: They tempted God in their hearts: and required meat for their lust.
78.20: They spake against God also, saying: Shall God prepare a table in the wilderness?
78.21: He smote the stony rock indeed, that the waters gushed out, and the streams flowed withal: but can he give bread also, or provide flesh for his people?
78.22: When the Lord heard this, he was wroth: so the fire was kindled in Jacob, and there came up heavy displeasure against Israel;
78.23: Because they believed not in God: and put not their trust in his help.
78.24: So he commanded the clouds above: and opened the doors of heaven.
78.25: He rained down manna also upon them for to eat: and gave them food from heaven.
78.26: So man did eat angels food: for he sent them meat enough.
78.27: He caused the east-wind to blow under heaven: and through his power he brought in the south-west-wind.
78.28: He rained flesh upon them as thick as dust: and feathered fowls like as the sand of the sea.
78.29: He let it fall among their tents: even round about their habitation.
78.30: So they did eat and were well filled, for he gave them their own desire: they were not disappointed of their lust.
78.31: But while the meat was yet in their mouths, the heavy wrath of God came upon them, and slew the wealthiest of them: yea, and smote down the chosen men that were in Israel.
78.32: But for all this they sinned yet more: and believed not his wondrous works.
78.33: Therefore their days did he consume in vanity: and their years in trouble.
78.34: When he slew them, they sought him: and turned them early, and inquired after God.
78.35: And they remembered that God was their strength: and that the high God was their redeemer.
78.36: Nevertheless, they did but flatter him with their mouth: and dissembled with him in their tongue.
78.37: For their heart was not whole with him: neither continued they stedfast in his covenant.
78.38: But he was so merciful, that he forgave their misdeeds: and destroyed them not.
78.39: Yea, many a time turned he his wrath away: and would not suffer his whole displeasure to arise.
78.40: For he considered that they were but flesh: and that they were even a wind that passeth away, and cometh not again.
78.41: Many a time did they provoke him in the wilderness: and grieved him in the desert.
78.42: They turned back, and tempted God: and moved the Holy One in Israel.
78.43: They thought not of his hand: and of the day when he delivered them from the hand of the enemy;
78.44: How he had wrought his miracles in Egypt: and his wonders in the field of Zoan.
78.45: He turned their waters into blood: so that they might not drink of the rivers.
78.46: He sent lice among them, and devoured them up: and frogs to destroy them.
78.47: He gave their fruit unto the caterpillar: and their labour unto the grasshopper.
78.48: He destroyed their vines with hail-stones: and their mulberry-trees with the frost.
78.49: He smote their cattle also with hail-stones: and their flocks with hot thunderbolts.
78.50: He cast upon them the furiousness of his wrath, anger, displeasure, and trouble: and sent evil angels among them.
78.51: He made a way to his indignation, and spared not their soul from death: but gave their life over to the pestilence;
78.52: And smote all the first-born in Egypt: the most principal and mightiest in the dwellings of Ham.
78.53: But as for his own people, he led them forth like sheep: and carried them in the wilderness like a flock.
78.54: He brought them out safely, that they should not fear: and overwhelmed their enemies with the sea.
78.55: And brought them within the borders of his sanctuary: even to his mountain which he purchased with his right hand.
78.56: He cast out the heathen also before them: caused their land to be divided among them for an heritage, and made the tribes of Israel to dwell in their tents.
78.57: So they tempted and displeased the most high God: and kept not his testimonies;
78.58: But turned their backs, and fell away like their forefathers: starting aside like a broken bow.
78.59: For they grieved him with their hill-altars: and provoked him to displeasure with their images.
78.60: When God heard this, he was wroth: and took sore displeasure at Israel.
78.61: So that he forsook the tabernacle in Silo: even the tent that he had pitched among men.
78.62: He delivered their power into captivity: and their beauty into the enemy's hand.
78.63: He gave his people over also unto the sword: and was wroth with his inheritance.
78.64: The fire consumed their young men: and their maidens were not given to marriage.
78.65: Their priests were slain with the sword: and there were no widows to make lamentation.
78.66: So the Lord awaked as one out of sleep: and like a giant refreshed with wine.
78.67: He smote his enemies in the hinder parts: and put them to a perpetual shame.
78.68: He refused the tabernacle of Joseph: and chose not the tribe of Ephraim;
78.69: But chose the tribe of Judah: even the hill of Sion which he loved.
78.70: And there he built his temple on high: and laid the foundation of it like the ground which he hath made continually.
78.71: He chose David also his servant: and took him away from the sheep-folds.
78.72: As he was following the ewes great with young ones he took him: that he might feed Jacob his people, and Israel his inheritance.
78.73: So he fed them with a faithful and true heart: and ruled them prudently with all his power.

79.1: O God, the heathen are come into thine inheritance: thy holy temple have they defiled, and made Jerusalem an heap of stones.
79.2: The dead bodies of thy servants have they given to be meat unto the fowls of the air: and the flesh of thy saints unto the beasts of the land.
79.3: Their blood have they shed like water on every side of Jerusalem: and there was no man to bury them.
79.4: We are become an open shame to our enemies: a very scorn and derision unto them that are round about us.
79.5: Lord, how long wilt thou be angry: shall thy jealousy burn like fire for ever?
79.6: Pour out thine indignation upon the heathen that have not known thee: and upon the kingdoms that have not called upon thy Name.
79.7: For they have devoured Jacob: and laid waste his dwelling-place.
79.8: O remember not our old sins, but have mercy upon us, and that soon: for we are come to great misery.
79.9: Help us, O God of our salvation, for the glory of thy Name: O deliver us, and be merciful unto our sins, for thy Name's sake.
79.10: Wherefore do the heathen say: Where is now their God?
79.11: O let the vengeance of thy servants blood that is shed: be openly shewed upon the heathen in our sight.
79.12: O let the sorrowful sighing of the prisoners come before thee: according to the greatness of thy power, preserve thou those that are appointed to die.
79.13: And for the blasphemy wherewith our neighbours have blasphemed thee: reward thou them, O Lord, seven-fold into their bosom.
79.14: So we, that are thy people, and sheep of thy pasture, shall give thee thanks for ever: and will alway be shewing forth thy praise from generation to generation.

80.1: Hear, O thou Shepherd of Israel, thou that leadest Joseph like a sheep: shew thyself also, thou that sittest upon the cherubims.
80.2: Before Ephraim, Benjamin, and Manasses: stir up thy strength, and come and help us.
80.3: Turn us again, O God: shew the light of thy countenance, and we shall be whole.
80.4: O Lord God of hosts: how long wilt thou be angry with thy people that prayeth?
80.5: Thou feedest them with the bread of tears: and givest them plenteousness of tears to drink.
80.6: Thou hast made us a very strife unto our neighbours: and our enemies laugh us to scorn.
80.7: Turn us again, thou God of hosts: shew the light of thy countenance, and we shall be whole.
80.8: Thou hast brought a vine out of Egypt: thou hast cast out the heathen, and planted it.
80.9: Thou madest room for it: and when it had taken root it filled the land.
80.10: The hills were covered with the shadow of it: and the boughs thereof were like the goodly cedar-trees.
80.11: She stretched out her branches unto the sea: and her boughs unto the river.
80.12: Why hast thou then broken down her hedge: that all they that go by pluck off her grapes?
80.13: The wild boar out of the wood doth root it up: and the wild beasts of the field devour it.
80.14: Turn thee again, thou God of hosts, look down from heaven: behold, and visit this vine;
80.15: And the place of the vineyard that thy right hand hath planted: and the branch that thou madest so strong for thyself.
80.16: It is burnt with fire, and cut down: and they shall perish at the rebuke of thy countenance.
80.17: Let thy hand be upon the man of thy right hand: and upon the son of man, whom thou madest so strong for thine own self.
80.18: And so will not we go back from thee: O let us live, and we shall call upon thy Name.
80.19: Turn us again, O Lord God of hosts: shew the light of thy countenance, and we shall be whole.

81.1: Sing we merrily unto God our strength: make a cheerful noise unto the God of Jacob.
81.2: Take the psalm, bring hither the tabret: the merry harp with the lute.
81.3: Blow up the trumpet in the new-moon: even in the time appointed, and upon our solemn feast-day.
81.4: For this was made a statute for Israel: and a law of the God of Jacob.
81.5: This he ordained in Joseph for a testimony: when he came out of the land of Egypt, and had heard a strange language.
81.6: I eased his shoulder from the burden: and his hands were delivered from making the pots.
81.7: Thou calledst upon me in troubles, and I delivered thee: and heard thee what time as the storm fell upon thee.
81.8: I proved thee also: at the waters of strife.
81.9: Hear, O my people, and I will assure thee, O Israel: if thou wilt hearken unto me,
81.10: There shall no strange god be in thee: neither shalt thou worship any other god.
81.11: I am the Lord thy God, who brought thee out of the land of Egypt: open thy mouth wide, and I shall fill it.
81.12: But my people would not hear my voice: and Israel would not obey me.
81.13: So I gave them up unto their own hearts' lusts: and let them follow their own imaginations.
81.14: O that my people would have hearkened unto me: for if Israel had walked in my ways,
81.15: I should soon have put down their enemies: and turned my hand against their adversaries.
81.16: The haters of the Lord should have been found liars: but their time should have endured for ever.
81.17: He should have fed them also with the finest wheat-flour: and with honey out of the stony rock should I have satisfied thee.

82.1: God standeth in the congregation of princes: he is a Judge among gods.
82.2: How long will ye give wrong judgement: and accept the persons of the ungodly?
82.3: Defend the poor and fatherless: see that such as are in need and necessity have right.
82.4: Deliver the outcast and poor: save them from the hand of the ungodly.
82.5: They will not be learned nor understand, but walk on still in darkness: all the foundations of the earth are out of course.
82.6: I have said, Ye are gods: and ye are all the children of the most Highest.
82.7: But ye shall die like men: and fall like one of the princes.
82.8: Arise, O God, and judge thou the earth: for thou shalt take all heathen to thine inheritance.

83.1: Hold not thy tongue, O God, keep not still silence: refrain not thyself, O God.
83.2: For lo, thine enemies make a murmuring: and they that hate thee have lift up their head.
83.3: They have imagined craftily against thy people's and taken counsel against thy secret ones.
83.4: They have said, Come, and let us root them out, that they be no more a people: and that the name of Israel may be no more in remembrance.
83.5: For they have cast their heads together with one consent: and are confederate against thee;
83.6: The tabernacles of the Edomites, and the Ismaelites: the Moabites and Hagarenes;
83.7: Gebal, and Ammon, and Amalek: the Philistines, with them that dwell at Tyre.
83.8: Assur also is joined with them: and have holpen the children of Lot.
83.9: But do thou to them as unto the Madianites: unto Sisera, and unto Jabin at the brook of Kison;
83.10: Who perished at Endor: and became as the dung of the earth.
83.11: Make them and their princes like Oreb and Zeb: yea, make all their princes like as Zeba and Salmana;
83.12: Who say, Let us take to ourselves: the houses of God in possession.
83.13: O my God, make them like unto a wheel: and as the stubble before the wind;
83.14: Like as the fire that burneth up the wood: and as the flame that consumeth the mountains.
83.15: Persecute them even so with thy tempest: and make them afraid with thy storm.
83.16: Make their faces ashamed, O Lord: that they may seek thy Name.
83.17: Let them be confounded and vexed ever more and more: let them be put to shame, and perish.
83.18: And they shall know that thou, whose Name is Jehovah: art only the most Highest over all the earth.

84.1: O how amiable are thy dwellings: thou Lord of hosts!
84.2: My soul hath a desire and longing to enter into the courts of the Lord: my heart and my flesh rejoice in the living God.
84.3: Yea, the sparrow hath found her an house, and the swallow a nest where she may lay her young: even thy altars, O Lord of hosts, my King and my God.
84.4: Blessed are they that dwell in thy house: they will be alway praising thee.
84.5: Blessed is the man whose strength is in thee: in whose heart are thy ways.
84.6: Who going through the vale of misery use it for a well: and the pools are filled with water.
84.7: They will go from strength to strength: and unto the God of gods appeareth every one of them in Sion.
84.8: O Lord God of hosts, hear my prayer: hearken, O God of Jacob.
84.9: Behold, O God our defender: and look upon the face of thine Anointed.
84.10: For one day in thy courts: is better than a thousand.
84.11: I had rather be a door-keeper in the house of my God: than to dwell in the tents of ungodliness.
84.12: For the Lord God is a light and defence: the Lord will give grace and worship, and no good thing shall he withhold from them that live a godly life.
84.13: O Lord God of hosts: blessed is the man that putteth his trust in thee.

85.1: Lord, thou art become gracious unto thy land: thou hast turned away the captivity of Jacob.
85.2: Thou hast forgiven the offence of thy people: and covered all their sins.
85.3: Thou hast taken away all thy displeasure: and turned thyself from thy wrathful indignation.
85.4: Turn us then, O God our Saviour: and let thine anger cease from us.
85.5: Wilt thou be displeased at us for ever: and wilt thou stretch out thy wrath from one generation to another?
85.6: Wilt thou not turn again, and quicken us: that thy people may rejoice in thee?
85.7: Shew us thy mercy, O Lord: and grant us thy salvation.
85.8: I will hearken what the Lord God will say concerning me: for he shall speak peace unto his people, and to his saints, that they turn not again.
85.9: For his salvation is nigh them that fear him: that glory may dwell in our land.
85.10: Mercy and truth are met together: righteousness and peace have kissed each other.
85.11: Truth shall flourish out of the earth: and righteousness hath looked down from heaven.
85.12: Yea, the Lord shall shew loving-kindness: and our land shall give her increase.
85.13: Righteousness shall go before him: and he shall direct his going in the way.

86.1: Bow down thine ear, O Lord, and hear me: for I am poor, and in misery.
86.2: Preserve thou my soul, for I am holy: my God, save thy servant that putteth his trust in thee.
86.3: Be merciful unto me, O Lord: for I will call daily upon thee.
86.4: Comfort the soul of thy servant: for unto thee, O Lord, do I lift up my soul.
86.5: For thou, Lord, art good and gracious: and of great mercy unto all them that call upon thee.
86.6: Give ear, Lord, unto my prayer: and ponder the voice of my humble desires.
86.7: In the time of my trouble I will call upon thee: for thou hearest me.
86.8: Among the gods there is none like unto thee, O Lord: there is not one that can do as thou doest.
86.9: All nations whom thou hast made shall come and worship thee, O Lord: and shall glorify thy Name.
86.10: For thou art great, and doest wondrous things: thou art God alone.
86.11: Teach me thy way, O Lord, and I will walk in thy truth: O knit my heart unto thee, that I may fear thy Name.
86.12: I will thank thee, O Lord my God, with all my heart: and will praise thy Name for evermore.
86.13: For great is thy mercy toward me: and thou hast delivered my soul from the nethermost hell.
86.14: O God, the proud are risen against me: and the congregations of naughty men have sought after my soul, and have not set thee before their eyes.
86.15: But thou, O Lord God, art full of compassion and mercy: long-suffering, plenteous in goodness and truth.
86.16: O turn thee then unto me, and have mercy upon me: give thy strength unto thy servant, and help the son of thine handmaid.
86.17: Shew some token upon me for good, that they who hate me may see it and be ashamed: because thou, Lord, hast holpen me and comforted me.

87.1: Her foundations are upon the holy hills: the Lord loveth the gates of Sion more than all the dwellings of Jacob.
87.2: Very excellent things are spoken of thee: thou city of God.
87.3: I will think upon Rahab and Babylon: with them that know me.
87.4: Behold ye the Philistines also: and they of Tyre, with the Morians; lo, there was he born.
87.5: And of Sion it shall be reported that he was born in her: and the most High shall stablish her.
87.6: The Lord shall rehearse it when he writeth up the people: that he was born there.
87.7: The singers also and trumpeters shall he rehearse: All my fresh springs shall be in thee.

88.1: O Lord God of my salvation, I have cried day and night before thee: O let my prayer enter into thy presence, incline thine ear unto my calling.
88.2: For my soul is full of trouble: and my life draweth nigh unto hell.
88.3: I am counted as one of them that go down into the pit: and I have been even as a man that hath no strength.
88.4: Free among the dead, like unto them that are wounded, and lie in the grave: who are out of remembrance, and are cut away from thy hand.
88.5: Thou hast laid me in the lowest pit: in a place of darkness, and in the deep.
88.6: Thine indignation lieth hard upon me: and thou hast vexed me with all thy storms.
88.7: Thou hast put away mine acquaintance far from me: and made me to be abhorred of them.
88.8: I am so fast in prison: that I cannot get forth.
88.9: My sight faileth for very trouble: Lord, I have called daily upon thee, I have stretched forth my hands unto thee.
88.10: Dost thou shew wonders among the dead: or shall the dead rise up again, and praise thee?
88.11: Shall thy loving-kindness be shewed in the grave: or thy faithfulness in destruction?
88.12: Shall thy wondrous works be known in the dark: and thy righteousness in the land where all things are forgotten?
88.13: Unto thee have I cried, O Lord: and early shall my prayer come before thee.
88.14: Lord, why abhorrest thou my soul: and hidest thou thy face from me?
88.15: I am in misery, and like unto him that is at the point to die: even from my youth up thy terrors have I suffered with a troubled mind.
88.16: Thy wrathful displeasure goeth over me: and the fear of thee hath undone me.
88.17: They came round about me daily like water: and compassed me together on every side.
88.18: My lovers and friends hast thou put away from me: and hid mine acquaintance out of my sight.

89.1: My song shall be alway of the loving-kindness of the Lord: with my mouth will I ever be shewing thy truth from one generation to another.
89.2: For I have said, Mercy shall be set up for ever: thy truth shalt thou stablish in the heavens.
89.3: I have made a covenant with my chosen: I have sworn unto David my servant;
89.4: Thy seed will I stablish for ever: and set up thy throne from one generation to another.
89.5: O Lord, the very heavens shall praise thy wondrous works: and thy truth in the congregation of the saints.
89.6: For who is he among the clouds: that shall be compared unto the Lord?
89.7: And what is he among the gods: that shall be like unto the Lord?
89.8: God is very greatly to be feared in the council of the saints: and to be had in reverence of all them that are round about him.
89.9: O Lord God of hosts, who is like unto thee: thy truth, most mighty Lord, is on every side.
89.10: Thou rulest the raging of the sea: thou stillest the waves thereof when they arise.
89.11: Thou hast subdued Egypt, and destroyed it: thou hast scattered thine enemies abroad with thy mighty arm.
89.12: The heavens are thine, the earth also is thine: thou hast laid the foundation of the round world, and all that therein is.
89.13: Thou hast made the north and the south: Tabor and Hermon shall rejoice in thy Name.
89.14: Thou hast a mighty arm: strong is thy hand, and high is thy right hand.
89.15: Righteousness and equity are the habitation of thy seat: mercy and truth shall go before thy face.
89.16: Blessed is the people, O Lord, that can rejoice in thee: they shall walk in the light of thy countenance.
89.17: Their delight shall be daily in thy Name: and in thy righteousness shall they make their boast.
89.18: For thou art the glory of their strength: and in thy loving-kindness thou shalt lift up our horns.
89.19: For the Lord is our defence: the Holy One of Israel is our King.
89.20: Thou spakest sometime in visions unto thy saints, and saidst: I have laid help upon one that is mighty; I have exalted one chosen out of the people.
89.21: I have found David my servant: with my holy oil have I anointed him.
89.22: My hand shall hold him fast: and my arm shall strengthen him.
89.23: The enemy shall not be able to do him violence: the son of wickedness shall not hurt him.
89.24: I will smite down his foes before his face: and plague them that hate him.
89.25: My truth also and my mercy shall be with him: and in my Name shall his horn be exalted.
89.26: I will set his dominion also in the sea: and his right hand in the floods.
89.27: He shall call me, Thou art my Father: my God, and my strong salvation.
89.28: And I will make him my first-born: higher than the kings of the earth.
89.29: My mercy will I keep for him for evermore: and my covenant shall stand fast with him.
89.30: His seed also will I make to endure for ever: and his throne as the days of heaven.
89.31: But if his children forsake my law: and walk not in my judgements;
89.32: If they break my statutes, and keep not my commandments: I will visit their offences with the rod, and their sin with scourges.
89.33: Nevertheless, my loving-kindness will I not utterly take from him: nor suffer my truth to fail.
89.34: My covenant will I not break, nor alter the thing that is gone out of my lips: I have sworn once by my holiness, that I will not fail David.
89.35: His seed shall endure for ever: and his seat is like as the sun before me.
89.36: He shall stand fast for evermore as the moon: and as the faithful witness in heaven.
89.37: But thou hast abhorred and forsaken thine Anointed: and art displeased at him.
89.38: Thou hast broken the covenant of thy servant: and cast his crown to the ground.
89.39: Thou hast overthrown all his hedges: and broken down his strong holds.
89.40: All they that go by spoil him: and he is become a reproach to his neighbours.
89.41: Thou hast set up the right hand of his enemies: and made all his adversaries to rejoice.
89.42: Thou hast taken away the edge of his sword: and givest him not victory in the battle.
89.43: Thou hast put out his glory: and cast his throne down to the ground.
89.44: The days of his youth hast thou shortened: and covered him with dishonour.
89.45: Lord, how long wilt thou hide thyself, for ever: and shall thy wrath burn like fire?
89.46: O remember how short my time is: where fore hast thou made all men for nought?
89.47: What man is he that liveth, and shall not see death: and shall he deliver his soul from the hand of hell?
89.48: Lord, where are thy old loving-kindnesses: which thou swarest unto David in thy truth?
89.49: Remember, Lord, the rebuke that thy servants have: and how I do bear in my bosom the rebukes of many people;
89.50: Wherewith thine enemies have blasphemed thee, and slandered the footsteps of thine Anointed: Praised be the Lord for evermore. Amen, and Amen.

90.1: Lord, thou hast been our refuge: from one generation to another.
90.2: Before the mountains were brought forth, or ever the earth and the world were made: thou art God from everlasting, and world without end.
90.3: Thou turnest man to destruction: again thou sayest, Come again, ye children of men.
90.4: For a thousand years in thy sight are but as yesterday: seeing that is past as a watch in the night.
90.5: As soon as thou scatterest them they are even as a sleep: and fade away suddenly like the grass.
90.6: In the morning it is green, and groweth up: but in the evening it is cut down, dried up, and withered.
90.7: For we consume away in thy displeasure: and are afraid at thy wrathful indignation.
90.8: Thou hast set our misdeeds before thee: and our secret sins in the light of thy countenance.
90.9: For when thou art angry all our days are gone: we bring our years to an end, as it were a tale that is told.
90.10: The days of our age are threescore years and ten; and though men be so strong that they come to fourscore years: yet is their strength then but labour and sorrow; so soon passeth it away, and we are gone.
90.11: But who regardeth the power of thy wrath: for even thereafter as a man feareth, so is thy displeasure.
90.12: So teach us to number our days: that we may apply our hearts unto wisdom.
90.13: Turn thee again, O Lord, at the last: and be gracious unto thy servants.
90.14: O satisfy us with thy mercy, and that soon: so shall we rejoice and be glad all the days of our life.
90.15: Comfort us again now after the time that thou hast plagued us: and for the years wherein we have suffered adversity.
90.16: Shew thy servants thy work: and their children thy glory.
90.17: And the glorious majesty of the Lord our God be upon us: prosper thou the work of our hands upon us, O prosper thou our handy-work.

91.1: Whoso dwelleth under the defence of the most High: shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty.
91.2: I will say unto the Lord, Thou art my hope, and my strong hold: my God, in him will I trust.
91.3: For he shall deliver thee from the snare of the hunter: and from the noisome pestilence.
91.4: He shall defend thee under his wings, and thou shalt be safe under his feathers: his faith fulness and truth shall be thy shield and buckler.
91.5: Thou shalt not be afraid for any terror by night: nor for the arrow that flieth by day;
91.6: For the pestilence that walketh in darkness: nor for the sickness that destroyeth in the noonday.
91.7: A thousand shall fall beside thee, and ten thousand at thy right hand: but it shall not come nigh thee.
91.8: Yea, with thine eyes shalt thou behold: and see the reward of the ungodly.
91.9: For thou, Lord, art my hope: thou hast set thine house of defence very high.
91.10: There shall no evil happen unto thee: neither shall any plague come nigh thy dwelling.
91.11: For he shall give his angels charge over thee: to keep thee in all thy ways.
91.12: They shall bear thee in their hands: that thou hurt not thy foot against a stone.
91.13: Thou shalt go upon the lion and adder: the young lion and the dragon shalt thou tread under thy feet.
91.14: Because he hath set his love upon me, therefore will I deliver him: I will set him up, because he hath known my Name.
91.15: He shall call upon me, and I will hear him: yea, I am with him in trouble; I will deliver him, and bring him to honour.
91.16: With long life will I satisfy him: and shew him my salvation.

92.1: It is a good thing to give thanks unto the Lord: and to sing praises unto thy Name, O most Highest;
92.2: To tell of thy loving-kindness early in the morning: and of thy truth in the night-season;
92.3: Upon an instrument of ten strings, and upon the lute: upon a loud instrument, and upon the harp.
92.4: For thou, Lord, hast made me glad through thy works: and I will rejoice in giving praise for the operations of thy hands.
92.5: O Lord, how glorious are thy works: thy thoughts are very deep.
92.6: An unwise man doth not well consider this: and a fool doth not understand it.
92.7: When the ungodly are green as the grass, and when all the workers of wickedness do flourish: then shall they be destroyed for ever; but thou, Lord, art the most Highest for evermore.
92.8: For lo, thine enemies, O Lord, lo, thine enemies shall perish: and all the workers of wickedness shall be destroyed.
92.9: But my horn shall be exalted like the horn of an unicorn: for I am anointed with fresh oil.
92.10: Mine eye also shall see his lust of mine enemies: and mine ear shall hear his desire of the wicked that arise up against me.
92.11: The righteous shall flourish like a palm-tree: and shall spread abroad like a cedar in Libanus.
92.12: Such as are planted in the house of the Lord: shall flourish in the courts of the house of our God.
92.13: They also shall bring forth more fruit in their age: and shall be fat and well-liking.
92.14: That they may shew how true the Lord my strength is: and that there is no unrighteousness in him.

93.1: The Lord is King, and hath put on glorious apparel: the Lord hath put on his apparel, and girded himself with strength.
93.2: He hath made the round world so sure: that it cannot be moved.
93.3: Ever since the world began hath thy seat been prepared: thou art from everlasting.
93.4: The floods are risen, O Lord, the floods have lift up their voice: the floods lift up their waves.
93.5: The waves of the sea are mighty, and rage horribly: but yet the Lord, who dwelleth on high, is mightier.
93.6: Thy testimonies, O Lord, are very sure: holiness becometh thine house for ever.

94.1: O Lord God, to whom vengeance belongeth, thou God, to whom vengeance belongeth, shew thyself.
94.2: Arise, thou Judge of the world: and reward the proud after their deserving.
94.3: Lord, how long shall the ungodly: how long shall the ungodly triumph?
