94.4: How long shall all wicked doers speak so disdainfully: and make such proud boasting?
94.5: They smite down thy people, O Lord: and trouble thine heritage.
94.6: They murder the widow and the stranger: and put the fatherless to death.
94.7: And yet they say, Tush, the Lord shall not see: neither shall the God of Jacob regard it.
94.8: Take heed, ye unwise among the people: O ye fools, when will ye understand?
94.9: He that planted the ear, shall he not hear: or he that made the eye, shall he not see?
94.10: Or he that nurtureth the heathen: it is he that teacheth man knowledge, shall not he punish?
94.11: The Lord knoweth the thoughts of man: that they are but vain.
94.12: Blessed is the man whom thou chastenest, O Lord: and teachest him in thy law;
94.13: That thou mayest give him patience in time of adversity: until the pit be digged up for the ungodly.
94.14: For the Lord will not fail his people: neither will he forsake his inheritance;
94.15: Until righteousness turn again unto judgement: all such as are true in heart shall follow it.
94.16: Who will rise up with me against the wicked: or who will take my part against the evil-doers?
94.17: If the Lord had not helped me: it had not failed but my soul had been put to silence.
94.18: But when I said, My foot hath slipt: thy mercy, O Lord, held me up.
94.19: In the multitude of the sorrows that I had in my heart: thy comforts have refreshed my soul.
94.20: Wilt thou have any thing to do with the stool of wickedness: which imagineth mischief as a law?
94.21: They gather them together against the soul of the righteous: and condemn the innocent blood.
94.22: But the Lord is my refuge: and my God is the strength of my confidence.
94.23: He shall recompense them their wickedness, and destroy them in their own malice: yea, the Lord our God shall destroy them.

95.1: O come, let us sing unto the Lord: let us heartily rejoice in the strength of our salvation.
95.2: Let us come before his presence with thanksgiving: and shew ourselves glad in him with psalms.
95.3: For the Lord is a great God: and a great King above all gods.
95.4: In his hand are all the corners of the earth: and the strength of the hills is his also.
95.5: The sea is his, and he made it: and his hands prepared the dry land.
95.6: O come, let us worship and fall down: and kneel before the Lord our Maker.
95.7: For he is the Lord our God: and we are the people of his pasture, and the sheep of his hand.
95.8: To-day if ye will hear his voice, harden not your hearts: as in the provocation, and as in the day of temptation in the wilderness;
95.9: When your fathers tempted me: proved me, and saw my works.
95.10: Forty years long was I grieved with this generation, and said: It is a people that do err in their hearts, for they have not known my ways;
95.11: Unto whom I sware in my wrath: that they should not enter into my rest.

96.1: O sing unto the Lord a new song: sing unto the Lord, all the whole earth.
96.2: Sing unto the Lord, and praise his Name: be telling of his salvation from day to day.
96.3: Declare his honour unto the heathen: and his wonders unto all people.
96.4: For the Lord is great, and cannot worthily be praised: he is more to be feared than all gods.
96.5: As for all the gods of the heathen, they are but idols: but it is the Lord that made the heavens.
96.6: Glory and worship are before him: power and honour are in his sanctuary.
96.7: Ascribe unto the Lord, O ye kindreds of the people: ascribe unto the Lord worship and power.
96.8: Ascribe unto the Lord the honour due unto his Name: bring presents, and come into his courts.
96.9: O worship the Lord in the beauty of holiness: let the whole earth stand in awe of him.
96.10: Tell it out among the heathen that the Lord is King: and that it is he who hath made the round world so fast that it cannot be moved; and how that he shall judge the people righteously.
96.11: Let the heavens rejoice, and let the earth be glad: let the sea make a noise, and all that therein is.
96.12: Let the field be joyful, and all that is in it: then shall all the trees of the wood rejoice before the Lord.
96.13: For he cometh, for he cometh to judge the earth: and with righteousness to judge the world, and the people with his truth.

97.1: The Lord is King, the earth may be glad thereof: yea, the multitude of the isles may be glad thereof.
97.2: Clouds and darkness are round about him: righteousness and judgement are the habitation of his seat.
97.3: There shall go a fire before him: and burn up his enemies on every side.
97.4: His lightnings gave shine unto the world: the earth saw it, and was afraid.
97.5: The hills melted like wax at the presence of the Lord: at the presence of the Lord of the whole earth.
97.6: The heavens have declared his righteousness: and all the people have seen his glory.
97.7: Confounded be all they that worship carved images, and that delight in vain gods: worship him, all ye gods.
97.8: Sion heard of it, and rejoiced: and the daughters of Judah were glad, because of thy judgements, O Lord.
97.9: For thou, Lord, art higher than all that are in the earth: thou art exalted far above all gods.
97.10: O ye that love the Lord, see that ye hate the thing which is evil: the Lord preserveth the souls of his saints; he shall deliver them from the hand of the ungodly.
97.11: There is sprung up a light for the righteous: and joyful gladness for such as are true-hearted.
97.12: Rejoice in the Lord, ye righteous: and give thanks for a remembrance of his holiness.

98.1: O sing unto the Lord a new song: for he hath done marvellous things.
98.2: With his own right hand, and with his holy arm: hath he gotten himself the victory.
98.3: The Lord declared his salvation: his righteousness hath he openly shewed in the sight of the heathen.
98.4: He hath remembered his mercy and truth toward the house of Israel: and all the ends of the world have seen the salvation of our God.
98.5: Shew yourselves joyful unto the Lord, all ye lands: sing, rejoice, and give thanks.
98.6: Praise the Lord upon the harp: sing to the harp with a psalm of thanksgiving.
98.7: With trumpets also and shawms: O shew yourselves joyful before the Lord the King.
98.8: Let the sea make a noise, and all that therein is: the round world, and they that dwell therein.
98.9: Let the floods clap their hands, and let the hills be joyful together before the Lord: for he is come to judge the earth.
98.10: With righteousness shall he judge the world: and the people with equity.

99.1: The Lord is King, be the people never so unpatient: he sitteth between the cherubims, be the earth never so unquiet.
99.2: The Lord is great in Sion: and high above all people.
99.3: They shall give thanks unto thy Name: which is great, wonderful, and holy.
99.4: The King's power loveth judgement; thou hast prepared equity: thou hast executed judgement and righteousness in Jacob.
99.5: O magnify the Lord our God: and fall down before his footstool, for he is holy.
99.6: Moses and Aaron among his priests, and Samuel among such as call upon his Name: these called upon the Lord, and he heard them.
99.7: He spake unto them out of the cloudy pillar: for they kept his testimonies, and the law that he gave them.
99.8: Thou heardest them, O Lord our God: thou forgavest them, O God, and punishedst their own inventions.
99.9: O magnify the Lord our God, and worship him upon his holy hill: for the Lord our God is holy.

100.1: O be joyful in the Lord, all ye lands: serve the Lord with gladness, and come before his presence with a song.
100.2: Be ye sure that the Lord he is God: it is he that hath made us, and not we ourselves; we are his people, and the sheep of his pasture.
100.3: O go your way into his gates with thanksgiving, and into his courts with praise: be thankful unto him, and speak good of his Name.
100.4: For the Lord is gracious, his mercy is ever lasting: and his truth endureth from generation to generation.

101.1: My song shall be of mercy and judgement: unto thee, O Lord, will I sing.
101.2: O let me have understanding: in the way of godliness.
101.3: When wilt thou come unto me: I will walk in my house with a perfect heart.
101.4: I will take no wicked thing in hand; I hate the sins of unfaithfulness: there shall no such cleave unto me.
101.5: A froward heart shall depart from me: I will not know a wicked person.
101.6: Whoso privily slandereth his neighbour: him will I destroy.
101.7: Whoso hath also a proud look and high stomach: I will not suffer him.
101.8: Mine eyes look upon such as are faithful in the land: that they may dwell with me.
101.9: Whoso leadeth a godly life: he shall be my servant.
101.10: There shall no deceitful person dwell in my house: he that telleth lies shall not tarry in my sight.
101.11: I shall soon destroy all the ungodly that are in the land: that I may root out all wicked doers from the city of the Lord.

102.1: Hear my prayer, O Lord: and let my crying come unto thee.
102.2: Hide not thy face from me in the time of my trouble: incline thine ear unto me when I call; O hear me, and that right soon.
102.3: For my days are consumed away like smoke: and my bones are burnt up as it were a fire-brand.
102.4: My heart is smitten down, and withered like grass: so that I forget to eat my bread.
102.5: For the voice of my groaning: my bones will scarce cleave to my flesh.
102.6: I am become like a pelican in the wilderness: and like an owl that is in the desert.
102.7: I have watched, and am even as it were a sparrow: that sitteth alone upon the house-top.
102.8: Mine enemies revile me all the day long: and they that are mad upon me are sworn together against me.
102.9: For I have eaten ashes as it were bread: and mingled my drink with weeping;
102.10: And that because of thine indignation and wrath: for thou hast taken me up, and cast me down.
102.11: My days are gone like a shadow: and I am withered like grass.
102.12: But thou, O Lord, shalt endure for ever: and thy remembrance throughout all generations.
102.13: Thou shalt arise, and have mercy upon Sion: for it is time that thou have mercy upon her, yea, the time is come.
102.14: And why? thy servants think upon her stones: and it pitieth them to see her in the dust.
102.15: The heathen shall fear thy Name, O Lord: and all the kings of the earth thy majesty;
102.16: When the Lord shall build up Sion: and when his glory shall appear;
102.17: When he turneth him unto the prayer of the poor destitute: and despiseth not their desire.
102.18: This shall be written for those that come after: and the people which shall be born shall praise the Lord.
102.19: For he hath looked down from his sanctuary: out of the heaven did the Lord behold the earth;
102.20: That he might hear the mournings of such as are in captivity: and deliver the children appointed unto death;
102.21: That they may declare the Name of the Lord in Sion: and his worship at Jerusalem;
102.22: When the people are gathered together: and the kingdoms also, to serve the Lord.
102.23: He brought down my strength in my journey: and shortened my days.
102.24: But I said, O my God, take me not away in the midst of mine age: as for thy years, they endure throughout all generations.
102.25: Thou, Lord, in the beginning hast laid the foundation of the earth: and the heavens are the work of thy hands.
102.26: They shall perish, but thou shalt endure: they all shall wax old as doth a garment;
102.27: And as a vesture shalt thou change them, and they shall be changed: but thou art the same, and thy years shall not fail.
102.28: The children of thy servants shall continue: and their seed shall stand fast in thy sight.

103.1: Praise the Lord, O my soul: and all that is within me praise his holy Name.
103.2: Praise the Lord, O my soul: and forget not all his benefits;
103.3: Who forgiveth all thy sin: and healeth all thine infirmities;
103.4: Who saveth thy life from destruction: and crowneth thee with mercy and loving-kindness;
103.5: Who satisfieth thy mouth with good things: making thee young and lusty as an eagle.
103.6: The Lord executeth righteousness and judgement: for all them that are oppressed with wrong.
103.7: He shewed his ways unto Moses: his works unto the children of Israel.
103.8: The Lord is full of compassion and mercy: long-suffering, and of great goodness.
103.9: He will not alway be chiding: neither keepeth he his anger for ever.
103.10: He hath not dealt with us after our sins: nor rewarded us according to our wickednesses.
103.11: For look how high the heaven is in comparison of the earth: so great is his mercy also toward them that fear him.
103.12: Look how wide also the east is from the west: so far hath he set our sins from us.
103.13: Yea, like as a father pitieth his own children: even so is the Lord merciful unto them that fear him.
103.14: For he knoweth whereof we are made: he remembereth that we are but dust.
103.15: The days of man are but as grass: for he flourisheth as a flower of the field.
103.16: For as soon as the wind goeth over it, it is gone: and the place thereof shall know it no more.
103.17: But the merciful goodness of the Lord endureth for ever and ever upon them that fear him: and his righteousness upon children's children;
103.18: Even upon such as keep his covenant: and think upon his commandments to do them.
103.19: The Lord hath prepared his seat in heaven: and his kingdom ruleth over all.
103.20: O praise the Lord, ye angels of his, ye that excel in strength: ye that fulfil his commandment, and hearken unto the voice of his words.
103.21: O praise the Lord, all ye his hosts: ye servants of his that do his pleasure.
103.22: O speak good of the Lord, all ye works of his, in all places of his dominion: praise thou the Lord, O my soul.

104.1: Praise the Lord, O my soul: O Lord my God, thou art become exceeding glorious; thou art clothed with majesty and honour.
104.2: Thou deckest thyself with light as it were with a garment: and spreadest out the heavens like a curtain.
104.3: Who layeth the beams of his chambers in the waters: and maketh the clouds his chariot, and walketh upon the wings of the wind.
104.4: He maketh his angels spirits: and his ministers a flaming fire.
104.5: He laid the foundations of the earth: that it never should move at any time.
104.6: Thou coveredst it with the deep like as with a garment: the waters stand in the hills.
104.7: At thy rebuke they flee: at the voice of thy thunder they are afraid.
104.8: They go up as high as the hills, and down to the valleys beneath: even unto the place which thou hast appointed for them.
104.9: Thou hast set them their bounds which they shall not pass: neither turn again to cover the earth.
104.10: He sendeth the springs into the rivers: which run among the hills.
104.11: All beasts of the field drink thereof: and the wild asses quench their thirst.
104.12: Beside them shall the fowls of the air have their habitation: and sing among the branches.
104.13: He watereth the hills from above: the earth is filled with the fruit of thy works.
104.14: He bringeth forth grass for the cattle: and green herb for the service of men;
104.15: That he may bring food out of the earth, and wine that maketh glad the heart of man: and oil to make him a cheerful countenance, and bread to strengthen man's heart.
104.16: The trees of the Lord also are full of sap: even the cedars of Libanus which he hath planted;
104.17: Wherein the birds make their nests: and the fir-trees are a dwelling for the stork.
104.18: The high hills are a refuge for the wild goats: and so are the stony rocks for the conies.
104.19: He appointed the moon for certain seasons: and the sun knoweth his going down.
104.20: Thou makest darkness that it maybe night: wherein all the beasts of the forest do move.
104.21: The lions roaring after their prey: do seek their meat from God.
104.22: The sun ariseth, and they get them away together: and lay them down in their dens.
104.23: Man goeth forth to his work, and to his labour: until the evening.
104.24: O Lord, how manifold are thy works: in wisdom hast thou made them all; the earth is full of thy riches.
104.25: So is the great and wide sea also: wherein are things creeping innumerable, both small and great beasts.
104.26: There go the ships, and there is that leviathan: whom thou hast made to take his pastime therein.
104.27: These wait all upon thee: that thou mayest give them meat in due season.
104.28: When thou givest it them they gather it: and when thou openest thy hand they are filled with good.
104.29: When thou hidest thy face they are troubled: when thou takest away their breath they die, and are turned again to their dust.
104.30: When thou lettest thy breath go forth they shall be made: and thou shalt renew the face of the earth.
104.31: The glorious majesty of the Lord shall endure for ever: the Lord shall rejoice in his works.
104.32: The earth shall tremble at the look of him: if he do but touch the hills, they shall smoke.
104.33: I will sing unto the Lord as long as I live: I will praise my God while I have my being.
104.34: And so shall my words please him: my joy shall be in the Lord.
104.35: As for sinners, they shall be consumed out of the earth, and the ungodly shall come to an end: praise thou the Lord, O my soul, praise the Lord.

105.1: O give thanks unto the Lord, and call upon his Name: tell the people what things he hath done.
105.2: O let your songs be of him, and praise him: and let your talking be of all his wondrous works.
105.3: Rejoice in his holy Name: let the heart of them rejoice that seek the Lord.
105.4: Seek the Lord and his strength: seek his face evermore.
105.5: Remember the marvellous works that he hath done: his wonders, and the judgements of his mouth,
105.6: O ye seed of Abraham his servant: ye children of Jacob his chosen.
105.7: He is the Lord our God: his judgements are in all the world.
105.8: He hath been alway mindful of his covenant and promise: that he made to a thousand generations;
105.9: Even the covenant that he made with Abraham: and the oath that he sware unto Isaac;
105.10: And appointed the same unto Jacob for a law: and to Israel for an everlasting testament;
105.11: Saying, Unto thee will I give the land of Canaan: the lot of your inheritance;
105.12: When there were yet but a few of them: and they strangers in the land;
105.13: What time as they went from one nation to another: from one kingdom to another people;
105.14: He suffered no man to do them wrong: but reproved even kings for their sakes;
105.15: Touch not mine Anointed: and do my prophets no harm.
105.16: Moreover, he called for a dearth upon the land: and destroyed all the provision of bread.
105.17: But he had sent a man before them: even Joseph, who was sold to be a bond-servant;
105.18: Whose feet they hurt in the stocks: the iron entered into his soul;
105.19: Until the time came that his cause was known: the word of the Lord tried him.
105.20: The king sent, and delivered him: the prince of the people let him go free.
105.21: He made him lord also of his house: and ruler of all his substance;
105.22: That he might inform his princes after his will: and teach his senators wisdom.
105.23: Israel also came into Egypt: and Jacob was a stranger in the land of Ham.
105.24: And he increased his people exceedingly: and made them stronger than their enemies;
105.25: Whose heart turned, so that they hated his people: and dealt untruly with his servants.
105.26: Then sent he Moses his servant: and Aaron whom he had chosen.
105.27: And these shewed his tokens among them: and wonders in the land of Ham.
105.28: He sent darkness, and it was dark: and they were not obedient unto his word.
105.29: He turned their waters into blood: and slew their fish.
105.30: Their land brought forth frogs: yea, even in their kings chambers.
105.31: He spake the word, and there came all manner of flies: and lice in all their quarters.
105.32: He gave them hail-stones for rain: and flames of fire in their land.
105.33: He smote their vines also and fig-trees: and destroyed the trees that were in their coasts.
105.34: He spake the word, and the grasshoppers came, and caterpillars innumerable: and did eat up all the grass in their land, and devoured the fruit of their ground.
105.35: He smote all the first-born in their land: even the chief of all their strength.
105.36: He brought them forth also with silver and gold: there was not one feeble person among their tribes.
105.37: Egypt was glad at their departing: for they were afraid of them.
105.38: He spread out a cloud to be a covering: and fire to give light in the night-season.
105.39: At their desire he brought quails: and he filled them with the bread of heaven.
105.40: He opened the rock of stone, and the waters flowed out: so that rivers ran in the dry places.
105.41: For why? he remembered his holy promise: and Abraham his servant.
105.42: And he brought forth his people with joy: and his chosen with gladness;
105.43: And gave them the lands of the heathen: and they took the labours of the people in possession;
105.44: That they might keep his statutes: and observe his laws.

106.1: O give thanks unto the Lord, for he is gracious: and his mercy endureth for ever.
106.2: Who can express the noble acts of the Lord: or shew forth all his praise?
106.3: Blessed are they that alway keep judgement: and do righteousness.
106.4: Remember me, O Lord, according to the favour that thou bearest unto thy people: O visit me with thy salvation;
106.5: That I may see the felicity of thy chosen: and rejoice in the gladness of thy people, and give thanks with thine inheritance.
106.6: We have sinned with our fathers: we have done amiss, and dealt wickedly.
106.7: Our fathers regarded not thy wonders in Egypt, neither kept they thy great goodness in remembrance: but were disobedient at the sea, even at the Red sea.
106.8: Nevertheless, he helped them for his Name's sake: that he might make his power to be known.
106.9: He rebuked the Red sea also, and it was dried up: so he led them through the deep, as through a wilderness.
106.10: And he saved them from the adversaries' hand: and delivered them from the hand of the enemy.
106.11: As for those that troubled them, the waters overwhelmed them: there was not one of them left.
106.12: Then believed they his words: and sang praise unto him.
106.13: But within a while they forgat his works: and would not abide his counsel.
106.14: But lust came upon them in the wilderness: and they tempted God in the desert.
106.15: And he gave them their desire: and sent leanness withal into their soul.
106.16: They angered Moses also in the tents: and Aaron the saint of the Lord.
106.17: So the earth opened, and swallowed up Dathan: and covered the congregation of Abiram.
106.18: And the fire was kindled in their company: the flame burnt up the ungodly.
106.19: They made a calf in Horeb: and worshipped the molten image.
106.20: Thus they turned their glory: into the similitude of a calf that eateth hay.
106.21: And they forgat God their Saviour: who had done so great things in Egypt;
106.22: Wondrous works in the land of Ham: and fearful things by the Red sea.
106.23: So he said, he would have destroyed them, had not Moses his chosen stood before him in the gap: to turn away his wrathful indignation, lest he should destroy them.
106.24: Yea, they thought scorn of that pleasant land: and gave no credence unto his word;
106.25: But murmured in their tents: and hearkened not unto the voice of the Lord.
106.26: Then lift he up his hand against them: to overthrow them in the wilderness;
106.27: To cast out their seed among the nations: and to scatter them in the lands.
106.28: They joined themselves unto Baal-peor: and ate the offerings of the dead.
106.29: Thus they provoked him to anger with their own inventions: and the plague was great among them.
106.30: Then stood up Phinees and prayed: and so the plague ceased.
106.31: And that was counted unto him for righteousness: among all posterities for evermore.
106.32: They angered mm also at the waters of strife: so that he punished Moses for their sakes;
106.33: Because they provoked his spirit: so that he spake unadvisedly with his lips.
106.34: Neither destroyed they the heathen: as the Lord commanded them;
106.35: But were mingled among the heathen: and learned their works.
106.36: Insomuch that they worshipped their idols, which turned to their own decay: yea, they offered their sons and their daughters unto devils;
106.37: And shed innocent blood, even the blood of their sons and of their daughters: whom they offered unto the idols of Canaan; and the land was defiled with blood.
106.38: Thus were they stained with their own works: and went a whoring with their own inventions.
106.39: Therefore was the wrath of the Lord kindled against his people: insomuch that he abhorred his own inheritance.
106.40: And he gave them over into the hands of the heathen: and they that hated them were lords over them.
106.41: Their enemies oppressed them: and had them in subjection.
106.42: Many a time did he deliver them: but they rebelled against him with their own inventions, and were brought down in their wickedness.
106.43: Nevertheless, when he saw their adversity: he heard their complaint.
106.44: He thought upon his covenant, and pitied them according unto the multitude of his mercies: yea, he made all those that led them away captive to pity them.
106.45: Deliver us, O Lord our God, and gather us from among the heathen: that we may give thanks unto thy holy Name, and make our boast of thy praise.
106.46: Blessed be the Lord God of Israel from everlasting and world without end: And let all the people say, Amen.

107.1: O give thanks unto the Lord, for he is gracious: and his mercy endureth for ever.
107.2: Let them give thanks whom the Lord hath redeemed: and delivered from the hand of the enemy;
107.3: And gathered them out of the lands, from the east and from the west: from the north and from the south.
107.4: They went astray in the wilderness out of the way: and found no city to dwell in;
107.5: Hungry and thirsty: their soul fainted in them.
107.6: So they cried unto the Lord in their trouble: and he delivered them from their distress.
107.7: He led them forth by the right way: that they might go to the city where they dwelt.
107.8: O that men would therefore praise the Lord for his goodness: and declare the wonders that he doeth for the children of men!
107.9: For he satisfieth the empty soul: and filleth the hungry soul with goodness.
107.10: Such as sit in darkness, and in the shadow of death: being fast bound in misery and iron;
107.11: Because they rebelled against the words of the Lord: and lightly regarded the counsel of the most Highest;
107.12: He also brought down their heart through heaviness: they fell down, and there was none to help them.
107.13: So when they cried unto the Lord in their trouble: he delivered them out of their distress.
107.14: For he brought them out of darkness, and out of the shadow of death: and brake their bonds in sunder.
107.15: O that men would therefore praise the Lord for his goodness: and declare the wonders that he doeth for the children of men.
107.16: For he hath broken the gates of brass: and smitten the bars of iron in sunder.
107.17: Foolish men are plagued for their offence: and because of their wickedness.
107.18: Their soul abhorred all manner of meat: and they were even hard at death's door.
107.19: So when they cried unto the Lord in their trouble: he delivered them out of their distress.
107.20: He sent his word, and healed them: and they were saved from their destruction.
107.21: O that men would therefore praise the Lord for his goodness: and declare the wonders that he doeth for the children of men!
107.22: That they would offer unto him the sacrifice of thanksgiving: and tell out his works with gladness!
107.23: They that go down to the sea in ships: and occupy their business in great waters;
107.24: These men see the works of the Lord: and his wonders in the deep.
107.25: For at his word the stormy wind ariseth: which lifteth up the waves thereof.
107.26: They are carried up to the heaven, and down again to the deep: their soul melteth away because of the trouble.
107.27: They reel to and fro, and stagger like a drunken man: and are at their wit's end.
107.28: So when they cry unto the Lord in their trouble: he delivereth them out of their distress.
107.29: For he maketh the storm to cease: so that the waves thereof are still.
107.30: Then are they glad, because they are at rest: and so he bringeth them unto the haven where they would be.
107.31: O that men would therefore praise the Lord for his goodness: and declare the wonders that he doeth for the children of men!
107.32: That they would exalt him also in the congregation of the people: and praise him in the seat of the elders!
107.33: Who turneth the floods into a wilderness: and drieth up the water-springs.
107.34: A fruitful land maketh he barren: for the wickedness of them that dwell therein.
107.35: Again, he maketh the wilderness a standing water: and water-springs of a dry ground.
107.36: And there he setteth the hungry: that they may build them a city to dwell in;
107.37: That they may sow their land, and plant vineyards: to yield them fruits of increase.
107.38: He blesseth them, so that they multiply exceedingly: and suffereth not their cattle to decrease.
107.39: And again, when they are minished and brought low: through oppression, through any plague or trouble;
107.40: Though he suffer them to be evil intreated through tyrants: and let them wander out of the way in the wilderness;
107.41: Yet helpeth he the poor out of misery: and maketh him households like a flock of sheep.
107.42: The righteous will consider this, and rejoice: and the mouth of all wickedness shall be stopped.
107.43: Whoso is wise will ponder these things: and they shall understand the loving-kindness of the Lord.

108.1: O God, my heart is ready, my heart is ready: I will sing and give praise with the best member that I have.
108.2: Awake, thou lute, and harp: I myself will awake right early.
108.3: I will give thanks unto thee, O Lord, among the people: I will sing praises unto thee among the nations.
108.4: For thy mercy is greater than the heavens: and thy truth reacheth unto the clouds.
108.5: Set up thyself, O God, above the heavens: and thy glory above all the earth.
108.6: That thy beloved may be delivered: let thy right hand save them, and hear thou me.
108.7: God hath spoken in his holiness: I will rejoice therefore, and divide Sichem, and mete out the valley of Succoth.
108.8: Gilead is mine, and Manasses is mine: Ephraim also is the strength of my head.
108.9: Judah is my law-giver, Moab is my wash-pot: over Edom will I cast out my shoe, upon Philistia will I triumph.
108.10: Who will lead me into the strong city: and who will bring me into Edom?
108.11: Hast not thou forsaken us, O God: and wilt not thou, O God, go forth with our hosts?
108.12: O help us against the enemy: for vain is the help of man.
108.13: Through God we shall do great acts: and it is he that shall tread down our enemies.

109.1: Hold not thy tongue, O God of my praise: for the mouth of the ungodly, yea, the mouth of the deceitful is opened upon me.
109.2: And they have spoken against me with false tongues: they compassed me about also with words of hatred, and fought against me without a cause.
109.3: For the love that I had unto them, lo, they take now my contrary part: but I give myself unto prayer.
109.4: Thus have they rewarded me evil for good: and hatred for my good will.
109.5: Set thou an ungodly man to be ruler over him: and let Satan stand at his right hand.
109.6: When sentence is given upon him, let him be condemned: and let his prayer be turned into sin.
109.7: Let his days be few: and let another take his office.
109.8: Let his children be fatherless: and his wife a widow.
109.9: Let his children be vagabonds, and beg their bread: let them seek it also out of desolate places.
109.10: Let the extortioner consume all that he hath: and let the stranger spoil his labour.
109.11: Let there be no man to pity him: nor to have compassion upon his fatherless children.
109.12: Let his posterity be destroyed: and in the next generation let his name be clean put out.
109.13: Let the wickedness of his fathers be had in remembrance in the sight of the Lord: and let not the sin of his mother be done away.
109.14: Let them alway be before the Lord: that he may root out the memorial of them from off the earth.
109.15: And that, because his mind was not to do good: but persecuted the poor helpless man, that he might slay him that was vexed at the heart.
109.16: His delight was in cursing, and it shall happen unto him: he loved not blessing, therefore shall it be far from him,
109.17: He clothed himself with cursing, like as with a raiment: and it shall come into his bowels like water, and like oil into his bones.
109.18: Let it be unto him as the cloke that he hath upon him: and as the girdle that he is alway girded withal.
109.19: Let it thus happen from the Lord unto mine enemies: and to those that speak evil against my soul.
109.20: But deal thou with me, O Lord God, according unto thy Name: for sweet is thy mercy.
109.21: O deliver me, for I am helpless and poor: and my heart is wounded within me.
109.22: I go hence like the shadow that departeth: and am driven away as the grasshopper.
109.23: My knees are weak through fasting: my flesh is dried up for want of fatness.
109.24: I became also a reproach unto them: they that looked upon me shaked their heads.
109.25: Help me, O Lord my God: O save me according to thy mercy.
109.26: And they shall know, how that this is thy hand: and that thou, Lord, hast done it.
109.27: Though they curse, yet bless thou: and let them be confounded that rise up against me; but let thy servant rejoice.
109.28: Let mine adversaries be clothed with shame: and let them cover themselves with their own confusion, as with a cloke.
109.29: As for me, I will give great thanks unto the Lord with my mouth: and praise him among the multitude.
109.30: For he shall stand at the right hand of the poor: to save his soul from unrighteous judges.

110.1: The Lord said unto my Lord: Sit thou on my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool.
110.2: The Lord shall send the rod of thy power out of Sion: be thou ruler, even in the midst among thine enemies.
110.3: In the day of thy power shall thy people offer thee free-will offerings with an holy worship: the dew of thy birth is of the womb of the morning.
110.4: The Lord sware, and will not repent: Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedech.
110.5: The Lord upon thy right hand: shall wound even kings in the day of his wrath.
110.6: He shall judge among the heathen; he shall fill the places with the dead bodies: and smite in sunder the heads over divers countries.
110.7: He shall drink of the brook in the way: therefore shall he lift up his head.

111.1: I will give thanks unto the Lord with my whole heart: secretly among the faithful, and in the congregation.
111.2: The works of the Lord are great: sought out of all them that have pleasure therein.
111.3: His work is worthy to be praised and had in honour: and his righteousness endureth for ever.
111.4: The merciful and gracious Lord hath so done his marvellous works: that they ought to be had in remembrance.
111.5: He hath given meat unto them that fear him: he shall ever be mindful of his covenant.
111.6: He hath shewed his people the power of his works: that he may give them the heritage of the heathen.
111.7: The works of his hands are verity and judgement: all his commandments are true.
111.8: They stand fast for ever and ever: and are done in truth and equity.
111.9: He sent redemption unto his people: he hath commanded his covenant for ever; holy and reverend is his Name.
111.10: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom: a good understanding have all they that do thereafter; the praise of it endureth for ever.

112.1: Blessed is the man that feareth the Lord: he hath great delight in his commandments.
112.2: His seed shall be mighty upon earth: the generation of the faithful shall be blessed.
112.3: Riches and plenteousness shall be in his house: and his righteousness endureth for ever.
112.4: Unto the godly there ariseth up light in the darkness: he is merciful, loving, and righteous.
112.5: A good man is merciful, and lendeth: and will guide his words with discretion.
112.6: For he shall never be moved: and the righteous shall be had in everlasting remembrance.
112.7: He will not be afraid of any evil tidings: for his heart standeth fast, and believeth in the Lord.
112.8: His heart is established, and will not shrink: until he see his desire upon his enemies.
112.9: He hath dispersed abroad, and given to the poor: and his righteousness remaineth for ever; his horn shall be exalted with honour.
112.10: The ungodly shall see it, and it shall grieve him: he shall gnash with his teeth, and consume away; the desire of the ungodly shall perish.

113.1: Praise the Lord, ye servants: O praise the Name of the Lord.
113.2: Blessed be the Name of the Lord: from this time forth for evermore.
113.3: The Lord's Name is praised: from the rising up of the sun unto the going down of the same.
113.4: The Lord is high above all heathen: and his glory above the heavens.
113.5: Who is like unto the Lord our God, that hath his dwelling so high: and yet humbleth himself to behold the things that are in heaven and earth?
113.6: He taketh up the simple out of the dust: and lifteth the poor out of the mire;
113.7: That he may set him with the princes: even with the princes of his people.
113.8: He maketh the barren woman to keep house: and to be a joyful mother of children.

114.1: When Israel came out of Egypt: and the house of Jacob from among the strange people,
114.2: Judah was his sanctuary: and Israel his dominion.
114.3: The sea saw that, and fled: Jordan was driven back.
114.4: The mountains skipped like rams: and the little hills like young sheep.
114.5: What aileth thee, O thou sea, that thou fleddest: and thou Jordan, that thou wast driven back?
114.6: Ye mountains, that ye skipped like rams: and ye little hills, like young sheep?
114.7: Tremble, thou earth, at the presence of the Lord: at the presence of the God of Jacob;
114.8: Who turned the hard rock into a standing water: and the flint-stone into a springing well.

115.1: Not unto us, O Lord, not unto us, but unto thy Name give the praise: for thy loving mercy and for thy truth's sake.
115.2: Wherefore shall the heathen say: Where is now their God?
115.3: As for our God, he is in heaven: he hath done whatsoever pleased him.
115.4: Their idols are silver and gold: even the work of men's hands.
115.5: They have mouths, and speak not: eyes have they, and see not.
115.6: They have ears, and hear not: noses have they, and smell not.
115.7: They have hands, and handle not; feet have they, and walk not: neither speak they through their throat.
115.8: They that make them are like unto them: and so are all such as put their trust in them.
115.9: But thou, house of Israel, trust thou in the Lord: he is their succour and defence.
115.10: Ye house of Aaron, put your trust in the Lord: he is their helper and defender.
115.11: Ye that fear the Lord, put your trust in the Lord: he is their helper and defender.
115.12: The Lord hath been mindful of us, and he shall bless us: even he shall bless the house of Israel, he shall bless the house of Aaron.
115.13: He shall bless them that fear the Lord: both small and great.
115.14: The Lord shall increase you more and more: you and your children.
115.15: Ye are the blessed of the Lord: who made heaven and earth.
115.16: All the whole heavens are the Lord's: the earth hath he given to the children of men.
115.17: The dead praise not thee, O Lord: neither all they that go down into silence.
115.18: But we will praise the Lord: from this time forth for evermore. Praise the Lord.

116.1: I am well pleased: that the Lord hath heard the voice of my prayer;
116.2: That he hath inclined his ear unto me: therefore will I call upon him as long as I live.
116.3: The snares of death compassed me round about: and the pains of hell gat hold upon me.
116.4: I shall find trouble and heaviness, and I will call upon the Name of the Lord: O Lord, I beseech thee, deliver my soul.
116.5: Gracious is the Lord, and righteous: yea, our God is merciful.
116.6: The Lord preserveth the simple: I was in misery, and he helped me.
116.7: Turn again then unto thy rest, O my soul: for the Lord hath rewarded thee.
116.8: And why? thou hast delivered my soul from death: mine eyes from tears, and my feet from falling.
116.9: I will walk before the Lord: in the land of the living.
116.10: I believed, and therefore will I speak; but I was sore troubled: I said in my haste, All men are liars.
116.11: What reward shall I give unto the Lord: for all the benefits that he hath done unto me?
116.12: I will receive the cup of salvation: and call upon the Name of the Lord.
116.13: I will pay my vows now in the presence of all his people: right dear in the sight of the Lord is the death of his saints.
116.14: Behold, O Lord, how that I am thy servant: I am thy servant, and the son of thine handmaid; thou hast broken my bonds in sunder.
116.15: I will offer to thee the sacrifice of thanksgiving: and will call upon the Name of the Lord.
116.16: I will pay my vows unto the Lord, in the sight of all his people: in the courts of the Lord's house, even in the midst of thee, O Jerusalem. Praise the Lord.

117.1: O praise the Lord, all ye heathen: praise him, all ye nations.
117.2: For his merciful kindness is ever more and more toward us: and the truth of the Lord endureth for ever. Praise the Lord.

118.1: O give thanks unto the Lord, for he is gracious: because his mercy endureth for ever.
118.2: Let Israel now confess that he is gracious: and that his mercy endureth for ever.
118.3: Let the house of Aaron now confess: that his mercy endureth for ever.
118.4: Yea, let them now that fear the Lord confess: that his mercy endureth for ever.
118.5: I called upon the Lord in trouble: and the Lord heard me at large.
118.6: The Lord is on my side: I will not fear what man doeth unto me.
118.7: The Lord taketh my part with them that help me: therefore shall I see my desire upon mine enemies.
118.8: It is better to trust in the Lord: than to put any confidence in man.
118.9: It is better to trust in the Lord: than to put any confidence in princes.
118.10: All nations compassed me round about: but in the Name of the Lord will I destroy them.
118.11: They kept me in on every side, they kept me in, I say, on every side: but in the Name of the Lord will I destroy them.
118.12: They came about me like bees, and are extinct even as the fire among the thorns: for in the Name of the Lord I will destroy them.
118.13: Thou hast thrust sore at me, that I might fall: but the Lord was my help.
118.14: The Lord is my strength, and my song i and is become my salvation.
118.15: The voice of joy and health is in the dwellings of the righteous: the right hand of the Lord bringeth mighty things to pass,
118.16: The right hand of the Lord hath the pre-eminence: the right hand of the Lord bringeth mighty things to pass.
118.17: I shall not die, but live: and declare the works of the Lord.
118.18: The Lord hath chastened and corrected me: but he hath not given me over unto death.
118.19: Open me the gates of righteousness: that I may go into them, and give thanks unto the Lord.
118.20: This is the gate of the Lord: the righteous shall enter into it.
118.21: I will thank thee, for thou hast heard me: and art become my salvation.
118.22: The same stone which the builders refused: is become the head-stone in the corner.
118.23: This is the Lord's doing: and it is marvellous in our eyes.
118.24: This is the day which the Lord hath made: we will rejoice and be glad in it.
118.25: Help me now, O Lord: O Lord, send us now prosperity.
118.26: Blessed be he that cometh in the Name of the Lord: we have wished you good luck, ye that are of the house of the Lord.
118.27: God is the Lord who hath shewed us light: bind the sacrifice with cords, yea, even unto the horns of the altar.
118.28: Thou art my God, and I will thank thee: thou art my God, and I will praise thee.
118.29: O give thanks unto the Lord, for he is gracious: and his mercy endureth for ever.

119.1: Blessed are those that are undefiled in the way: and walk in the law of the Lord.
119.2: Blessed are they that keep his testimonies: and seek him with their whole heart.
119.3: For they who do no wickedness: walk in his ways.
119.4: Thou hast charged: that we shall diligently keep thy commandments.
119.5: O that my ways were made so direct: that I might keep thy statutes!
119.6: So shall I not be confounded: while I have respect unto all thy commandments.
119.7: I will thank thee with an unfeigned heart: when I shall have learned the judgements of thy righteousness.
119.8: I will keep thy ceremonies: O forsake me not utterly.
119.9: Wherewithal shall a young man cleanse his way: even by ruling himself after thy word.
119.10: With my whole heart have I sought thee: let me not go wrong out of thy commandments.
119.11: Thy words have I hid within my heart: that I should not sin against thee.
119.12: Blessed art thou, O Lord: O teach me thy statutes.
119.13: With my lips have I been telling: of all the judgements of thy mouth.
119.14: I have had as great delight in the way of thy testimonies: as in all manner of riches.
119.15: I will talk of thy commandments: and have respect unto thy ways.
119.16: My delight shall be in thy statutes: and I will not forget thy word.
119.17: O do well unto thy servant: that I may live, and keep thy word.
119.18: Open thou mine eyes: that I may see the wondrous things of thy law.
119.19: I am a stranger upon earth: O hide not thy commandments from me.
119.20: My soul breaketh out for the very fervent desire: that it hath alway unto thy judgements.
119.21: Thou hast rebuked the proud: and cursed are they that do err from thy commandments.
119.22: O turn from me shame and rebuke: for I have kept thy testimonies.
119.23: Princes also did sit and speak against me: but thy servant is occupied in thy statutes.
119.24: For thy testimonies are my delight: and my counsellors.
119.25: My soul cleaveth to the dust: O quicken thou me, according to thy word.
119.26: I have acknowledged my ways, and thou heardest me: O teach me thy statutes.
119.27: Make me to understand the way of thy commandments: and so shall I talk of thy wondrous works.
119.28: My soul melteth away for very heaviness: comfort thou me according unto thy word.
119.29: Take from me the way of lying: and cause thou me to make much of thy law.
119.30: I have chosen the way of truth: and thy judgements have I laid before me.
119.31: I have stuck unto thy testimonies: O Lord, confound me not.
119.32: I will run the way of thy commandments: when thou hast set my heart at liberty.
119.33: Teach me, O Lord, the way of thy statutes: and I shall keep it unto the end.
119.34: Give me understanding, and I shall keep thy law: yea, I shall keep it with my whole heart.
119.35: Make me to go in the path of thy commandments: for therein is my desire.
119.36: Incline my heart unto thy testimonies: and not to covetousness.
119.37: O turn away mine eyes, lest they behold vanity: and quicken thou me in thy way.
119.38: O stablish thy word in thy servant: that I may fear thee.
119.39: Take away the rebuke that I am afraid of: for thy judgements are good.
119.40: Behold, my delight is in thy commandments: O quicken me in thy righteousness.
119.41: Let thy loving mercy come also unto me, O Lord: even thy salvation, according unto thy word.
119.42: So shall I make answer unto my blasphemers: for my trust is in thy word.
119.43: O take not the word of thy truth utterly out of my mouth: for my hope is in thy judgements.
119.44: So shall I alway keep thy law: yea, for ever and ever.
119.45: And I will walk at liberty: for I seek thy commandments.
119.46: I will speak of thy testimonies also, even before kings: and will not be ashamed.
119.47: And my delight shall be in thy commandments: which I have loved.
119.48: My hands also will I lift up unto thy commandments, which I have loved: and my study shall be in thy statutes.
119.49: O think upon thy servant, as concerning thy word: wherein thou hast caused me to put my trust.
119.50: The same is my comfort in my trouble: for thy word hath quickened me.
119.51: The proud have had me exceedingly in derision: yet have I not shrinked from thy law.
119.52: For I remembered thine everlasting judgements, O Lord: and received comfort.
119.53: I am horribly afraid: for the ungodly that forsake thy law.
119.54: Thy statutes have been my songs: in the house of my pilgrimage.
119.55: I have thought upon thy Name, O Lord, in the night-season: and have kept thy law.
119.56: This I had: because I kept thy commandments.
119.57: Thou art my portion, O Lord: I have promised to keep thy law.
119.58: I made my humble petition in thy presence with my whole heart: O be merciful unto me, according to thy word.
119.59: I called mine own ways to remembrance: and turned my feet unto thy testimonies.
119.60: I made haste, and prolonged not the time: to keep thy commandments.
119.61: The congregations of the ungodly have robbed me: but I have not forgotten thy law.
119.62: At midnight I will rise to give thanks unto thee: because of thy righteous judgements.
119.63: I am a companion of all them that fear thee: and keep thy commandments.
119.64: The earth, O Lord, is full of thy mercy: O teach me thy statutes.
119.65: O Lord, thou hast dealt graciously with thy servant: according unto thy word.
119.66: O learn me true understanding and knowledge: for I have believed thy commandments.
119.67: Before I was troubled, I went wrong: but now have I kept thy word.
119.68: Thou art good and gracious: O teach me thy statutes.
119.69: The proud have imagined a lie against me: but I will keep thy commandments with my whole heart.
119.70: Their heart is as fat as brawn: but my delight hath been in thy law.
119.71: It is good for me that I have been in trouble: that I may learn thy statutes.
119.72: The law of thy mouth is dearer unto me: than thousands of gold and silver.
119.73: Thy hands have made me and fashioned me: O give me understanding, that I may learn thy commandments.
119.74: They that fear thee will be glad when they see me: because I have put my trust in thy word.
119.75: I know, O Lord, that thy judgements are right: and that thou of very faithfulness hast caused me to be troubled.
119.76: O let thy merciful kindness be my comfort: according to thy word unto thy servant.
119.77: O let thy loving mercies come unto me, that I may live: for thy law is my delight.
119.78: Let the proud be confounded, for they go wickedly about to destroy me: but I will be occupied in thy commandments.
119.79: Let such as fear thee, and have known thy testimonies: be turned unto me.
119.80: O let my heart be sound in thy statutes: that I be not ashamed.
119.81: My soul hath longed for thy salvation: and I have a good hope because of thy word.
119.82: Mine eyes long sore for thy word: saying, O when wilt thou comfort me?
119.83: For I am become like a bottle in the smoke: yet do I not forget thy statutes.
119.84: How many are the days of thy servant: when wilt thou be avenged of them that persecute me?
119.85: The proud have digged pits for me: which are not after thy law.
119.86: All thy commandments are true: they persecute me falsely; O be thou my help.
119.87: They had almost made an end of me upon earth: but I forsook not thy commandments.
119.88: O quicken me after thy loving-kindness: and so shall I keep the testimonies of thy mouth.
119.89: O Lord, thy word: endureth for ever in heaven.
119.90: Thy truth also remaineth from one generation to another: thou hast laid the foundation of the earth, and it abideth.
119.91: They continue this day according to thine ordinance: for all things serve thee.
119.92: If my delight had not been in thy law: I should have perished in my trouble.
119.93: I will never forget thy commandments: for with them thou hast quickened me.
119.94: I am thine, O save me: for I have sought thy commandments.
119.95: The ungodly laid wait for me to destroy me: but I will consider thy testimonies.
119.96: I see that all things come to an end: but thy commandment is exceeding broad.
119.97: Lord, what love have I unto thy law: all the day long is my study in it.
119.98: Thou through thy commandments hast made me wiser than mine enemies: for they are ever with me.
119.99: I have more understanding than my teachers: for thy testimonies are my study.
119.100: I am wiser than the aged: because I keep thy commandments.
119.101: I have refrained my feet from every evil way: that I may keep thy word.
119.102: I have not shrunk from thy judgements: for thou teachest me.
119.103: O how sweet are thy words unto my throat: yea, sweeter than honey unto my mouth.
119.104: Through thy commandments I get understanding: therefore I hate all evil ways.
119.105: Thy word is a lantern unto my feet: and a light unto my paths.
119.106: I have sworn, and am stedfastly purposed: to keep thy righteous judgements.
119.107: I am troubled above measure: quicken me, O Lord, according to thy word.
119.108: Let the free-will offerings of my mouth please thee, O Lord: and teach me thy judgements.
119.109: My soul is alway in my hand: yet do I not forget thy law.
119.110: The ungodly have laid a snare for me: but yet I swerved not from thy commandments.
119.111: Thy testimonies have I claimed as mine heritage for ever: and why? they are the very joy of my heart.
119.112: I have applied my heart to fulfil thy statutes alway: even unto the end.
119.113: I hate them that imagine evil things: but thy law do I love.
119.114: Thou art my defence and shield: and my trust is in thy word.
119.115: Away from me, ye wicked: I will keep the commandments of my God.
119.116: O stablish me according to thy word, that I may live: and let me not be disappointed of my hope.
119.117: Hold thou me up, and I shall be safe: yea, my delight shall be ever in thy statutes.
119.118: Thou hast trodden down all them that depart from thy statutes: for they imagine but deceit.
119.119: Thou puttest away all the ungodly of the earth like dross: therefore I love thy testimonies.
119.120: My flesh trembleth for fear of thee: and I am afraid of thy judgements.
119.121: I deal with the thing that is lawful and right: O give me not over unto mine oppressors.
119.122: Make thou thy servant to delight in that which is good: that the proud do me no wrong.
119.123: Mine eyes are wasted away with looking for thy health: and for the word of thy righteousness.
119.124: O deal with thy servant according unto thy loving mercy: and teach me thy statutes.
119.125: I am thy servant, O grant me understanding: that I may know thy testimonies.
119.126: It is time for thee, Lord, to lay to thine hand: for they have destroyed thy law.
119.127: For I love thy commandments: above gold and precious stone.
119.128: Therefore hold I straight all thy commandments: and all false ways I utterly abhor.
119.129: Thy testimonies are wonderful: therefore doth my soul keep them.
119.130: When thy word goeth forth: it giveth light and understanding unto the simple.
119.131: I opened my mouth, and drew in my breath: for my delight was in thy commandments.
119.132: O look thou upon me, and be merciful unto me: as thou usest to do unto those that love thy Name.
119.133: Order my steps in thy word: and so shall no wickedness have dominion over me.
119.134: O deliver me from the wrongful dealings of men: and so shall I keep thy commandments.
119.135: Shew the light of thy countenance upon thy servant: and teach me thy statutes.
119.136: Mine eyes gush out with water: because men keep not thy law.
119.137: Righteous art thou, O Lord: and true is thy judgement.
119.138: The testimonies that thou hast commanded: are exceeding righteous and true.
119.139: My zeal hath even consumed me: because mine enemies have forgotten thy words.
119.140: Thy word is tried to the uttermost: and thy servant loveth it.
119.141: I am small, and of no reputation: yet do I not forget thy commandments.
119.142: Thy righteousness is an everlasting righteousness: and thy law is the truth.
119.143: Trouble and heaviness have taken hold upon me: yet is my delight in thy commandments.
119.144: The righteousness of thy testimonies is everlasting: O grant me understanding, and I shall live.
119.145: I call with my whole heart: hear me, O Lord, I will keep thy statutes.
119.146: Yea, even unto thee do I call: help me, and I shall keep thy testimonies.
119.147: Early in the morning do I cry unto thee: for in thy word is my trust.
119.148: Mine eyes prevent the night-watches: that I might be occupied in thy words.
119.149: Hear my voice, O Lord, according unto thy loving-kindness: quicken me, according as thou art wont.
119.150: They draw nigh that of malice persecute me: and are far from thy law.
119.151: Be thou nigh at hand, O Lord: for all thy commandments are true.
119.152: As concerning thy testimonies, I have known long since: that thou hast grounded them for ever.
119.153: O consider mine adversity, and deliver me: for I do not forget thy law.
119.154: Avenge thou my cause, and deliver me: quicken me, according to thy word.
119.155: Health is far from the ungodly: for they regard not thy statutes.
119.156: Great is thy mercy, O Lord: quicken me, as thou art wont.
119.157: Many there are that trouble me, and persecute me: yet do I not swerve from thy testimonies.
119.158: It grieveth me when I see the transgressors: because they keep not thy law.
119.159: Consider, O Lord, how I love thy commandments: O quicken me, according to thy loving-kindness.
119.160: Thy word is true from everlasting: all the judgements of thy righteousness endure for evermore.
119.161: Princes have persecuted me without a cause: but my heart standeth in awe of thy word.
119.162: I am as glad of thy word: as one that findeth great spoils.
119.163: As for lies, I hate and abhor them: but thy law do I love.
119.164: Seven times a day do I praise thee: because of thy righteous judgements.
119.165: Great is the peace that they have who love thy law: and they are not offended at it.
119.166: Lord, I have looked for thy saving health: and done after thy commandments.
119.167: My soul hath kept thy testimonies: and loved them exceedingly.
119.168: I have kept thy commandments and testimonies: for all my ways are before thee.
119.169: Let my complaint come before thee, O Lord: give me understanding, according to thy word.
119.170: Let my supplication come before thee: deliver me, according to thy word.
119.171: My lips shall speak of thy praise: when thou hast taught me thy statutes.
119.172: Yea, my tongue shall sing of thy word: for all thy commandments are righteous.
119.173: Let thine hand help me: for I have chosen thy commandments.
119.174: I have longed for thy saving health, O Lord: and in thy law is my delight.
119.175: O let my soul live, and it shall praise thee: and thy judgements shall help me.
119.176: I have gone astray like a sheep that is lost: O seek thy servant, for I do not forget thy commandments.

120.1: When I was in trouble I called upon the Lord: and he heard me.
120.2: Deliver my soul, O Lord, from lying lips: and from a deceitful tongue.
120.3: What reward shall be given or done unto thee, thou false tongue: even mighty and sharp arrows, with hot burning coals.
120.4: Woe is me, that I am constrained to dwell with Mesech: and to have my habitation among the tents of Kedar.
120.5: My soul hath long dwelt among them: that are enemies unto peace.
120.6: I labour for peace, but when I speak unto them thereof: they make them ready to battle.

121.1: I will lift up mine eyes unto the hills: from whence cometh my help.
121.2: My help cometh even from the Lord: who hath made heaven and earth.
121.3: He will not suffer thy foot to be moved: and he that keepeth thee will not sleep.
121.4: Behold, he that keepeth Israel: shall neither slumber nor sleep.
121.5: The Lord himself is thy keeper: the Lord is thy defence upon thy right hand;
121.6: So that the sun shall not burn thee by day: neither the moon by night.
121.7: The Lord shall preserve thee from all evil: yea, it is even he that shall keep thy soul.
121.8: The Lord shall preserve thy going out, and thy coming in: from this time forth for evermore.

122.1: I was glad when they said unto me: We will go into the house of the Lord.
122.2: Our feet shall stand in thy gates: O Jerusalem.
122.3: Jerusalem is built as a city: that is at unity in itself.
122.4: For thither the tribes go up, even the tribes of the Lord: to testify unto Israel, to give thanks unto the Name of the Lord.
122.5: For there is the seat of judgement: even the seat of the house of David.
122.6: O pray for the peace of Jerusalem: they shall prosper that love thee.
122.7: Peace be within thy walls: and plenteousness within thy palaces.
122.8: For my brethren and companions sakes: I will wish thee prosperity.
122.9: Yea, because of the house of the Lord our God: I will seek to do thee good.

123.1: Unto thee lift I up mine eyes: O thou that dwellest in the heavens.
123.2: Behold, even as the eyes of servants look unto the hand of their masters, and as the eyes of a maiden unto the hand of her mistress: even so our eyes wait upon the Lord our God, until he have mercy upon us.
123.3: Have mercy upon us, O Lord, have mercy upon us: for we are utterly despised.
123.4: Our soul is filled with the scornful reproof of the wealthy: and with the despitefulness of the proud.

124.1: If the Lord himself had not been on our side, now may Israel say: if the Lord himself had not been on our side, when men rose up against us;
124.2: They had swallowed us up quick: when they were so wrathfully displeased at us.
124.3: Yea, the waters had drowned us: and the stream had gone over our soul.
124.4: The deep waters of the proud: had gone even over our soul.
124.5: But praised be the Lord: who hath not given us over for a prey unto their teeth.
124.6: Our soul is escaped even as a bird out of the snare of the fowler: the snare is broken, and we are delivered.
124.7: Our help standeth in the Name of the Lord: who hath made heaven and earth.

125.1: They that put their trust in the Lord shall be even as the mount Sion: which may not be removed, but standeth fast for ever.
125.2: The hills stand about Jerusalem: even so standeth the Lord round about his people, from this time forth for evermore.
125.3: For the rod of the ungodly cometh not into the lot of the righteous: lest the righteous put their hand unto wickedness.
125.4: Do well, O Lord: unto those that are good and true of heart.
125.5: As for such as turn back unto their own wickedness: the Lord shall lead them forth with the evil-doers; but peace shall be upon Israel.

126.1: When the Lord turned again the captivity of Sion: then were we like unto them that dream.
126.2: Then was our mouth filled with laughter: and our tongue with joy.
126.3: Then said they among the heathen: The Lord hath done great things for them.
126.4: Yea, the Lord hath done great things for us already: whereof we rejoice.
126.5: Turn our captivity, O Lord: as the rivers in the south.
126.6: They that sow in tears: shall reap in joy.
126.7: He that now goeth on his way weeping, and beareth forth good seed: shall doubtless come again with joy, and bring his sheaves with him.

127.1: Except the Lord build the house: their labour is but lost that build it.
127.2: Except the Lord keep the city: the watchman waketh but in vain.
127.3: It is but lost labour that ye haste to rise up early, and so late take rest, arid eat the bread of carefulness: for so he giveth his beloved sleep.
127.4: Lo, children and the fruit of the womb: are an heritage and gift that cometh of the Lord.
127.5: Like as the arrows in the hand of the giant: even so are the young children.
127.6: Happy is the man that hath his quiver full of them: they shall not be ashamed when they speak with their enemies in the gate.

128.1: Blessed are all they that fear the Lord: and walk in his ways.
128.2: For thou shalt eat the labours of thine hands: O well is thee, and happy shalt thou be.
128.3: Thy wife shall be as the fruitful vine: upon the walls of thine house.
128.4: Thy children like the olive-branches: round about thy table.
128.5: Lo, thus shall the man be blessed: that feareth the Lord.
128.6: The Lord from out of Sion shall so bless thee: that thou shalt see Jerusalem in prosperity all thy life long.
128.7: Yea, that thou shalt see thy children's children: and peace upon Israel.

129.1: Many a time have they fought against me from my youth up: may Israel now say.
129.2: Yea, many a time have they vexed me from my youth up: but they have not prevailed against me.
129.3: The plowers plowed upon my back: and made long furrows.
129.4: But the righteous Lord: hath hewn the snares of the ungodly in pieces.
129.5: Let them be confounded and turned backward: as many as have evil will at Sion.
129.6: Let them be even as the grass growing upon the house-tops: which withereth afore it be plucked up;
129.7: Whereof the mower filleth not his hand: neither he that bindeth up the sheaves his bosom.
129.8: So that they who go by say not so much as, The Lord prosper you: we wish you good luck in the Name of the Lord.

130.1: Out of the deep have I called unto thee, O Lord: Lord, hear my voice.
130.2: O let thine ears consider well: the voice of my complaint.
130.3: If thou, Lord, wilt be extreme to mark what is done amiss: O Lord, who may abide it?
130.4: For there is mercy with thee: therefore shalt thou be feared.
130.5: I look for the Lord; my soul doth wait for him: in his word is my trust.
130.6: My soul fleeth unto the Lord: before the morning watch, I say, before the morning watch.
130.7: O Israel, trust in the Lord, for with the Lord there is mercy: and with him is plenteous redemption.
130.8: And he shall redeem Israel: from all his sins.

131.1: O Lord, I am not high-minded: I have no proud looks.
131.2: I do not exercise myself in great matters: which are too high for me.
131.3: But I refrain my soul, and keep it low, like as a child that is weaned from his mother: yea, my soul is even as a weaned child.
131.4: O Israel, trust in the Lord: from this time forth for evermore.

132.1: Lord, remember David: and all his trouble;
132.2: How he sware unto the Lord: and vowed a vow unto the Almighty God of Jacob;
132.3: I will not come within the tabernacle of mine house: nor climb up into my bed;
132.4: I will not suffer mine eyes to sleep, nor mine eye-lids to slumber: neither the temples of my head to take any rest;
132.5: Until I find out a place for the temple of the Lord: an habitation for the mighty God of Jacob.
132.6: Lo, we heard of the same at Ephrata: and found it in the wood.
132.7: We will go into his tabernacle: and fall low on our knees before his footstool.
132.8: Arise, O Lord, into thy resting-place: thou, and the ark of thy strength.
132.9: Let thy priests be clothed with righteousness: and let thy saints sing with joyfulness.
132.10: For thy servant David's sake: turn not away the presence of thine Anointed.
132.11: The Lord hath made a faithful oath unto David: and he shall not shrink from it;
132.12: Of the fruit of thy body: shall I set upon thy seat.
132.13: If thy children will keep my covenant, and my testimonies that I shall learn them: their children also shall sit upon thy seat for evermore.
132.14: For the Lord hath chosen Sion to be an habitation for himself: he hath longed for her.
132.15: This shall be my rest for ever: here will I dwell, for I have a delight therein.
132.16: I will bless her victuals with increase: and will satisfy her poor with bread.
132.17: I will deck her priests with health: and her saints shall rejoice and sing.
132.18: There shall I make the horn of David to flourish: I have ordained a lantern for mine Anointed.
132.19: As for his enemies, I shall clothe them with shame: but upon himself shall his crown flourish.

133.1: Behold, how good and joyful a thing it is: brethren, to dwell together in unity!
133.2: It is like the precious ointment upon the head, that ran down unto the beard: even unto Aaron's beard, and went down to the skirts of his clothing.
133.3: Like as the dew of Hermon: which fell upon the hill of Sion.
133.4: For there the Lord promised his blessing: and life for evermore.

134.1: Behold now, praise the Lord: all ye servants of the Lord;
134.2: Ye that by night stand in the house of the Lord: even in the courts of the house of our God.
134.3: Lift up your hands in the sanctuary: and praise the Lord.
134.4: The Lord that made heaven and earth: give thee blessing out of Sion.

135.1: O praise the Lord, laud ye the Name of the Lord: praise it, O ye servants of the Lord;
135.2: Ye that stand in the house of the Lord: in the courts of the house of our God.
135.3: O praise the Lord, for the Lord is gracious: O sing praises unto his Name, for it is lovely.
135.4: For why? the Lord hath chosen Jacob unto himself: and Israel for his own possession.
135.5: For I know that the Lord is great: and that our Lord is above all gods.
135.6: Whatsoever the Lord pleased, that did he in heaven and in earth: and in the sea, and in all deep places.
135.7: He bringeth forth the clouds from the ends of the world: and sendeth forth lightnings with the rain, bringing the winds out of his treasures.
135.8: He smote the first-born of Egypt: both of man and beast.
135.9: He hath sent tokens and wonders into the midst of thee, O thou land of Egypt: upon Pharaoh, and all his servants.
135.10: He smote divers nations: and slew mighty kings;
135.11: Sehon king of the Amorites, and Og the king of Basan: and all the kingdoms of Canaan;
135.12: And gave their land to be an heritage: even an heritage unto Israel his people.
135.13: Thy Name, O Lord, endureth for ever: so doth thy memorial, O Lord, from one generation to another.
135.14: For the Lord will avenge his people: and be gracious unto his servants.
135.15: As for the images of the heathen, they are but silver and gold: the work of men's hands.
135.16: They have mouths, and speak not: eyes have they, but they see not.
135.17: They have ears, and yet they hear not: neither is there any breath in their mouths.
135.18: They that make them are like unto them: and so are all they that put their trust in them.
135.19: Praise the Lord, ye house of Israel; praise the Lord, ye house of Aaron.
135.20: Praise the Lord, ye house of Levi: ye that fear the Lord, praise the Lord.
135.21: Praised be the Lord out of Sion: who dwelleth at Jerusalem.

136.1: O give thanks unto the Lord, for he is gracious: and his mercy endureth for ever.
136.2: O give thanks unto the God of all gods: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136.3: O thank the Lord of all lords: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136.4: Who only doeth great wonders: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136.5: Who by his excellent wisdom made the heavens: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136.6: Who laid out the earth above the waters: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136.7: Who hath made great lights: for his mercy endureth for ever;
136.8: The sun to rule the day: for his mercy endureth for ever;
136.9: The moon and the stars to govern the night: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136.10: Who smote Egypt with their first-born: for his mercy endureth for ever;
136.11: And brought out Israel from among them: for his mercy endureth for ever;
136.12: With a mighty hand, and stretched out arm: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136.13: Who divided the Red sea in two parts: for his mercy endureth for ever;
136.14: And made Israel to go through the midst of it: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136.15: But as for Pharaoh and his host, he overthrew them in the Red sea: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136.16: Who led his people through the wilderness: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136.17: Who smote great kings: for his mercy endureth for ever;
136.18: Yea, and slew mighty kings: for his mercy endureth for ever;
136.19: Sehon king of the Amorites: for his mercy endureth for ever;
136.20: And Og the king of Basan: for his mercy endureth for ever;
136.21: And gave away their land for an heritage: for his mercy endureth for ever;
136.22: Even for an heritage unto Israel his servant: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136.23: Who remembered us when we were in trouble: for his mercy endureth for ever;
136.24: And hath delivered us from our enemies: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136.25: Who giveth food to all flesh: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136.26: O give thanks unto the God of heaven: for his mercy endureth for ever.
136.27: O give thanks unto the Lord of lords: for his mercy endureth for ever.

137.1: By the waters of Babylon we sat down and wept: when we remembered thee, O Sion.
137.2: As for our harps, we hanged them up: upon the trees that are therein.
137.3: For they that led us away captive required of us then a song, and melody in our heaviness: Sing us one of the songs of Sion.
137.4: How shall we sing the Lord's song: in a strange land?
137.5: If I forget thee, O Jerusalem: let my right hand forget her cunning.
137.6: If I do not remember thee, let my tongue cleave to the roof of my mouth: yea, if I prefer not Jerusalem in my mirth.
137.7: Remember the children of Edom, O Lord, in the day of Jerusalem: how they said, Down with it, down with it, even to the ground.
137.8: O daughter of Babylon, wasted with misery: yea, happy shall he be that rewardeth thee, as thou hast served us.
137.9: Blessed shall he be that taketh thy children: and throweth them against the stones.

138.1: I will give thanks unto thee, O Lord, with my whole heart: even before the gods will I sing praise unto thee.
138.2: I will worship toward thy holy temple, and praise thy Name, because of thy loving-kindness and truth: for thou hast magnified thy Name and thy word above all things.
138.3: When I called upon thee, thou heardest me: and enduedst my soul with much strength.
138.4: All the kings of the earth shall praise thee, O Lord: for they have heard the words of thy mouth.
138.5: Yea, they shall sing in the ways of the Lord: that great is the glory of the Lord.
138.6: For though the Lord be high, yet hath he respect unto the lowly: as for the proud, he beholdeth them afar off.
138.7: Though I walk in the midst of trouble, yet shalt thou refresh me: thou shalt stretch forth thy hand upon the furiousness of mine enemies, and thy right hand shall save me.
138.8: The Lord shall make good his loving-kindness toward me: yea, thy mercy, O Lord, endureth for ever; despise not then the works of thine own hands.

139.1: Lord, thou hast searched me out and known me: thou knowest my down-sitting and mine up-rising, thou understandest my thoughts long before.
139.2: Thou art about my path, and about my bed: and spiest out all my ways.
139.3: For lo, there is not a word in my tongue: but thou, O Lord, knowest it altogether.
139.4: Thou hast fashioned me behind and before: and laid thine hand upon me.
139.5: Such knowledge is too wonderful and excellent for me: I cannot attain unto it.
139.6: Whither shall I go then from thy Spirit: or whither shall I go then from thy presence?
139.7: If I climb up into heaven, thou art there: if I go down to hell, thou art there also.
139.8: If I take the wings of the morning: and remain in the uttermost parts of the sea;
139.9: Even there also shall thy hand lead me: and thy right hand shall hold me.
139.10: If I say, Peradventure the darkness shall cover me: then shall my night be turned to day.
139.11: Yea, the darkness is no darkness with thee, but the night is as clear as the day: the darkness and light to thee are both alike.
139.12: For my reins are thine: thou hast covered me in my mother's womb.
139.13: I will give thanks unto thee, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made: marvellous are thy works, and that my soul knoweth right well.
139.14: My bones are not hid from thee: though I be made secretly, and fashioned beneath in the earth.
139.15: Thine eyes did see my substance, yet being unperfect: and in thy book were all my members written;
139.16: Which day by day were fashioned: when as yet there was none of them.
139.17: How dear are thy counsels unto me, O God: O how great is the sum of them!
139.18: If I tell them, they are more in number than the sand: when I wake up I am present with thee.
139.19: Wilt thou not slay the wicked, O God: depart from me, ye blood-thirsty men.
139.20: For they speak unrighteously against thee: and thine enemies take thy Name in vain.
139.21: Do not I hate them, O Lord, that hate thee: and am not I grieved with those that rise up against thee?
139.22: Yea, I hate them right sore: even as though they were mine enemies.
139.23: Try me, O God, and seek the ground of my heart: prove me, and examine my thoughts.
139.24: Look well if there be any way of wickedness in me: and lead me in the way everlasting.

140.1: Deliver me, O Lord, from the evil man: and preserve me from the wicked man.
140.2: Who imagine mischief in their hearts: and stir up strife all the day long.
140.3: They have sharpened their tongues like a serpent: adders poison is under their lips.
140.4: Keep me, O Lord, from the hands of the ungodly; preserve me from the Wicked men, who are purposed to overthrow my goings.
140.5: The proud have laid a snare for me, and spread a net abroad with cords: yea, and set traps in my way.
140.6: I said unto the Lord, Thou art my God: hear the voice of my prayers, O Lord.
140.7: O Lord God, thou strength of my health: thou hast covered my head in the day of battle.
140.8: Let not the ungodly have his desire, O Lord: let not his mischievous imagination prosper, lest they be too proud.
140.9: Let the mischief of their own lips fall upon the head of them: that compass me about.
140.10: Let hot burning coals fall upon them: let them be cast into the fire and into the pit, that they never rise up again.
140.11: A man full of words shall not prosper upon the earth: evil shall hunt the wicked person to overthrow him,
140.12: Sure I am that the Lord will avenge the poor: and maintain the cause of the helpless.
140.13: The righteous also shall give thanks unto thy Name: and the just shall continue in thy sight.

141.1: O Lord, I call upon thee, haste thee unto me: and consider my voice when I cry unto thee.
141.2: Let my prayer be set forth in thy sight as the incense: and let the lifting up of my hands be an evening sacrifice.
141.3: Set a watch, O Lord, before my mouth: and keep the door of my lips.
141.4: O let not mine heart be inclined to any evil thing: let me not be occupied in ungodly works with the men that work wickedness, lest I eat of such things as please them.
141.5: Let the righteous rather smite me friendly: and reprove me.
141.6: But let not their precious balms break my head: yea, I will pray yet against their wickedness.
141.7: Let their judges be overthrown in stony places: that they may hear my words, for they are sweet.
141.8: Our bones lie scattered before the pit: like as when one breaketh and heweth wood upon the earth.
141.9: But mine eyes look unto thee, O Lord God: in thee is my trust, O cast not out my soul.
141.10: Keep me from the snare that they have laid for me: and from the traps of the wicked doers.
