141.11: Let the ungodly fall into their own nets together: and let me ever escape them.

142.1: I cried unto the Lord with my voice: yea, even unto the Lord did I make my supplication.
142.2: I poured out my complaints before him: and shewed him of my trouble.
142.3: When my spirit was in heaviness thou knewest my path: in the way wherein I walked have they privily laid a snare for me.
142.4: I looked also upon my right hand: and saw there was no man that would know me.
142.5: I had no place to flee unto: and no man cared for my soul.
142.6: I cried unto thee, O Lord, and said: Thou art my hope, and my portion in the land of the living.
142.7: Consider my complaint: for I am brought very low.
142.8: O deliver me from my persecutors: for they are too strong for me.
142.9: Bring my soul out of prison, that I may give thanks unto thy Name: which thing if thou wilt grant me, then shall the righteous resort unto my company.

143.1: Hear my prayer, O Lord, and consider my desire: hearken unto me for thy truth and righteousness sake.
143.2: And enter not into judgement with thy servant: for in thy sight shall no man living be justified.
143.3: For the enemy hath persecuted my soul; he hath smitten my life down to the ground: he hath laid me in the darkness, as the men that have been long dead.
143.4: Therefore is my spirit vexed within me: and my heart within me is desolate.
143.5: Yet do I remember the time past; I muse upon all thy works: yea, I exercise myself in the works of thy hands.
143.6: I stretch forth my hands unto thee: my soul gaspeth unto thee as a thirsty land.
143.7: Hear me, O Lord, and that soon, for my spirit waxeth faint: hide not thy face from me, lest I be like unto them that go down into the pit.
143.8: O let me hear thy loving-kindness betimes in the morning, for in thee is my trust: shew thou me the way that I should walk in, for I lift up my soul unto thee.
143.9: Deliver me, O Lord, from mine enemies: for I flee unto thee to hide me.
143.10: Teach me to do the thing that pleaseth thee, for thou art my God: let thy loving Spirit lead me forth into the land of righteousness.
143.11: Quicken me, O Lord, for thy Name's sake: and for thy righteousness sake bring my soul out of trouble.
143.12: And of thy goodness slay mine enemies: and destroy all them that vex my soul; for I am thy servant.

144.1: Blessed be the Lord my strength: who teacheth my hands to war, and my fingers to fight;
144.2: My hope and my fortress, my castle and deliverer, my defender in whom I trust: who subdueth my people that is under me.
144.3: Lord, what is man, that thou hast such respect unto him: or the son of man, that thou so regardest him?
144.4: Man is like a thing of nought: his time passeth away like a shadow.
144.5: Bow thy heavens, O Lord, and come down: touch the mountains, and they shall smoke.
144.6: Cast forth thy lightning, and tear them: shoot out thine arrows, and consume them.
144.7: Send down thine hand from above: deliver me, and take me out of the great waters, from the hand of strange children;
144.8: Whose mouth talketh of vanity: and their right hand is a right hand of wickedness.
144.9: I will sing a new song unto thee, O God: and sing praises unto thee upon a ten-stringed lute.
144.10: Thou hast given victory unto kings: and hast delivered David thy servant from the peril of the sword.
144.11: Save me, and deliver me from the hand of strange children: whose mouth talketh of vanity, and their right hand is a right hand of iniquity.
144.12: That our sons may grow up as the young plants: and that our daughters may be as the polished corners of the temple.
144.13: That our garners may be full and plenteous with all manner of store: that our sheep may bring forth thousands and ten thousands in our streets.
144.14: That our oxen may be strong to labour, that there be no decay: no leading into captivity, and no complaining in our streets.
144.15: Happy are the people that are in such a case: yea, blessed are the people who have the Lord for their God.

145.1: I will magnify thee, O God, my King: and I will praise thy Name for ever and ever.
145.2: Every day will I give thanks unto thee: and praise thy Name for ever and ever.
145.3: Great is the Lord, and marvellous worthy to be praised: there is no end of his greatness.
145.4: One generation shall praise thy works unto another: and declare thy power.
145.5: As for me, I will be talking of thy worship: thy glory, thy praise, and wondrous works;
145.6: So that men shall speak of the might of thy marvellous acts: and I will also tell of thy greatness.
145.7: The memorial of thine abundant kindness shall be shewed: and men shall sing of thy righteousness.
145.8: The Lord is gracious and merciful: long-suffering and of great goodness.
145.9: The Lord is loving unto every man: and his mercy is over all his works.
145.10: All thy works praise thee, O Lord: and thy saints give thanks unto thee.
145.11: They shew the glory of thy kingdom: and talk of thy power;
145.12: That thy power, thy glory, and mightiness of thy kingdom: might be known unto men.
145.13: Thy kingdom is an everlasting kingdom: and thy dominion endureth throughout all ages.
145.14: The Lord upholdeth all such as fall: and lifteth up all those that are down.
145.15: The eyes of all wait upon thee, O Lord: and thou givest them their meat in due season.
145.16: Thou openest thine hand: and fillest all things living with plenteousness.
145.17: The Lord is righteous in all his ways: and holy in all his works.
145.18: The Lord is nigh unto all them that call upon him: yea, all such as call upon him faithfully.
145.19: He will fulfil the desire of them that fear him: he also will hear their cry, and will help them.
145.20: The Lord preserveth all them that love him: but scattereth abroad all the ungodly.
145.21: My mouth shall speak the praise of the Lord: and let all flesh give thanks unto his holy Name for ever and ever.

146.1: Praise the Lord, O my soul; while I live will I praise the Lord: yea, as long as I have any being, I will sing praises unto my God.
146.2: O put not your trust in princes, nor in any child of man: for there is no help in them.
146.3: For when the breath of man goeth forth he shall turn again to his earth: and then all his thoughts perish.
146.4: Blessed is he that hath the God of Jacob for his help: and whose hope is in the Lord his God;
146.5: Who made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that therein is: who keepeth his promise for ever;
146.6: Who helpeth them to right that suffer wrong: who feedeth the hungry.
146.7: The Lord looseth men out of prison: the Lord giveth sight to the blind.
146.8: The Lord helpeth them that are fallen: the Lord careth for the righteous.
146.9: The Lord careth for the strangers, he defendeth the fatherless and widow: as for the way of the ungodly, he turneth it upside down.
146.10: The Lord thy God, O Sion, shall be King for evermore: and throughout all generations.

147.1: O praise the Lord, for it is a good thing to sing praises unto our God: yea, a joyful and pleasant thing it is to be thankful.
147.2: The Lord doth build up Jerusalem: and gather together the out-casts of Israel.
147.3: He healeth those that are broken in heart: and giveth medicine to heal their sickness.
147.4: He telleth the number of the stars: and calleth them all by their names.
147.5: Great is our Lord, and great is his power: yea, and his wisdom is infinite.
147.6: The Lord setteth up the meek: and bringeth the ungodly down to the ground.
147.7: O sing unto the Lord with thanksgiving: sing praises upon the harp unto our God;
147.8: Who covereth the heaven with clouds, and prepareth rain for the earth: and maketh the grass to grow upon the mountains, and herb for the use of men;
147.9: Who giveth fodder unto the cattle: and feedeth the young ravens that call upon him.
147.10: He hath no pleasure in the strength of an horse: neither delighteth he in any man's legs.
147.11: But the Lord's delight is in them that fear him: and put their trust in his mercy.
147.12: Praise the Lord, O Jerusalem: praise thy God, O Sion.
147.13: For he hath made fast the bars of thy gates: and hath blessed thy children within thee.
147.14: He maketh peace in thy borders: and filleth thee with the flour of wheat.
147.15: He sendeth forth his commandment upon earth: and his word runneth very swiftly.
147.16: He giveth snow like wool: and scattereth the hoar-frost like ashes.
147.17: He casteth forth his ice like morsels: who is able to abide his frost?
147.18: He sendeth out his word, and melteth them: he bloweth with his wind, and the waters flow.
147.19: He sheweth his word unto Jacob: his statutes and ordinances unto Israel.
147.20: He hath not dealt so with any nation: neither have the heathen knowledge of his laws.

148.1: O praise the Lord of heaven: praise him in the height.
148.2: Praise him, all ye angels of his: praise him, all his host.
148.3: Praise him, sun and moon: praise him, all ye stars and light.
148.4: Praise him, all ye heavens: and ye waters that are above the heavens.
148.5: Let them praise the Name of the Lord: for he spake the word, and they were made; he commanded, and they were created.
148.6: He hath made them fast for ever and ever: he hath given them a law which shall not be broken.
148.7: Praise the Lord upon earth: ye dragons, and all deeps;
148.8: Fire and hail, snow and vapours: wind and storm, fulfilling his word;
148.9: Mountains and all hills: fruitful trees and all cedars;
148.10: Beasts and all cattle: worms and feathered fowls;
148.11: Kings of the earth and all people: princes and all judges of the world;
148.12: Young men and maidens, old men and children, praise the Name of the Lord: for his Name only is excellent, and his praise above heaven and earth.
148.13: He shall exalt the horn of his people; all his saints shall praise him: even the children of Israel, even the people that serveth him.

149.1: O sing unto the Lord a new song: let the congregation of saints praise him.
149.2: Let Israel rejoice in him that made him: and let the children of Sion be joyful in their King.
149.3: Let them praise his Name in the dance: let them sing praises unto him with tabret and harp.
149.4: For the Lord hath pleasure in his people: and helpeth the meek-hearted.
149.5: Let the saints be joyful with glory: let them rejoice in their beds.
149.6: Let the praises of God be in their mouth: and a two-edged sword in their hands;
149.7: To be avenged of the heathen: and to rebuke the people;
149.8: To bind their kings in chains: and their nobles with links of iron.
149.9: That they may be avenged of them, as it is written: Such honour have all his saints.

150.1: O praise God in his holiness: praise him in the firmament of his power.
150.2: Praise him in his noble acts: praise him according to his excellent greatness.
150.3: Praise him in the sound of the trumpet: praise him upon the lute and harp.
150.4: Praise him in the cymbals and dances: praise him upon the strings and pipe.
150.5: Praise him upon the well-tuned cymbals: praise him upon the loud cymbals.
150.6: Let every thing that hath breath: praise the Lord.
